best_plan.executive_summary: Offline-first React Native app using WatermelonDB for local SQLite persistence, syncing completed HACCP checklist records to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate.
best_plan.architecture_overview: React Native Expo app with WatermelonDB offline store syncing via custom FastAPI sync endpoints.
best_plan.data_model: safety_checklists, checklist_completions, temperature_records, allergen_declarations, equipment_sanitization_logs, audit_trail_events

target_output.thinking_summary: The key gap in rounds 1-2 was underspecified sync semantics. ISS-001 needs a concrete unique key on checklist_completion records and explicit server adjudication rules for duplicate client UUIDs. ISS-002 needs a WatermelonDB-level retry queue with sync_status field and exponential backoff. The health authority export template versioning (ISS-003) was resolved in round 2 and just needs to be carried forward correctly. HACCP compliance demands that these sync details are precise — ambiguity in conflict resolution could produce duplicate or missing regulatory records.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified that checklist_completion records are unique on composite key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date); server treats client-generated UUID as idempotency key — duplicate UUID returns 200 with existing record; composite key collision returns 409 with existing record payload so client can reconcile; documented in api_design and data_model sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent submissions from multiple staff on the same checklist type and shift date are deterministically handled without duplicate compliance records

fix_report.action_taken: Defined WatermelonDB sync_status field on local checklist_completion model (values: pending, synced, failed, retrying); POST /sync/push returns per-record status array; mobile client iterates response and marks failed records as sync_status=failed; a Zustand-managed retry scheduler re-queues failed records on next connectivity event with exponential backoff capped at 4 retries before escalating to supervisor alert; described in workflows and deployment sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial sync failures are tracked at the record level in WatermelonDB and retried automatically without data loss or silent drops

target_output.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile Platform — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile application enabling kitchen staff to complete HACCP-required food safety checklists — including temperature logs, allergen handling confirmations, and equipment sanitization records — with up to 8-hour offline capability via WatermelonDB SQLite storage and deterministic conflict-resolved sync to a FastAPI backend on AWS ECS Fargate. Compliance managers access a cross-location audit dashboard and can export tamper-evident audit trails in health authority-accepted formats.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native Expo SDK 51 app with WatermelonDB as the offline-first reactive data layer, syncing to a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate via two purpose-built sync endpoints. Checklist completions carry client-generated UUIDs as idempotency keys; the server enforces a composite unique constraint on (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) to prevent duplicate regulatory records. Authentication flows through Cognito with MFA mandatory for supervisors and managers. All completed records are hashed with SHA-256 and chained into a per-location append-only audit_trail_events sequence, making retroactive tampering detectable. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all server-side compliance data; a Redis ElastiCache instance handles sync session state and rate limiting.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native, Expo SDK 51, Expo Router, WatermelonDB 0.27, Zustand, Expo NetInfo, Expo TaskManager, Expo SecureStore. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, SQLite (WatermelonDB on device), Redis 7 ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, RDS Multi-AZ us-east-1, S3 for compliance PDF exports. CI/CD: Buildkite with iOS and Android lanes, TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Sentry for mobile crash reporting, CloudWatch for sync metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Offline Checklist Completion: staff select shift type and work through CCP rows, entering temperature probe readings, allergen confirmation checkboxes, and sanitization log entries entirely offline with no network dependency. Sync Engine: WatermelonDB push/pull sync to FastAPI with idempotency, composite key conflict detection, and per-record retry queue with exponential backoff. Compliance Audit Trail: server-side SHA-256 hash chaining of all submitted records, detectable chain breaks trigger manager alerts. Health Authority Export: server-side export template registry supporting jurisdiction-specific PDF and CSV formats keyed by format_version param, no app release required for new format additions. Admin Compliance Dashboard: multi-location managers view overdue checklist alerts, temperature exceedance events, completion rate heatmaps, and initiate audit exports.
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo app (WatermelonDB local SQLite, Zustand UI state, Expo NetInfo connectivity detection, Expo TaskManager background sync), FastAPI sync service (POST /sync/push with per-record idempotency, GET /sync/pull delta by last_sync_at), FastAPI compliance service (audit trail query, export template rendering), Cognito user pool (MFA enforced for supervisor/manager roles), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (checklist_completions, temperature_records, audit_trail_events), Redis ElastiCache (sync session locks, rate limit counters), S3 (exported compliance PDFs with server-side encryption), ALB with WAF (rate limiting, OWASP rule set).
target_output.workflows: Offline Checklist Completion and Sync: Staff log in (Cognito, cached JWT in Expo SecureStore), app pre-fetches active checklist templates and location config into WatermelonDB on login. During shift, staff complete opening temperature checks, allergen station confirmations, and sanitization logs — all persisted locally with sync_status=pending. When network restored, Expo TaskManager triggers POST /sync/push with all pending records as a batch; server processes each record, enforces (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) unique constraint, returns per-record status array. Mobile client iterates response: synced records marked sync_status=synced, failed records marked sync_status=failed and scheduled for retry with exponential backoff (30s, 2m, 8m, 30m); after 4 failures supervisor alert is surfaced. Compliance Audit Trail Generation: After sync, server writes each accepted completion to audit_trail_events with SHA-256 hash of the completion payload chained to the previous event hash for the location. Compliance managers request an export via GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y, server renders the records using the registered jurisdiction template and returns a signed S3 PDF URL valid for 1 hour.
target_output.data_model: safety_checklists (id UUID PK, location_id FK, checklist_type ENUM opening/closing/hourly/custom, template_version INT, active BOOL, created_at). checklist_completions (id UUID PK client-generated idempotency key, checklist_id FK, staff_id FK, location_id FK, shift_date DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completion_hash VARCHAR, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed/retrying, UNIQUE(checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date)). temperature_records (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, probe_point VARCHAR, celsius_value DECIMAL, within_range BOOL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, allergen_type VARCHAR, confirmed_by_staff_id FK, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). equipment_sanitization_logs (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, equipment_id FK, sanitizer_type VARCHAR, contact_time_seconds INT, pass BOOL). audit_trail_events (id UUID PK, location_id FK, sequence_num BIGINT, action VARCHAR, actor_id FK, record_id UUID, content_hash VARCHAR, chained_hash VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(location_id, sequence_num)). location_profiles (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, jurisdiction_code VARCHAR, export_format_version VARCHAR, timezone VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /sync/push body: {records: [{id, checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, items: [...], submitted_at}]} returns {results: [{id, status: synced|conflict|error, existing_record_id?}]}. GET /sync/pull?location_id=X&since=ISO8601 returns delta of records modified since timestamp. GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y&to_date=Z returns {download_url, expires_at}. GET /checklists?location_id=X&active=true returns checklist templates for pre-fetch. PATCH /checklists/{id}/completions/{cid}/approve for supervisor sign-off. Auth: Cognito JWT bearer on all endpoints; location_id scope enforced server-side.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FSMA compliance: all checklist_completion and temperature_record data retained 3 years with automated RDS snapshot lifecycle policy. HACCP audit integrity: SHA-256 content hash per completion record, chained to per-location sequence in audit_trail_events; hash chain verification endpoint available for health inspector review. Cognito MFA mandatory for supervisor and manager roles; staff use PIN-based custom auth challenge. Expo SecureStore for JWT token storage on device (hardware-backed keystore on Android). TLS 1.3 on all API traffic; WAF OWASP rule set on ALB. RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency injection: staff scope to assigned location_id, managers have read-all. Allergen declaration records are immutable post-submission — PATCH forbidden on allergen_declarations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1: API service (min 2 tasks, max 6, CPU 60% scale-out), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary in us-east-1a, standby in us-east-1b, automated failover <60s. Redis ElastiCache single-node for sync session state (non-critical). ALB with WAF in front of ECS tasks. Buildkite CI pipeline: Python unit tests (pytest, 85% coverage gate), FastAPI integration tests against Dockerized PostgreSQL, Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight distribution) and Android (Play Store internal track), Lighthouse-equivalent API smoke tests against staging. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task before new API deployment. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. OTA updates for non-native JS changes via Expo EAS Update, bypassing app store review cycle.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI service: trace sync push/pull endpoints, alert on p99 latency >1.5s or error rate >2%. CloudWatch custom metrics: sync_queue_depth per location, conflict_rate (409 responses / total sync requests), retry_escalation_count (records reaching 4th retry). Sentry for React Native: crash reports tagged by device OS version and app version. Grafana dashboard (Datadog source): sync health per location, overdue checklist count by location, temperature exceedance rate by facility. PagerDuty alert on: API error rate >2% for 5 minutes, hash chain integrity check failure for any location, sync queue depth >500 records for >30 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month at 50 locations with 200 daily checklist completions. ECS Fargate auto-scales from 2 to 6 tasks on CPU threshold; peak sync load at shift-change handled by Redis-based sync session concurrency control. RDS Multi-AZ ~$350/month; Fargate tasks ~$250/month at baseline. S3 export storage minimal (<5GB/year). EAS Build credits managed under Expo plan. Scaling to 500 locations requires read replica for compliance dashboard queries and potential move to Aurora Serverless for cost efficiency at variable load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Cognito auth with MFA, opening and closing checklist templates for 10-location pilot, offline WatermelonDB storage, POST /sync/push and GET /sync/pull with idempotency and conflict resolution, basic admin dashboard showing completion rates, Buildkite CI with TestFlight distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Temperature probe integration (manual entry and future Bluetooth), allergen declaration workflow, equipment sanitization logs, health authority export templates for 3 target jurisdictions, audit trail hash chain verification UI. Phase 3 (8 weeks): IoT Bluetooth temperature probe auto-capture via Expo BLE, automated health department submission API integration, predictive non-conformance alerts based on historical temperature trend analysis, 500-location scalability hardening with Aurora Serverless migration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: black + isort + mypy strict in CI. FastAPI endpoints typed with Pydantic v2 models; no raw dict responses. 85% pytest coverage required for sync and compliance endpoints. React Native: ESLint + Prettier, TypeScript strict mode. WatermelonDB model changes require migration files in the models/migrations directory — no schema changes without a migration. All sync endpoint changes require integration test coverage against a Dockerized PostgreSQL instance in CI. FSMA retention policies encoded as Alembic data migrations, not application-layer logic, to ensure they survive code refactors. ADR documents required for any change to the conflict resolution or audit chain strategy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync protocol complexity — custom push/pull adds implementation burden vs. using a managed sync service. Mitigation: strict integration test suite for sync semantics; conflict resolution encapsulated in a single SyncService class. Risk: Expo OTA update delivery fails on devices with poor connectivity — compliance checklist templates could be stale. Mitigation: version-check at launch; if template_version mismatch exceeds 1 minor version, force full sync before allowing checklist access. Tradeoff: SHA-256 hash chaining adds per-record compute on server but is required for tamper-evident audit trail; acceptable overhead at expected record volume. Risk: iOS background sync limitations — Expo TaskManager background fetch is throttled by iOS. Mitigation: primary sync triggered on foreground resume; background fetch as secondary mechanism only. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for relational integrity of checklist-completion-temperature record hierarchy; cost higher but correctness critical for compliance data.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Composite unique key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) confirmed as the conflict detection boundary — a completion is per-staff per shift, not shared. Export template versioning uses server-side registry keyed by jurisdiction_code avoiding app store release dependency. WatermelonDB chosen over direct expo-sqlite for its built-in sync primitives and reactive query subscriptions. EAS Update (OTA) confirmed for JS-layer changes; native module changes still require app store submission. Buildkite selected over GitHub Actions for superior iOS simulator support and parallelism.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolution of sync conflict and retry semantics for HACCP-compliant offline-first mobile app.

project_goal.value: First online presence for a boutique artisan bakery — daily product availability, baker story, photo gallery, and wholesale contact form
project_goal.rationale: Baker needs a brand-building site to attract retail and wholesale customers without e-commerce complexity

target_users.value: Local retail customers browsing daily availability, and wholesale buyers submitting inquiry forms
target_users.rationale: Two distinct audience segments identified by the bakery owner

project_class.rationale: No cart, no auth, no database — purely informational and brand content

capabilities.rationale: Static rendering only; form submissions handled via Netlify Forms

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with YAML-driven daily availability and a contact form

risk_level.rationale: No sensitive data, no transactions, no server-side processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal data collected or stored on self-managed infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Fully public site — no authentication or access controls needed

access_model.value: Fully public; no login required; wholesale inquiry form captures name and email via Netlify Forms only
access_model.rationale: Open access for brand discovery

feature_scope.value: Daily product availability page driven by YAML data file, about-the-baker story with photography, baked goods photo gallery, wholesale inquiry contact form
feature_scope.rationale: All features identified by bakery owner in discovery session

mvp_scope.value: Home page, daily availability page, baker story page, gallery, contact form — all deployable as a single Netlify site
mvp_scope.rationale: Full site is MVP; no post-launch feature phases required for initial launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced by Netlify, basic CSP headers, no cookies except Netlify Forms session token
security_baseline.rationale: Standard static site security for a low-risk public site

frontend_stack.value: Eleventy (11ty) 3.x with HTML/CSS and Nunjucks templates
frontend_stack.rationale: 11ty zero-JavaScript default aligns with simple bakery site; baker-friendly YAML data files for daily availability

backend_stack.value: None — static site; form submissions via Netlify Forms to baker email
backend_stack.rationale: No server-side runtime justified for this scope

data_platform.value: None — daily availability stored as YAML in _data/availability.yaml in the GitHub repository
data_platform.rationale: Baker updates availability by editing a single YAML file; no CMS overhead

hosting_target.value: Netlify free tier with custom domain
hosting_target.rationale: Zero hosting cost, automatic HTTPS, built-in form handling

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data stored on self-managed infrastructure; Netlify Forms submission data governed by Netlify data processing terms; baker deletes form submissions after responding
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Minimal data footprint for a small business operator

future_scope.value: Online pre-order form for weekly bread subscription, Instagram feed embed, seasonal menu archive
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch enhancements if site drives wholesale interest

constraints.value: Baker must be able to update daily availability without a developer; total hosting cost zero on free tier; site must load in under 2 seconds on mobile
constraints.rationale: Owner technical constraints and budget reality for a small artisan business

observability_baseline.value: Plausible Analytics (privacy-respecting, no cookie consent required) for page view and referral tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: Plausible requires no GDPR consent banner, reducing friction for a simple informational site

execution_preference.value: Single sprint delivery with baker reviewing staging site before DNS cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Small project scope supports single-sprint delivery

project.goal: Artisan bakery first online presence with daily availability and wholesale contact
project.target_users: Local retail customers and wholesale buyers

frontend.styling: Custom CSS with CSS custom properties for brand colors
frontend.gallery: CSS grid gallery with lazy-loaded images

data.forms: Netlify Forms submissions to baker email

devops.environments: Netlify deploy previews for PRs, production on main branch push

reasoner_reviews.product: The daily availability YAML workflow is the right solution for a non-technical baker — it must be the simplest possible edit: open availability.yaml, change a quantity or add a line, commit. The photo gallery must showcase the baked goods effectively with high-quality images; lazy loading is essential for mobile performance given the image-heavy nature.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Eleventy is well-suited here — zero client-side JavaScript by default, fast builds, and YAML data files are a natural fit for the availability update workflow. Netlify Forms removes all form infrastructure. The main technical risk is image optimization: unoptimized gallery photos will blow the 2-second mobile load time budget.
reasoner_reviews.security: Netlify Forms handles spam filtering via Akismet integration. CSP should block all inline scripts and only allow the Plausible Analytics script domain. No cookies are set by the site itself — Plausible is cookieless. Netlify enforces HTTPS.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Free Netlify tier supports 100GB bandwidth and 300 build minutes per month — sufficient for a local bakery site. Image optimization at build time is the main constraint to address for the 2-second mobile load target.

frontend.app_structure: Eleventy project with _data/ for YAML data, _includes/ for Nunjucks layout and partials, src/pages/ for page templates, src/assets/ for CSS and images.
frontend.pages_and_flows: / (home with hero, daily availability summary, gallery preview), /availability (full daily product listing from YAML), /about (baker biography and bakery story), /gallery (full baked goods photo grid), /wholesale (inquiry form)
frontend.performance_notes: Images processed at build time via eleventy-img plugin generating WebP at 400px, 800px, 1200px breakpoints with responsive srcset. Lazy loading via loading=lazy on below-fold images. Plausible Analytics script deferred. CSS inlined for critical path. Total page weight target: < 200KB for non-gallery pages.
frontend.frontend_risks: Baker may commit oversized raw photos to _data/images/; a GitHub Actions CI step should validate that no source image exceeds 3MB before merge.

security.auth_design: No authentication required
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify Forms honeypot field to block bot submissions; Akismet spam filtering enabled on form
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by design; no consent banner required. No cookies set by the site.
security.security_risks: Netlify Forms submission data accessible to baker via Netlify dashboard — baker must be trained not to share dashboard credentials.

data.entities: product_listings (YAML: name, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, price_display, available_qty, notes), daily_availability (YAML: date, items[]), gallery_images (filesystem: filename, alt_text, category), baker_profiles (YAML: name, bio, years_baking, photo_filename), wholesale_inquiry_forms (Netlify Forms submission: contact_name, business_name, email, inquiry_text)
data.storage_design: All content in GitHub repository as YAML data files and image assets. No external database or CMS.
data.data_risks: Baker committing incorrect YAML syntax could fail the build; a GitHub Actions YAML lint step prevents malformed data from deploying.

devops.deployment_topology: Push to main branch triggers Netlify build (npx @11ty/eleventy) → eleventy-img processes and optimizes all gallery images → static output deployed to Netlify CDN. PR branches get automatic Netlify deploy preview with unique URL.
devops.environments: Production: Netlify site with custom domain (artisanbakery.com) and auto-provisioned TLS cert. Preview: Netlify deploy previews per PR branch for baker review.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: validate YAML syntax of availability.yaml and product data files, check no source image exceeds 3MB, run Eleventy build to confirm no template errors. Netlify handles the production deployment on main push.
devops.devops_risks: Netlify free tier build minute limit (300/month) is adequate for this project volume. Risk: baker accidentally pushing to main without PR preview review — mitigate by configuring branch protection requiring PR.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a first-pass plan for a minimalist artisan bakery static site. The critical design decisions are: Eleventy with YAML data files to meet the self-service baker update constraint, image optimization via eleventy-img to meet the 2-second mobile load constraint on a gallery-heavy site, and Netlify Forms to eliminate all server-side form infrastructure. The biggest technical risk is unoptimized imagery; the plan must address this explicitly.
target_output.title: Artisan Bakery Static Website Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-server static website for a boutique artisan bakery built with Eleventy 3.x and deployed to Netlify free tier. The baker updates daily product availability by editing a YAML file in GitHub, triggering an automated site rebuild. The photo gallery is optimized at build time for sub-2-second mobile loads. Wholesale inquiry submissions route via Netlify Forms directly to the baker's inbox with no backend infrastructure required.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Eleventy site: HTML generated at build time from Nunjucks templates and YAML data sources. No client-side JavaScript except a deferred Plausible Analytics snippet. Daily availability data lives in _data/availability.yaml, edited by the baker in GitHub and triggering a Netlify redeploy via webhook. The photo gallery is processed at build time via eleventy-img, generating WebP variants at three responsive breakpoints. Netlify serves the static output globally via its CDN with automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Eleventy 3.x, Nunjucks templating, custom CSS with CSS custom properties. Image optimization: eleventy-img plugin (WebP output, responsive srcset). Forms: Netlify Forms with Akismet spam filtering. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, GDPR-compliant). Hosting: Netlify free tier with custom domain. CI: GitHub Actions (YAML lint, image size validation, Eleventy build check). Version control: GitHub.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Daily Availability Page: renders product_listings from _data/availability.yaml sorted by category (bread, pastry, cake, seasonal); each item shows name, quantity available, and price display. Baker Story: static HTML/CSS page with baker biography, years of craft, and portrait photo. Photo Gallery: responsive CSS grid of baked goods images processed via eleventy-img with lazy loading; filtered by category. Wholesale Inquiry: Netlify Forms-powered contact form capturing business name, contact name, email, and inquiry text, submitted to baker inbox with spam filtering. Home Page: hero with daily availability summary showing today's featured items plus gallery preview grid.
target_output.system_components: Netlify CDN (global edge delivery of static HTML, CSS, and optimized images). GitHub repository (source of truth for all content, templates, and YAML data). eleventy-img (build-time image processing pipeline). Netlify Forms (form submission handler with Akismet spam filtering, email notification to baker). Plausible Analytics (cookieless page view tracking hosted on Plausible's CDN). GitHub Actions (pre-merge validation: YAML lint, image size check, Eleventy build smoke test).
target_output.workflows: Daily Availability Update: Baker opens _data/availability.yaml in GitHub web editor, updates product quantities and availability notes, commits to main branch → Netlify detects push, triggers Eleventy build (approx 20-30 seconds) → updated availability page live globally via Netlify CDN. Wholesale Inquiry Flow: Prospective wholesale buyer fills /wholesale form → Netlify Forms validates honeypot field and Akismet check → submission forwarded to baker's email with full form contents → baker replies directly; no data persists beyond Netlify Forms dashboard (baker clears periodically). Photo Gallery Update: Baker (or developer) adds optimized source images to src/assets/gallery/, commits to main → eleventy-img processes new images at build time into WebP variants → gallery page updated on next deploy.
target_output.data_model: product_listings (YAML: name STRING, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, available_qty INTEGER, price_display STRING, notes STRING optional). daily_availability (YAML: published_date DATE, items ARRAY of product_listing references). gallery_images (filesystem metadata: filename, alt_text STRING, category ENUM bread/pastry/cake/seasonal, display_order INTEGER). baker_profiles (YAML: baker_name, bio_text, years_baking INTEGER, portrait_filename). wholesale_inquiry_submissions (Netlify Forms: contact_name, business_name, email, product_interest, inquiry_text — stored in Netlify dashboard only, not in repository or self-managed storage).
target_output.api_design: No self-hosted API. External integrations only: Netlify Forms POST https://www.netlify.com/form-handler (browser-native form action, no JavaScript required). Plausible Analytics script GET https://plausible.io/js/script.js (deferred). Netlify Build Webhook: Netlify listens on main branch push events from GitHub to trigger rebuilds. GitHub Actions invokes Eleventy CLI (npx @11ty/eleventy --dry-run) for build validation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Netlify with auto-renewed Let's Encrypt certificate. CSP header via netlify.toml: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://plausible.io; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Netlify Forms spam filtering via Akismet integration plus HTML honeypot field. Plausible is cookieless — no GDPR consent banner required. No cookies set by the site. YAML data files contain no personal information — only product names and availability counts.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Netlify build command: npx @11ty/eleventy. Publish directory: _site. Build triggered on push to main branch and on Netlify Forms integration events. Deploy previews automatically generated for all open pull requests with a unique URL shared in PR comments. Baker workflow: edit availability.yaml via GitHub web UI → commit to main → site rebuilds in ~25 seconds → live. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous successful deploy from the Netlify deploy history dashboard. Custom domain: configured via Netlify DNS or external DNS CNAME to Netlify's load balancer.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard showing daily unique visitors, top pages (availability, gallery, wholesale), and referral sources (Instagram, Google, direct). Netlify deploy notifications via email on build failure. GitHub Actions status checks visible in PR view. No APM or error tracking required for a zero-JavaScript static site. Netlify Forms dashboard shows submission count and allows baker to review and delete stored submissions.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Total monthly cost: $0. Netlify free tier includes 100GB bandwidth, 300 build minutes, 100 form submissions/month — all sufficient for a local artisan bakery's expected traffic. Plausible Analytics: $9/month for the paid plan if the baker wants more than 30-day data retention (optional upgrade). Custom domain registration ~$15/year via Namecheap or Google Domains. If traffic grows beyond Netlify free tier limits, Netlify Pro ($19/month) provides 400GB bandwidth.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Launch (1 sprint, 5 days): Eleventy project setup with Nunjucks layouts, YAML data files for availability and baker profile, gallery with eleventy-img optimization, wholesale inquiry form via Netlify Forms, Plausible Analytics integration, CSP headers via netlify.toml, GitHub Actions CI, deploy to Netlify with custom domain. No phase 2 required for MVP — the full feature set is the initial delivery. Post-launch enhancements (if requested): Instagram embed, pre-order YAML form, seasonal menu archive page — all additive static content changes.
target_output.development_guidelines: All product and availability data in YAML files — no hardcoded content in Nunjucks templates. Image optimization required via eleventy-img; raw img tags forbidden. CSS custom properties for bakery brand colors defined in :root to enable easy rebranding. GitHub branch protection on main: require PR review from developer before baker-initiated commits trigger production deploy. YAML lint (yamllint) enforced in GitHub Actions. No npm packages with node_modules committed to repository.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Baker commits invalid YAML syntax causing a failed build. Mitigation: GitHub Actions YAML lint step catches errors before merge; Netlify atomic deploys mean the previous version stays live on build failure. Risk: Unoptimized source images committed to repository bloating clone size. Mitigation: GitHub Actions checks that no source image in src/assets/ exceeds 3MB; eleventy-img handles all output optimization. Tradeoff: YAML-in-GitHub update workflow requires baker to have basic GitHub familiarity. Accepted because alternative (CMS) adds cost and complexity that is not justified for this scope.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Eleventy over Hugo for more familiar Nunjucks templating for the developer. Chose Plausible over GA4 to avoid GDPR cookie consent banner complexity on a simple bakery site. Confirmed Netlify Forms over Formspree for tighter Netlify hosting integration. No CMS needed — YAML data files are sufficient for this content volume.

metadata.notes: Greenfield static bakery site with YAML-driven daily availability updates and Netlify Forms wholesale inquiry handling.

project_goal.value: AI system that generates personalized dish recommendations and dynamic wine/beverage pairing suggestions for restaurant guests via table-side tablet, using LLM-driven conversational responses informed by dietary preferences, allergy flags, flavor history, and the current seasonal menu
project_goal.rationale: Core product differentiator for participating restaurants to increase guest satisfaction and upsell

target_users.value: Restaurant guests at the table via tablet; restaurant managers and sommelier staff configuring menu and preference rules
target_users.rationale: Dual audience: guests interacting in real time and staff configuring the system

project_class.rationale: LLM-driven recommendation engine with guest profiling and conversational interface

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full stack with LLM integration and guest preference analytics

complexity_level.rationale: LLM prompt construction with dynamic guest context, flavor history modeling, and allergen safety constraints

risk_level.rationale: Personal preference data and allergen flags require careful handling but not health-record-level controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Guest profiles include dietary restrictions, flavor preferences, and visit history — personal data under GDPR/CCPA

external_exposure.rationale: Tablet API is accessible from restaurant network; guest sessions initiated without pre-existing account

access_model.value: Guest sessions are anonymous or loyalty-linked; restaurant staff authenticate via Firebase Auth with role-based access to admin and menu configuration panels
access_model.rationale: Guests interact without mandatory account creation; staff need authenticated admin access

feature_scope.value: Conversational dish recommendations, wine and beverage pairing suggestions, allergen-safe filtering, flavor preference history tracking, ingredient question answering, post-visit preference learning, staff menu configuration panel, recommendation analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Complete guest-facing and staff-facing feature set

mvp_scope.value: Allergen-safe dish recommendations, wine pairing suggestions, conversational ingredient Q&A, anonymous guest sessions with optional loyalty profile linking
mvp_scope.rationale: Core guest value without requiring loyalty program integration at launch

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, allergen flags treated as safety-critical and never overridden by LLM output, guest data pseudonymized at rest, staff access via MFA-enforced Firebase Auth
security_baseline.rationale: Allergen safety is a liability risk requiring deterministic enforcement outside LLM control

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, optimized for 10-inch touch tablets in kiosk mode
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and tablet browser compatibility

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance for LLM streaming responses and ML team familiarity with Python

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for guest profiles and menu data; Redis 7 for active session context caching
data_platform.rationale: Relational store for structured menu and guest data; Redis for low-latency session context retrieval

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API backend; Firebase Hosting for tablet frontend
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run serverless scaling for variable restaurant traffic; Firebase Hosting for global CDN delivery

privacy_retention_policy.value: Guest flavor history retained 2 years or until deletion request; anonymous session data purged after 30 days; GDPR right-to-erasure endpoint required
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR and CCPA compliance for guest personal preference data

future_scope.value: Native mobile app for pre-visit preference setting, integration with reservation systems to pre-populate guest context, sommelier knowledge base fine-tuning
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap following tablet MVP validation

constraints.value: LLM must never suggest dishes containing flagged allergens regardless of prompt context; recommendations must complete within 3 seconds P95; tablet operates on restaurant Wi-Fi which may be intermittent
constraints.rationale: Safety, UX, and environment constraints

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for infrastructure, LLM call latency and token usage dashboards, recommendation acceptance rate tracking
observability_baseline.rationale: Core operational and product metrics for the LLM-driven system

execution_preference.value: Agile with 2-week sprints; LLM prompt engineering iterated in parallel with feature development
execution_preference.rationale: Prompt quality requires continuous iteration alongside code

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU restaurant deployments (guest consent for preference profiling, right-to-erasure); CCPA for California deployments; allergen disclosure obligations under EU FIC Regulation and US FDA Food Labeling requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Personal preference data and allergen information trigger GDPR/CCPA and food safety labeling compliance

llm_integration.value: Google Gemini 1.5 Pro via Vertex AI for conversational recommendations; system prompt constructed with current seasonal menu, guest allergen flags, flavor history top-5 preferences, and table context; allergen filtering enforced deterministically before LLM prompt construction, not by LLM output
llm_integration.rationale: Vertex AI for GCP-native integration; allergen safety must be deterministic, not LLM-dependent

project.goal: LLM-driven personalized menu recommendations and pairing suggestions for table-side tablets
project.target_users: Restaurant guests (anonymous or loyalty-linked) and staff administrators

frontend.approach: Tablet kiosk mode, touch-optimized, offline-graceful degradation

backend.framework: FastAPI with async LLM streaming endpoints

security.auth: Firebase Auth for staff; anonymous JWT for guest sessions; allergen safety deterministic enforcement

data.llm: Google Gemini 1.5 Pro via Vertex AI

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual-audience design (guest tablet + staff admin) is right for the product. Anonymous session support with optional loyalty linking reduces onboarding friction for guests who do not want to create accounts. The allergen Q&A feature is high-value but creates liability risk that must be explicitly addressed in the architecture — the system should never be the sole source of truth for allergy safety.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with Vertex AI streaming via Server-Sent Events is the correct approach for responsive LLM output on the tablet. Guest session context cached in Redis with a 2-hour TTL aligns with typical dining duration and avoids redundant PostgreSQL reads on every message. The flavor history model should be a ranked preference vector (not raw order history) to keep prompt context manageable and prevent token bloat at scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: Allergen filtering must execute as a deterministic server-side pass before the menu items are included in the LLM prompt — not as a post-processing step on LLM output. LLM models can hallucinate item names or ignore negative constraints under adversarial prompts. Pseudonymization of guest_id in the analytics pipeline separates behavioral data from PII.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-second P95 latency requires prompt caching and pre-built menu embeddings. Vertex AI streaming helps perceived responsiveness even when total generation time exceeds the target. Intermittent restaurant Wi-Fi requires the tablet to gracefully degrade to showing a static menu if the API is unreachable, rather than showing an error screen.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Flavor history modeling needs a defined schema for 'preference' — raw order history alone is noisy. The system should track explicit preference signals (user thumbs-up/down on suggestions) and implicit signals (order selection matching recommendation) separately, with explicit signals weighted higher.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with four domain modules: recommendations (LLM orchestration and prompt construction), menu (menu item retrieval and allergen-filtered candidate set building), guest-profiles (preference history read/write), analytics (recommendation acceptance event ingestion). LLM calls are async with Server-Sent Events streaming to the tablet frontend.
backend.llm_integration_design: Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro for conversational recommendations. Prompt template: system role as restaurant sommelier and menu expert, injected with current menu JSON (allergen-filtered to guest flags), guest top-5 flavor preferences from flavor_histories table, and conversation history (last 6 turns). Allergen-safe candidate set computed before prompt assembly — excluded items never appear in the prompt. Max prompt tokens capped at 8000 to control cost.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/sessions (create anonymous or loyalty-linked session), POST /api/v1/sessions/:id/recommend (streaming SSE endpoint for LLM recommendations), GET /api/v1/menu/items?allergen_safe=true (allergen-filtered menu for session), POST /api/v1/sessions/:id/feedback (record recommendation acceptance), POST /api/v1/guests/:id/erasure (GDPR right-to-erasure).
backend.failure_handling: LLM timeout (>5s): return static top-rated dishes for the session's dietary profile from PostgreSQL without LLM call. Vertex AI rate limit: queue with Redis-backed job queue, stream partial response placeholder while queuing. Allergen filter error: fail closed — return empty candidate set and display dietary restriction advisory rather than risking unsafe recommendation.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA configured for kiosk mode (no browser chrome, full-screen, touch events). Route structure: /session (session start + loyalty linking), /recommend (main recommendation chat interface), /menu (allergen-filtered full menu browse), /staff/login and /staff/dashboard (gated behind Firebase Auth).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Guest Session Flow: tablet displays welcome screen, guest optionally scans loyalty QR code or continues anonymously, inputs dietary restrictions via touch checklist (gluten-free, nut allergy, vegan, etc.), enters recommendation chat. Recommendation Chat: conversational interface with streaming LLM responses, dish cards with photos and allergen badges, one-tap 'add to interest' action. Staff Dashboard: menu item management, allergen data editor, recommendation acceptance rate analytics.
frontend.performance_notes: Streaming SSE rendering with progressive text display prevents blank wait screen during LLM generation. Menu item images served via Firebase Hosting CDN with WebP format and lazy loading. Service Worker caches menu item data and images for 1-hour offline fallback.

security.auth_design: Guest sessions: anonymous JWT issued by POST /sessions, 4-hour expiry matching max dining duration. Loyalty-linked sessions: guest scans QR code that contains a signed loyalty token; backend verifies signature and links session to guest_profile. Staff: Firebase Auth with Google Workspace SSO and MFA enforcement.
security.authorization_model: Guest JWT authorizes access only to their own session data. Staff roles: menu_editor (item CRUD), analytics_viewer (read-only dashboard), admin (full access). All allergen data mutations require menu_editor or admin role.
security.privacy_controls: guest_id pseudonymized with SHA-256 HMAC in analytics event tables. flavor_histories.guest_id references a pseudonym, not raw PII. GDPR erasure endpoint deletes guest_profiles row and sets flavor_histories.guest_id to anonymized tombstone.

data.entities: guest_profiles (id UUID PK, loyalty_id VARCHAR nullable, email_encrypted VARCHAR nullable, allergen_flags TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). flavor_histories (id UUID PK, guest_id UUID FK, flavor_attribute VARCHAR, preference_score FLOAT, signal_type ENUM explicit/implicit, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). menu_items (id UUID PK, restaurant_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, description TEXT, category VARCHAR, allergens TEXT[], price_cents INT, available BOOLEAN, season_tags TEXT[]). pairing_recommendations (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, item_ids UUID[], recommendation_text TEXT, accepted BOOLEAN nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). conversation_sessions (id UUID PK, guest_id UUID FK nullable, restaurant_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, turn_count INT).
data.analytics_events: recommendation_shown (session_id, item_ids, model_version), recommendation_accepted (session_id, item_id), flavor_signal_recorded (guest_id_pseudonym, flavor_attribute, signal_type).

devops.deployment_topology: GCP Cloud Run for FastAPI backend: serverless, scales to zero between meal services, concurrency 80 per instance. Firebase Hosting for React tablet frontend. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private VPC access. Redis via Memorystore. Vertex AI API calls from Cloud Run with Workload Identity — no API key files.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on push to main, run pytest + mypy, build container, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run staging. On tag v*, promote to production with canary split (10% traffic for 30 minutes, then full rollout).
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboards: LLM p50/p95 latency, token usage per request, recommendation acceptance rate. Cloud Logging with structured JSON. Alerting: LLM error rate >5% in 5 minutes triggers PagerDuty page.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an LLM-driven guest recommendation system where allergen safety is the highest-priority constraint — implemented deterministically before prompt construction, not delegated to LLM output filtering. Guest session context is cached in Redis to avoid repeated PostgreSQL reads on each conversation turn. Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro via Cloud Run Workload Identity removes API key management. Flavor history as a ranked preference vector rather than raw order history keeps prompt context tokens bounded and improves recommendation relevance.
target_output.title: Table-Side AI Menu Recommendation System Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An LLM-driven AI system for restaurant table-side tablets that generates personalized dish recommendations and wine pairings through conversational interaction. Built on Python 3.12 FastAPI with Google Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro, React 18 tablet frontend, and PostgreSQL for guest profiles and flavor history on GCP Cloud Run. Guest allergen flags are enforced deterministically before LLM prompt construction — excluded items never appear in the model context. Anonymous guest sessions and optional loyalty profile linking balance privacy with personalization.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer architecture on GCP: React 18 tablet frontend served via Firebase Hosting CDN, FastAPI backend on Cloud Run with streaming SSE responses, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 plus Memorystore Redis for session context. Guest sessions start with allergen declaration; the backend builds an allergen-filtered menu candidate set from PostgreSQL, loads the guest's top flavor preferences from flavor_histories, and constructs a Vertex AI prompt capped at 8000 tokens. LLM responses stream via Server-Sent Events to provide progressive display on the tablet while generation completes. Staff access a separate admin panel backed by the same API with Firebase Auth MFA enforcement. Allergen safety is never delegated to LLM output — it is enforced by the candidate-set filter that runs before the model sees any item names.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript 5.4, React Query for session state, Framer Motion for animated dish card transitions, Service Worker for offline menu cache. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, httpx for async Vertex AI calls, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg driver, pydantic-settings for config. LLM: Google Vertex AI Gemini 1.5 Pro (streaming). Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16. Cache: Memorystore Redis 7. Auth: Firebase Auth (staff), anonymous JWT with PyJWT (guests). Hosting: GCP Cloud Run, Firebase Hosting, Artifact Registry. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Google Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Guest allergen declaration: touch checklist on session start capturing 14 EU-regulated allergens plus custom flags; stored in Redis session and PostgreSQL guest_profiles if loyalty-linked. Conversational recommendations: SSE-streaming LLM responses with dish cards rendered progressively; Gemini prompt includes allergen-filtered menu, guest flavor preferences, last 6 conversation turns. Wine and beverage pairing: LLM system prompt includes sommelier persona and wine list; pairing suggestions streamed inline with food recommendations. Ingredient Q&A: guests ask 'what is in the duck confit?' and LLM responds from menu item description context. Recommendation feedback: one-tap accept/decline on dish cards updates flavor_histories with explicit signals. Staff menu editor: item CRUD with allergen declaration form and availability toggle; changes invalidate Redis menu cache. Analytics dashboard: recommendation acceptance rates, top-accepted items by week, average session turn count.
target_output.system_components: Firebase Hosting: serves compiled React tablet app with 1-hour menu data Service Worker cache. Cloud Run (FastAPI): recommendations module (LLM orchestration), menu module (allergen-filtered candidate building), guest-profiles module (flavor history CRUD), analytics module (event ingestion). Vertex AI: Gemini 1.5 Pro accessed via Workload Identity from Cloud Run — no API key secrets. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16: guest_profiles, flavor_histories, menu_items, pairing_recommendations, conversation_sessions. Memorystore Redis 7: active session context (guest allergen flags + flavor summary, 4-hour TTL), menu item cache (1-hour TTL). Firebase Auth: staff Google Workspace SSO with MFA.
target_output.workflows: Guest Recommendation Flow: Guest sits at tablet, taps 'Start' on welcome screen. Session created via POST /api/v1/sessions; anonymous JWT returned. Guest selects allergen flags on touch checklist. Backend builds allergen-filtered menu candidate set from PostgreSQL, caches in Redis session key. Guest types or speaks 'What should I try tonight? I love umami flavors.' Backend loads flavor_histories top-5 preferences, constructs Gemini prompt with filtered menu and preferences. SSE stream opens; dish recommendation cards render progressively as tokens arrive. Guest taps 'Sounds great' on the lamb ragu card — feedback event recorded to pairing_recommendations.accepted=true, flavor_histories updated with implicit umami signal. Post-Visit Learning Flow: After session ends, a Cloud Run job processes all accepted/declined recommendations for the session, computes updated flavor preference scores using weighted explicit+implicit signals, and upserts flavor_histories rows for loyalty-linked guests to improve next-visit recommendations.
target_output.data_model: guest_profiles (id UUID PK, loyalty_id VARCHAR UNIQUE nullable, email_encrypted VARCHAR nullable, allergen_flags TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}', preferred_language VARCHAR DEFAULT 'en', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). flavor_histories (id UUID PK, guest_id UUID FK REFERENCES guest_profiles, flavor_attribute VARCHAR NOT NULL, preference_score FLOAT NOT NULL, signal_type ENUM explicit/implicit, sample_count INT, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). menu_items (id UUID PK, restaurant_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, description TEXT, category VARCHAR, allergens TEXT[], price_cents INT, available BOOLEAN, season_tags TEXT[], updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). conversation_sessions (id UUID PK, guest_id UUID FK nullable, restaurant_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, turn_count INT). pairing_recommendations (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, recommended_item_ids UUID[], recommendation_type ENUM dish/wine/pairing, model_version VARCHAR, accepted BOOLEAN nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_flags_reference (code VARCHAR PK, name VARCHAR, eu_regulated BOOLEAN) — static lookup for 14 EU allergens.
target_output.api_design: Guest API: POST /api/v1/sessions (create session, returns anonymous JWT + session_id), POST /api/v1/sessions/:id/messages (send message, returns SSE stream of LLM response with dish cards), POST /api/v1/sessions/:id/feedback {item_id, accepted: bool}, GET /api/v1/menu/items?session_id=:id (allergen-filtered menu), POST /api/v1/guests/:id/erasure (GDPR deletion). Staff API (Firebase Auth JWT required): GET /api/v1/staff/menu/items, POST /api/v1/staff/menu/items, PATCH /api/v1/staff/menu/items/:id {available, allergens, price_cents}, GET /api/v1/staff/analytics/recommendations?period=7d. All errors return {error: {code, message, request_id, safe_for_display: bool}}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Allergen safety: deterministic server-side filter builds allergen_safe_item_ids set from menu_items WHERE NOT allergens && guest_allergen_flags before any LLM call; filtered set is the only items included in the prompt — LLM cannot hallucinate excluded items. Disclaimer displayed to guests: 'For severe allergies, always confirm with your server.' GDPR/CCPA: guest_id pseudonymized in analytics events via HMAC-SHA256. Erasure endpoint deletes guest_profiles, replaces flavor_histories.guest_id with SHA-256 tombstone. Consent banner on session start for preference profiling with opt-out. Guest JWT: 4-hour expiry, HS256, session-scoped. Staff JWT: Firebase Auth tokens with MFA enforcement. TLS 1.3 enforced on Cloud Run ingress. Vertex AI called via Workload Identity — no static API key stored in secrets management.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run: FastAPI container on python:3.12-slim, max-instances 20, min-instances 1 (to avoid cold start during dinner service), concurrency 80. Artifact Registry for container images. Firebase Hosting deployed via firebase deploy in CI. Cloud SQL: PostgreSQL 16, db-n1-standard-2, private VPC connector for Cloud Run access, automated daily backups with 7-day retention, point-in-time recovery enabled. Memorystore Redis 7: 1GB Basic tier. GitHub Actions: on push to main — pytest, mypy strict, docker build, push to staging Cloud Run. On tag v* — canary deploy 10% traffic, automated smoke test hitting /api/v1/health and POST /sessions, full rollout after 30 minutes if error rate <1%.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboards: LLM call p50/p95/p99 latency, token usage per request (input + output tokens tracked separately), recommendation acceptance rate by restaurant, session turn count histogram. Cloud Logging structured JSON: every LLM call logs session_id, model_version, prompt_token_count, completion_token_count, allergen_filter_applied, duration_ms. Alerts: LLM error rate >5% in 5-minute window, P95 latency >4s (sustained 10 min), recommendation acceptance rate drop >20% week-over-week triggers product review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run scales to zero between meal services — primary cost driver is Vertex AI token usage. At 50 tables per restaurant with 10-turn average sessions: ~500 LLM calls/dinner service. Gemini 1.5 Pro at ~8000 input + 500 output tokens: approximately $0.25/dinner service per restaurant. Monthly estimated: $8-15 per restaurant for LLM costs plus ~$60 for Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, and Memorystore. Scaling path: Cloud Run max-instances 20 handles 1600 concurrent sessions; beyond that, increase max-instances and upgrade Cloud SQL tier.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Guest allergen declaration and anonymous sessions, allergen-filtered LLM recommendations via Vertex AI streaming SSE, dish and wine pairing suggestions, React tablet kiosk frontend with dish cards, staff menu editor with allergen fields, Firebase Auth for staff, Cloud Run + Cloud SQL + Redis deployment, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Loyalty profile linking via QR code scan, flavor history recording (explicit and implicit signals), post-visit preference learning job, GDPR erasure endpoint, analytics dashboard for staff. Phase 3 (future): Native mobile pre-visit preference app, reservation system integration for pre-populated guest context, sommelier knowledge base RAG for detailed pairing rationale.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: mypy strict mode, ruff linting, pytest with >80% coverage on recommendation and allergen-filter modules. Allergen filter function covered by property-based tests (hypothesis) to prove safety invariants. React: TypeScript strict mode, React Testing Library for component tests. LLM prompt templates version-controlled as separate .txt files, not embedded in Python strings — enables A/B testing prompt variants without code deploys. All LLM calls include model_version in database records for retrospective analysis. Never log LLM prompt content containing guest allergen flags to Cloud Logging — only token counts and latency.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LLM hallucinates dish ingredients not in the menu description, leading to guest receiving incorrect information about allergen content. Mitigation: allergen safety is deterministic pre-filter; LLM disclaimer shown to guests for severe allergies; menu descriptions reviewed by staff for accuracy. Risk: Vertex AI rate limits during simultaneous dinner services across many restaurants. Mitigation: Redis queue with graceful degradation to static top-rated dish list. Tradeoff: Gemini 1.5 Pro over smaller fine-tuned model — better zero-shot culinary knowledge and pairing suggestions at higher cost; fine-tuning evaluated in Phase 3. Tradeoff: Server-Sent Events over WebSocket — simpler server implementation and Cloud Run compatibility at the cost of server-only push direction.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Google Vertex AI over OpenAI API for GCP-native Workload Identity authentication (no API key secrets) and data residency in GCP regions matching Cloud Run deployment. Chose anonymous sessions with optional loyalty linking over mandatory account creation to reduce guest friction. Selected flavor preference score vector over raw order history in LLM context to bound token usage. Allergen safety confirmed as deterministic pre-filter, not post-LLM output parsing.

metadata.notes: Greenfield AI recommendation system for restaurant tablets; allergen safety as deterministic constraint is the key architectural decision.

project_goal.value: Landing page for an AI-driven restaurant inventory optimization SaaS, capturing demo request leads via a food cost savings calculator and converting cost-conscious restaurant operators
project_goal.rationale: Primary goal is lead generation and conversion for the SaaS product

target_users.rationale: Primary buyer persona identified in go-to-market strategy

project.goal: High-converting landing page capturing demo request leads for AI inventory SaaS
project.target_users: Restaurant owners and operators

backend.api: Single POST /api/demo-request route forwarding to HubSpot

security.auth: None required

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint and type-check
devops.environments: preview (per PR), production

reasoner_reviews.product: The food cost savings calculator is the primary conversion driver — it must feel instant and personalized. Inputs should include monthly food revenue and current food cost %, outputting projected annual savings with a range. The CTA immediately following the calculator result is the highest-conversion placement. Testimonials should use real operator language about food cost percentages, not generic SaaS praise.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export to Vercel is the correct choice. The calculator should be a pure client-side React component with no API calls — all computation in the browser. The demo form submission via a single Next.js API route keeps infrastructure minimal. Defer GA4 script loading to after first user interaction to protect Lighthouse score.
reasoner_reviews.security: hCaptcha on the demo request form prevents spam lead pollution in HubSpot. GDPR cookie consent must gate GA4 initialization for EU visitors — use a consent management platform or inline consent logic before gtag() fires. No server-side data storage means minimal attack surface.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse >95 requires image optimization via next/image with AVIF/WebP output, no render-blocking CSS beyond critical path, and async loading of all analytics scripts. Vercel's edge CDN ensures <2s LCP globally without additional configuration.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 app directory with single page route (/) and API route (/api/demo-request); layout.tsx with GDPR consent banner logic; no dynamic routes required
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single page with sections: Hero (headline + CTA), FoodCostCalculator (interactive widget), ROIStats (static), Testimonials, PricingTiers, DemoRequestForm, Footer
frontend.state_management: React useState for calculator inputs (monthly_revenue, current_food_cost_pct) and computed savings output; no global state manager needed
frontend.performance_notes: next/image for all hero and case study images with AVIF/WebP, sizes attr for responsive; defer gtag and hCaptcha scripts with next/script strategy=lazyOnload; inline critical CSS via Tailwind purge; no external font loading — system font stack

security.auth_design: No authentication; public page
security.abuse_prevention: hCaptcha v2 invisible on demo request form submission; Vercel rate limiting on /api/demo-request (20 requests/hour per IP)
security.privacy_controls: GDPR consent banner gates GA4 gtag() initialization; consent stored in localStorage; no server-side cookies

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a Next.js static landing page with a single conversion goal: demo request leads for a restaurant AI SaaS. The calculator widget is purely client-side — no API call, instant feedback. The only server-side component is a thin Vercel Function forwarding form data to HubSpot. Key constraints are Lighthouse >95 and <2s LCP, which drives all technical decisions: next/image with AVIF, async analytics scripts, system fonts, minimal JS bundle. GDPR consent must gate GA4 before EU visitors interact with tracking.
target_output.title: Restaurant AI Inventory SaaS Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A statically exported Next.js 14 landing page deployed on Vercel for an AI-driven restaurant inventory optimization SaaS, converting food-cost-conscious restaurant operators into demo request leads. Features an interactive food cost savings calculator widget (pure client-side React), ROI statistics, operator testimonials, and a HubSpot-integrated demo request form with hCaptcha bot protection. GDPR consent management gates GA4 initialization for EU visitors. No backend database — all lead data flows directly to HubSpot CRM.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 app directory with static export (output: export) deployed to Vercel edge CDN. The landing page is a single server-rendered-to-static HTML page with client-side React islands for the calculator widget and form. A single Vercel Function (Node.js) handles POST /api/demo-request, validating hCaptcha token, then forwarding the lead payload to HubSpot Forms API. GA4 is loaded via next/script with strategy=lazyOnload, initialized only after GDPR consent is confirmed via localStorage flag. All images processed through next/image generating AVIF and WebP variants with responsive srcset attributes.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui form components, React 18 useState for calculator. Deployment: Vercel with static export and CDN edge delivery. Form backend: Vercel Functions (Node.js), HubSpot Forms API v3. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 (gtag.js, lazyOnload), Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals. Bot protection: hCaptcha v2 invisible. CI: GitHub Actions (ESLint, TypeScript type-check, next build). No database, no cache, no auth layer.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Food Cost Savings Calculator: inputs monthly_revenue (slider + number input) and current_food_cost_pct (slider), computes projected annual savings using a configurable savings_rate_factor constant (default 18% reduction), displays savings range with high/low bounds. Demo Request Form: name, email, restaurant count, current food cost %, optional message; hCaptcha invisible challenge on submit; on success shows confirmation and fires GA4 demo_request_submitted event. ROI Stats Section: static figures (avg 18% food cost reduction, 3-week payback period) sourced from case study data. Testimonials: 3 operator quotes with restaurant name, location, and food cost % improvement achieved. Pricing Tiers: 3 plan cards with feature comparison and CTA buttons.
target_output.system_components: Vercel edge CDN serving static HTML/CSS/JS bundle, next/image optimization pipeline generating AVIF and WebP variants, Vercel Function /api/demo-request (Node.js, hCaptcha verification + HubSpot API call), hCaptcha invisible widget loaded via next/script lazyOnload, GA4 gtag.js loaded via next/script lazyOnload behind consent gate, localStorage-based GDPR consent manager, HubSpot Forms API as external lead sink.
target_output.workflows: Calculator Interaction: Visitor adjusts monthly_revenue and food_cost_pct sliders; React useState recomputes projected_savings in real time with no API call; when savings result appears, GA4 fires calculator_result_viewed event with savings_bucket dimension. Demo Request Submission: Visitor completes form and clicks submit; hCaptcha invisible challenge fires in background; on token receipt, client POSTs to /api/demo-request with form data + captcha token; Vercel Function verifies token with hCaptcha siteverify API, then POSTs to HubSpot Forms API; on success, client shows thank-you state and fires demo_request_submitted GA4 event with lead_source=landing_calculator or lead_source=direct.
target_output.data_model: No server-side data model. Client state: calculator_state {monthly_revenue: number, food_cost_pct: number, projected_savings: number}. Form state: {name, email, restaurant_count, food_cost_pct, message, captcha_token, status: idle|submitting|success|error}. GA4 events: calculator_result_viewed (params: savings_bucket), demo_request_submitted (params: lead_source, restaurant_count_tier), pricing_cta_clicked (params: plan_name). All lead data schema defined by HubSpot contact and form properties.
target_output.api_design: Single internal endpoint: POST /api/demo-request. Request body: {name, email, restaurant_count, food_cost_pct, message, captcha_token}. Server actions: 1) POST to hCaptcha siteverify endpoint with captcha_token and HCAPTCHA_SECRET env var. 2) If verified, POST to HubSpot Forms API https://api.hsforms.com/submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formId} with mapped contact fields. Returns: 200 {success: true} or 400/500 with error message. Rate limited by Vercel to 20 requests/hour per IP.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel — HTTP requests automatically redirected. hCaptcha invisible v2 on demo form prevents automated lead spam. GDPR: consent banner on first visit gates GA4 gtag() initialization; consent stored in localStorage under gdpr_consent_granted key; no server-side cookies set by landing page infrastructure. CAN-SPAM: unsubscribe link included in all HubSpot follow-up email sequences (managed in HubSpot, not in landing page code). Content Security Policy header via next.config.js restricting script-src to Vercel, hCaptcha, and Google Analytics origins. No PII stored on Vercel infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub repository with main branch triggering Vercel production deploy on merge. Pull requests generate Vercel preview deployments with unique URLs for stakeholder review. GitHub Actions: eslint --max-warnings 0, tsc --noEmit, next build — all must pass before PR merge. Vercel project config: output=export, NEXT_PUBLIC_HCAPTCHA_SITEKEY and HCAPTCHA_SECRET set in Vercel environment variables (encrypted). HubSpot portal ID and form ID set as Vercel env vars. No staging environment needed — preview deployments serve this purpose. Rollback via Vercel dashboard instant promote of prior deployment.
target_output.observability: GA4 dashboard: demo_request_submitted conversion rate, calculator_result_viewed to demo_request_submitted funnel, traffic source breakdown. Vercel Analytics: LCP, CLS, FID per route — alert configured for LCP regression above 2.5s. Vercel Function logs: hCaptcha verification failures and HubSpot API errors surfaced in Vercel Log Drains. No APM or alerting infrastructure required given zero-persistence architecture. Monthly review of HubSpot contact source data to validate lead quality from calculator vs. direct form paths.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Near-zero infrastructure cost: Vercel Hobby or Pro plan (~$20/month) covers static hosting and Vercel Functions for expected lead volume (<500 demo requests/month). GA4 free tier. HubSpot Marketing Hub Starter ~$18/month. hCaptcha free tier. Total <$40/month. Scales to millions of static page views without cost increase — CDN delivery is flat-rate. Vercel Function invocations at demo form volume are well within free tier limits.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Hero section, food cost savings calculator widget, demo request form with HubSpot integration and hCaptcha, GA4 tracking with calculator and form events, GDPR consent banner, Lighthouse >95 validation, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 Content Expansion (4 weeks): ROI statistics section, 3 operator testimonials, pricing tier comparison cards, savings case study data visualizations. Phase 3 Optimization (ongoing): A/B test hero headline and CTA copy via Vercel Edge Config feature flags, localized content for UK and Australian markets, SEO-optimized blog section with restaurant cost management content.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode; no any types in calculator logic or form handlers. Tailwind only — no inline styles or custom CSS files. next/image required for all images — no raw img tags. GA4 events typed via a gtag.ts helper module with event name enum to prevent typos. hCaptcha token never logged or stored. All Vercel environment variables documented in .env.example with placeholder values committed to the repository. PR checklist: Lighthouse CI score verified in preview deployment before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HubSpot API outage causes form submission failures visible to users. Mitigation: Vercel Function returns success to user and queues failed submission to a dead-letter log in Vercel Log Drain for manual recovery — acceptable for low-volume lead capture. Risk: hCaptcha false positives blocking legitimate restaurant operators from submitting. Mitigation: hCaptcha invisible v2 minimizes false positives; fallback challenge appears only when risk score is high. Tradeoff: Next.js static export over fully server-rendered for CDN performance — means no server-side personalization, acceptable for a single-purpose landing page. Risk: GA4 consent blocking reduces analytics data for EU visitors. Mitigation: Vercel Analytics (consent-free, privacy-preserving) captures Core Web Vitals regardless of GA4 consent state.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: HubSpot Forms API selected over Zapier webhook for direct CRM integration without middleware dependency. hCaptcha selected over reCAPTCHA v3 to avoid Google dependency on a GDPR-sensitive page. Static export confirmed over ISR — no dynamic data requires server-side rendering. Savings calculator factor set at 18% reduction constant, adjustable via a Next.js public env var without code change. System font stack confirmed to eliminate render-blocking Google Fonts request.

metadata.notes: Landing page for restaurant AI SaaS with calculator-driven lead capture and GDPR consent management.

project_goal.value: CLI tool for franchise IT admins to audit and harden in-store network device security — POS terminals, KDS units, Wi-Fi access points — scanning for default credentials, outdated firmware, open management ports, and optionally applying hardening fixes via SSH
project_goal.rationale: Core security operations need for franchise IT compliance enforcement

target_users.value: Franchise IT administrators responsible for network security across restaurant locations; typically one IT admin per 10-20 locations running quarterly audits
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of the tool during franchise onboarding and routine security audits

project_class.rationale: Purely a command-line utility with no browser UI; runs locally on admin workstations or jump hosts

capabilities.rationale: CLI logic, local SQLite state, SSH and SNMP device integrations; no auth service or frontend required

complexity_level.rationale: Network scanning with multi-protocol device communication and report generation; manageable scope

risk_level.rationale: Tool stores device credentials and SSH private keys; misuse or compromise could expose POS network to attack

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores device credentials, SSH keys, network topology, and security finding details — highly sensitive operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on local admin workstation or jump host; no network exposure; no cloud backend

access_model.value: Single-user CLI; tool requires sudo/admin privileges on host; no multi-user auth; SQLite DB file permissions restrict access to admin user
access_model.rationale: Local tool with OS-level access control; multi-user auth not required for single-admin use case

feature_scope.value: Network subnet scanning for POS/KDS/AP device discovery, firmware version checks against CVE database, default credential testing, open management port scanning, remediation report generation (JSON/HTML), SSH-based hardening configuration application from policy templates
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope for v1 audit and remediation CLI

mvp_scope.value: Subnet scan with device discovery, firmware version check for Toast POS and Unifi AP devices, default credential check against bundled credential list, JSON and HTML report output
mvp_scope.rationale: Core audit functionality without SSH remediation; read-only scan is lower risk for initial release

security_baseline.value: SQLite DB encrypted via SQLCipher with passphrase derived from admin keychain; SSH private keys stored in OS keychain, never in SQLite; tool binary signed and notarized for distribution; no telemetry or external network calls during scan
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data on admin workstation requires at-rest encryption; air-gap compliance for restricted POS networks

frontend_stack.value: None — CLI only; terminal output via Cobra and lipgloss for colored output
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; CLI tool with styled terminal output

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Cobra CLI framework; custom SNMP and SSH integration packages
backend_stack.rationale: Go produces a single statically-linked binary; Cobra is the standard Go CLI framework

data_platform.value: SQLite 3.45 via go-sqlite3 (CGo) with SQLCipher extension for encryption at rest
data_platform.rationale: Local state storage with no server dependency; SQLCipher provides at-rest encryption for confidential device data

hosting_target.value: Distributed as signed macOS/Linux binary via internal GitHub Releases; no cloud hosting
hosting_target.rationale: Self-contained binary for air-gapped franchise network environments

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit findings retained in local SQLite indefinitely; admins manually purge stale location data; no cloud sync or backup
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Data stays on admin workstation; no central retention policy required for local tool

future_scope.value: SSH hardening remediation commands from policy templates; integration with centralized vulnerability tracking system; multi-location batch audit mode
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features after read-only audit is validated

constraints.value: Must run offline with no external network calls during scan; SQLite DB must be encrypted; single binary distribution; no dependency on cloud services; must handle /16 subnet scans within 5 minutes
constraints.rationale: POS networks often air-gapped; no external dependencies allowed; performance SLA for usability

observability_baseline.value: Structured log file written to ~/.posaudit/logs/audit_{date}.log with JSON lines; verbose flag for debug output; no external metrics or APM
observability_baseline.rationale: Local tool; no cloud observability required; file-based logs sufficient for troubleshooting

execution_preference.value: Releases cut on GitHub Actions on tag push; semantic versioning; no continuous deployment
execution_preference.rationale: CLI tool with manual release cadence; no automated deployment pipeline needed

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS network security requirements for cardholder data environments: open management ports on POS devices in scope, default credentials a direct PCI-DSS requirement violation (Req 8.3.6), firmware CVE tracking required; internal franchise IT security policies mandate quarterly audits with signed-off reports
compliance_context.rationale: POS terminals are in CDE scope; tool directly enforces PCI-DSS Req 6.3 (firmware) and 8.3.6 (default creds)

project.goal: Audit and harden in-store POS network device security via CLI
project.target_users: Franchise IT administrators
project.timeline: Q2 MVP (read-only scan), Q3 SSH remediation

backend.framework: Cobra CLI with lipgloss for terminal output
backend.integrations: Custom SNMP v2c scanner, SSH client via golang.org/x/crypto/ssh, nmap-compatible TCP port scanner

security.auth: OS-level access control; SQLCipher for DB encryption
security.storage: SQLite with SQLCipher; SSH keys in OS keychain (macOS Security framework / Linux libsecret)
