devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: 2 tasks minimum (API + background task worker for price polling and EDI retries). ALB for API ingress. RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL. ElastiCache Redis. GitHub Releases for desktop installers (Windows .msi, macOS .dmg). Tauri CI builds cross-platform installers.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on push to main — cargo test + clippy, Docker build, push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. On tag v* — cargo build --release for Windows (cross) and macOS, package Tauri installers, upload to GitHub Releases. Desktop auto-update via Tauri updater plugin checking GitHub Releases endpoint.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on ECS Fargate. Custom metrics: edi_transmission_success_rate by supplier_id, price_list_poll_age_hours by supplier, po_submission_to_ack_duration_hours. Alert: EDI failure rate >20% per supplier in 1 hour.

ISS-001.title: Offline PO sync conflict resolution strategy not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan does not specify how conflicts are resolved when a PO draft is edited offline and the same PO is modified on the server before sync. Without a defined strategy, sync could silently overwrite server-side changes.

history.note: Identified during architect_reasoner review — offline sync conflict handling absent from plan

ISS-002.title: EDI mTLS certificate provisioning process not described
ISS-002.detail: Security section mentions mTLS for EDI endpoints but does not specify how per-supplier certificates are provisioned, stored, or rotated. Without this detail the partner onboarding process is incomplete.

history.note: Gap identified in security review — mTLS cert lifecycle missing

latest_recommendations: Define the offline sync conflict resolution strategy explicitly — last-write-wins with timestamp or vector clock — and document in architecture_overview and deployment_and_operations sections
Describe the per-supplier mTLS certificate provisioning lifecycle: how certificates are issued, how they are loaded into the API at runtime, and the rotation procedure for expiring certs

Per-supplier OAuth scopes provide correct authorization boundary for partner API
Tauri + Vue 3 offline-first design is appropriate for the coordinator desktop use case

previous_plan.title: Restaurant Supply Chain Desktop PO Management Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 desktop application for restaurant supply chain coordinators backed by a Rust/Axum REST API on ECS Fargate, enabling offline PO drafting, supplier price list management, and EDI order transmission to suppliers. Offline PO drafts stored in local SQLite sync to the API backend on reconnection.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 desktop client with Vue 3 frontend communicating with Rust/Axum API on ECS Fargate. PostgreSQL 16 for PO data. Redis for price list caching. Cognito PKCE auth for desktop. EDI transmission via background Tokio task.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito PKCE for desktop, per-supplier OAuth scopes, mTLS for EDI endpoints, TLS 1.3, financial data encrypted at rest.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate 2 tasks, RDS Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis. GitHub Actions CI/CD. Desktop installers via GitHub Releases with Tauri auto-updater. Offline PO drafts sync on reconnect.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two blocking issues from round 1: (1) defining the offline PO sync conflict resolution strategy — adopting last-write-wins with server-side updated_at comparison and coordinator confirmation UX for detected conflicts; (2) specifying the EDI mTLS certificate provisioning lifecycle using AWS ACM Private CA with per-supplier certificates loaded from Secrets Manager into Axum at startup and rotation managed via a runbook-triggered process. Both resolutions are integrated into the architecture_overview, security_and_compliance, and deployment_and_operations sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined last-write-wins conflict resolution strategy for offline PO sync: desktop Tauri app stores draft updated_at timestamp; on sync, API compares client updated_at against server updated_at; if server is newer, API returns 409 Conflict with server version; coordinator must explicitly choose client or server version via conflict resolution dialog before sync completes. Documented in architecture_overview and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Coordinators are never silently overwritten; all conflicts are surfaced for explicit resolution before commit

fix_report.action_taken: Documented per-supplier EDI mTLS certificate provisioning lifecycle: certificates issued via AWS ACM Private CA with per-supplier CN (e.g., CN=SUPP-001.edi.supplychain.internal); stored in AWS Secrets Manager as PEM bundle per supplier_id; Axum API loads all active supplier cert bundles from Secrets Manager at startup and caches in memory; rotation triggered manually via runbook when cert is within 30 days of expiry; Datadog monitor alerts when any supplier cert expires within 30 days. Added to security_and_compliance section.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partner onboarding process has clear certificate provisioning steps; cert expiry is monitored and alerted

target_output.title: Restaurant Supply Chain Desktop PO Management Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 desktop application for restaurant supply chain coordinators backed by a Rust/Axum REST API on AWS ECS Fargate, enabling offline PO drafting with conflict-safe sync, real-time supplier price list management, and EDI 850 order transmission to approved suppliers. Coordinators create purchase orders against contracted pricing, submit for EDI transmission, and receive acknowledgment and advance ship notice updates through the desktop interface. Supplier system integrations consume the same API via OAuth 2.0 client credentials. This round resolves offline sync conflict handling and EDI mTLS certificate lifecycle.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four-layer architecture: Tauri 2 + Vue 3 desktop frontend, Rust/Axum REST API on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16 for persistent PO and supplier data, and Redis for price list caching. Desktop offline-first: PO drafts stored in local SQLite via Tauri plugin:store with updated_at timestamps on every mutation. On reconnect, the sync engine calls POST /api/v1/sync/purchase-orders with client drafts and their updated_at values. The API applies last-write-wins: if client updated_at > server updated_at, the server accepts the update; if server updated_at >= client, the API returns 409 Conflict with the server version in the response body. The Vue 3 conflict resolution dialog presents both versions side-by-side and requires coordinator selection before sync completes — no silent overwrites. EDI transmission runs as a background Tokio task consuming from an edi_transmissions queue in PostgreSQL; per-supplier mTLS certificates loaded from AWS Secrets Manager at API startup.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Tauri 2, Vue 3, TypeScript 5.4, Pinia state management, Tauri plugin:store for offline SQLite, Tauri plugin:shell for OS keychain. API: Rust 1.78, Axum 0.7, tower middleware (auth, rate-limit, tracing), sqlx async PostgreSQL driver, redis-rs async client, tokio for background tasks. EDI: custom x12 crate for X12 4010 850/855/856 parsing and generation. Auth: AWS Cognito (PKCE for desktop, client credentials for supplier API). Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache. Hosting: ECS Fargate, ALB, AWS Secrets Manager, ACM Private CA. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: PO Creation: coordinator selects supplier and line items from price-list-backed product catalog; contracted price vs. current price variance shown inline; draft saved to local SQLite with updated_at. Offline Sync: on reconnect, sync engine reconciles local drafts with server via last-write-wins with coordinator-confirmed conflict resolution. EDI Transmission: coordinator submits PO triggering EDI 850 generation and background transmission; real-time WebSocket status updates in EDI Monitor view. Inbound EDI: 855 acknowledgments and 856 ASNs parsed by API and surfaced as PO status updates in desktop. Supplier Price List: configurable polling per supplier (1-24h TTL); stale price list indicator on PO creation form. Partner API: supplier systems authenticate with per-supplier OAuth client credentials and call GET /purchase-orders?supplier_id={their_id} for order visibility.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2 desktop app: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia stores, Tauri plugin:store (offline SQLite), OS keychain JWT storage via plugin:shell, sync engine (conflict detection logic). Rust/Axum API on ECS Fargate: purchase-orders module, suppliers module, edi module (X12 generation/parsing), sync module (conflict resolution endpoint), background Tokio scheduler for price list polling and EDI retries. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: primary data store. Redis 7 ElastiCache: supplier price list cache (configurable TTL per supplier). AWS ACM Private CA: per-supplier EDI mTLS certificates. AWS Secrets Manager: DB credentials, Redis URL, per-supplier mTLS PEM bundles. ALB: API ingress with WAF. Datadog APM agent on ECS tasks.
target_output.workflows: PO Offline Draft and Sync Flow: Coordinator drafts PO offline — items added, quantities entered, updated_at recorded. On reconnect, sync engine iterates local drafts and calls POST /api/v1/sync/purchase-orders with [{draft_id, updated_at, ...}]. API checks each draft against server: if client updated_at > server updated_at, server accepts update; if conflict, API returns 409 with server record. Vue 3 conflict dialog presents coordinator with both versions; coordinator selects authoritative version. Selected version committed via PATCH /api/v1/purchase-orders/:id/resolve-conflict. EDI Transmission Flow: Coordinator clicks 'Submit PO' on approved draft. API generates X12 4010 850 transaction set from PO data, writes edi_transmissions record with status=pending, background Tokio task picks up pending transmissions and sends via mTLS HTTPS to supplier EDI endpoint. On response, status updated to acknowledged. Supplier's 855 acknowledgment parsed by EDI ingestion endpoint, PO status updated to acknowledged, desktop WebSocket event fires.
target_output.data_model: purchase_orders (id UUID PK, coordinator_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/acknowledged/shipped/received/cancelled, po_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, delivery_date DATE, offline_draft BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). order_line_items (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, product_catalog_id UUID FK, product_sku VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, quantity DECIMAL(10,3), unit_price_cents INT, contracted_price_cents INT, variance_pct FLOAT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ((unit_price_cents - contracted_price_cents)::FLOAT / contracted_price_cents * 100) STORED). supplier_profiles (id UUID PK, supplier_name VARCHAR, contact_email VARCHAR, edi_capable BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, edi_isa_id VARCHAR nullable, edi_gs_id VARCHAR nullable, price_poll_interval_hours INT DEFAULT 24, api_client_id VARCHAR nullable, mTLS_cert_expiry DATE nullable). price_lists (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, product_catalog_id UUID FK, product_sku VARCHAR, unit_price_cents INT, effective_from DATE, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ). edi_transmissions (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, transaction_type ENUM 850/855/856, direction ENUM outbound/inbound, status ENUM pending/sent/acknowledged/failed, raw_x12 TEXT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, ack_received_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0, error_detail TEXT nullable). delivery_receipts (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, received_by UUID FK, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, line_variances JSONB).
target_output.api_design: All endpoints at /api/v1 with Bearer JWT (Cognito). POs: POST /purchase-orders, GET /purchase-orders?status=&supplier_id= (paginated), GET /purchase-orders/:id, PATCH /purchase-orders/:id/submit, PATCH /purchase-orders/:id/resolve-conflict {resolution: 'client'|'server', client_draft: {...}}, POST /sync/purchase-orders [{draft_id, updated_at, ...}] -> [{status: 'accepted'|'conflict', server_record?}]. Price lists: GET /suppliers/:id/price-list (returns from Redis cache). EDI: POST /edi/transmit/:po_id, GET /edi/acknowledgments?po_id=, POST /edi/inbound (supplier EDI push, mTLS required). Partner supplier API: GET /partner/purchase-orders?supplier_id= (scope: supplier:{id}:orders required).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Cognito PKCE for desktop (system browser redirect, custom URI scheme tauri://auth callback). Per-supplier Cognito machine clients with scope supplier:{supplier_id}:orders; Axum middleware validates scope claim against requested supplier_id parameter. EDI mTLS: per-supplier X.509 certificates issued via AWS ACM Private CA with CN=SUPP-{id}.edi.supplychain.internal; stored as PEM bundles in Secrets Manager under edi/certs/{supplier_id}; Axum API loads all bundles from Secrets Manager at startup into in-memory TLS certificate store, refreshed on SIGHUP for rotation without restart. Certificate expiry monitored by Datadog monitor on supplier_profiles.mTLS_cert_expiry; alert fires 30 days before expiry. Financial data: purchase_orders and price_lists encrypted at rest via RDS KMS. Offline drafts in Tauri plugin:store encrypted via OS-level encryption on macOS (Data Protection) and Windows (DPAPI). Compliance: SOC 2 Type II — all PO mutations logged to audit_log table with user_id, action, changed_fields JSONB, timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: 2 tasks minimum (1 API, 1 background worker for price polling and EDI retries), auto-scaling on CPU 60%. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ in us-east-1. ElastiCache Redis 7 single-node (cache.t3.small). ALB with WAF rules. GitHub Actions: on push to main — cargo test --all, cargo clippy, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check on /health. On tag v* — cross-compile Rust API for linux/amd64, build Tauri installers for Windows and macOS via GitHub Actions matrix, upload to GitHub Releases. Desktop auto-update: Tauri updater plugin checks https://releases.api.supplychain.internal/latest.json on startup. Secrets Manager rotation runbook: when Datadog alert fires for cert expiry, IT admin generates new ACM PCA certificate, updates Secrets Manager PEM bundle, sends SIGHUP to ECS task to reload certificate store without downtime.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces on all Axum request handlers. Custom metrics: supplychain.po_sync_conflicts_total (counter — tracks offline conflict frequency by coordinator), supplychain.edi_transmission_success_rate (gauge by supplier_id), supplychain.edi_transmission_retry_count (histogram), supplychain.price_list_age_hours (gauge by supplier_id — alerts when >supplier_poll_interval + 2h). Alerts: EDI failure rate >30% per supplier over 1 hour (P1 PagerDuty), price list age >26h for daily-poll supplier (P3 Slack), mTLS cert expiry within 30 days (P2 PagerDuty).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: ~$120/month for 2 tasks. RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$130/month. ElastiCache cache.t3.small: ~$25/month. Secrets Manager: ~$5/month. Cognito: free tier covers coordinator user count. Total: ~$280/month for a 50-coordinator deployment. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling handles coordinator burst; EDI background worker scales independently via separate ECS service; RDS read replica added when analytics queries compete with OLTP.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): PO CRUD with offline draft in Tauri SQLite, last-write-wins sync with conflict resolution dialog, supplier profile management, manual price list import, Cognito PKCE auth, Rust/Axum API on ECS Fargate, GitHub Actions CI/CD with Windows and macOS Tauri installer builds. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Real-time supplier price list polling with Redis cache, configurable TTL per supplier, EDI 850 generation and X12 4010 transmission to pilot suppliers, WebSocket EDI status feed in desktop. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Inbound EDI 855/856 parsing, delivery receipt confirmation flow, partner supplier API with OAuth client credentials, SOC 2 audit log.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy with deny(warnings), rustfmt enforced in CI. Axum handlers are thin — business logic in domain service structs. EDI X12 parser covered by property-based tests (proptest) against X12 4010 transaction set schemas. Tauri frontend: Vue 3 Composition API with TypeScript strict mode. Pinia stores covered by Vitest unit tests. Sync engine conflict detection logic tested with table-driven tests covering all timestamp ordering cases. ADR document per major architecture decision: offline sync strategy (ADR-001), EDI mTLS provisioning (ADR-002). Desktop installer release notes auto-generated from git changelog.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Tauri plugin:store SQLite local storage can become corrupted if the desktop app crashes during a write. Mitigation: use WAL mode for SQLite; implement periodic backup of local draft store to API. Risk: X12 4010 EDI format is supplier-specific in practice — ISA/GS segment variations require per-supplier configuration. Mitigation: supplier_profiles table stores per-supplier ISA and GS IDs; EDI generation module applies them per transmission. Tradeoff: last-write-wins sync with coordinator confirmation over CRDTs — significantly simpler implementation at the cost of requiring human intervention on conflicts; acceptable given low expected conflict rate for discrete PO objects. Tradeoff: ACM Private CA over public CA for EDI mTLS — eliminates supplier certificate trust issues at the cost of operating a private CA; justified by closed partner network.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Offline sync conflict strategy resolved as last-write-wins with coordinator confirmation dialog (ADR-001) — rejected CRDT approach as too complex for structured PO documents. EDI mTLS certificate provisioning resolved as ACM Private CA with Secrets Manager storage and SIGHUP-triggered reload (ADR-002). Confirmed X12 4010 as EDI format for backward compatibility with legacy supplier systems.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of desktop supply chain PO management app; addresses offline sync conflict resolution and EDI mTLS certificate provisioning gaps from round 1.

project_goal.value: Fullstack taproom operations platform for a regional brewery group managing keg inventory, draft line assignments, tasting room reservations, merchandise POS, and per-location configuration via a modernized CI/CD pipeline replacing manual FTP deployments
project_goal.rationale: Consolidates keg tracking, reservations, and POS into one platform with deployable per-location config

target_users.value: Taproom staff, taproom managers, and brewery group operations directors
target_users.rationale: Covers POS usage, line management, reservation approvals, and cross-location reporting

project_class.rationale: Requires SvelteKit frontend, Go Gin API backend, and data persistence

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel, payments
capabilities.rationale: Full set including Stripe payments for merchandise POS and Keycloak auth

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-location keg inventory, real-time line management, POS integration, per-location config

risk_level.rationale: Financial data from POS transactions; no health or identity risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: POS transaction records and merchandise revenue data

external_exposure.rationale: Staff-only access; reservation booking is authenticated; no public API

access_model.value: Keycloak RBAC: taproom_staff (POS and checklist), taproom_manager (line management, reservation approval, keg inventory), ops_director (cross-location analytics, config management)
access_model.rationale: Separation of operational and oversight capabilities

feature_scope.value: Keg inventory tracking, draft line assignment board, keg kick/tap logging, tasting room reservation workflow, merchandise POS with Stripe, per-location beer release management, cross-location analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational scope for taproom management

mvp_scope.value: Keg inventory and line assignment, tasting room reservation booking and approval, basic merchandise POS with Stripe, location-level admin config
mvp_scope.rationale: Core operational workflows for pilot at 3 locations

security_baseline.value: Keycloak with OIDC, TLS 1.3, Stripe Elements for PCI-scoped payment capture, RBAC enforcement in Go middleware, no card data stored
security_baseline.rationale: PCI scope minimized by Stripe Elements; RBAC critical for financial data access

frontend_stack.value: SvelteKit with TypeScript on bare-metal Kubernetes
frontend_stack.rationale: SvelteKit performance characteristics suit a tablet-based taproom POS interface

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin on bare-metal Kubernetes
backend_stack.rationale: Go performance for concurrent POS transaction processing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for session state and real-time line board updates
data_platform.rationale: Relational data for keg/line relationships; Redis pub/sub for real-time board updates

hosting_target.value: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes with GitLab CI
hosting_target.rationale: Cost-effective self-managed Kubernetes for multi-location deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: POS transactions retained 7 years for TTB brewing record requirements; guest reservation data retained 12 months then purged; staff data retained for employment duration plus 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: TTB record-keeping requirements for alcohol sales

future_scope.value: Loyalty program integration, digital menu board sync from keg inventory, mobile app for reservation management
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 guest-facing features

constraints.value: Must run on Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes; Stripe for all payment processing; no AWS/GCP/Azure
constraints.rationale: Infrastructure cost constraints and existing Hetzner contract

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for infrastructure and application metrics, Grafana dashboards for per-location keg volume and POS transaction rate, Loki for structured log aggregation
observability_baseline.rationale: Self-hosted observability stack consistent with Hetzner bare-metal constraint

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI/CD with per-location Kubernetes namespaces for configuration isolation
execution_preference.rationale: Per-location namespace isolation enables independent config and rollout

compliance_context.value: TTB Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau record-keeping for alcohol sales; alcohol beverage compliance for age verification at reservation booking; PCI-DSS SAQ-A scope via Stripe Elements for merchandise POS
compliance_context.rationale: Alcohol sales regulation and PCI scope for payment processing

project.goal: Taproom operations platform with keg inventory, reservations, POS, and per-location config management
project.target_users: Taproom staff, managers, ops directors

frontend.state: Svelte stores for reactive keg board, TanStack Query for server state

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC with RBAC

data.cache: Redis 7 for session state and pub/sub

devops.environments: dev, staging, production (per-location namespaces)

reasoner_reviews.product: The keg line board is the highest-frequency touchpoint for taproom staff and must feel instantaneous. Real-time updates when a keg is tapped or kicked need to propagate to all connected clients immediately — Redis pub/sub to SvelteKit SSE is the right approach. Reservation approval workflow needs manager notification (email or in-app) when a new booking arrives. TTB record-keeping means POS transactions must be immutable once recorded.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go Gin with GORM suits this workload. The keg inventory system should model keg_tap_lines as a join between keg_inventory and tap_line_assignments with a current_keg_id FK — line board state derivable from a single query. Stripe Elements for merchandise POS keeps all card data on Stripe's servers, achieving PCI SAQ-A scope. Redis pub/sub channel per location for real-time keg board events pushed to SvelteKit SSE endpoint.
reasoner_reviews.security: Keycloak RBAC must enforce location_id scoping at the API middleware level — a taproom_staff token must not access keg inventory for a different location even if they guess the location ID. POS transaction records should be append-only with no UPDATE or DELETE operations allowed — enforced via PostgreSQL row-level security or application-layer constraint. Stripe webhook signatures must be verified before processing payment events.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes requires managing Kubernetes certificate management (cert-manager with Let's Encrypt), load balancing (MetalLB), and persistent storage (Longhorn for PostgreSQL PVCs) without cloud-managed equivalents. Per-location namespaces in Kubernetes provide config isolation via ConfigMaps keyed by location slug.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan did not specify the real-time keg board update mechanism in detail — SSE vs WebSocket choice was left open. Also, the Stripe webhook processing path was mentioned but the idempotency handling for duplicate webhook deliveries was not addressed.

frontend.app_structure: SvelteKit app with route groups: (auth) for login, (taproom) for staff POS and checklist views, (manager) for line board and reservation approval, (admin) for ops director cross-location views
frontend.pages_and_flows: Keg Line Board (live updating grid of tap lines with current beer and volume estimates), Keg Inventory list with tap/kick actions, Tasting Room Reservation calendar with approval modal, Merchandise POS with Stripe Elements card capture, Beer Release management, Cross-location analytics dashboard
frontend.state_management: Svelte stores for real-time keg board state (updated via SSE), TanStack Query for reservation and inventory data, Stripe.js loaded for POS payment flow
frontend.performance_notes: SvelteKit SSR for initial page load; client-side hydration for interactive POS and keg board; Stripe Elements iframe loaded only on POS route

backend.service_design: Go Gin application with route groups: /api/v1/keg, /api/v1/lines, /api/v1/reservations, /api/v1/pos, /api/v1/admin. Keycloak JWT middleware validates tokens and extracts location_id claim.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with GORM for PostgreSQL queries. SSE endpoint GET /api/v1/keg/line-events?location_id=X for real-time keg board updates. Stripe webhook POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe.
backend.failure_handling: Stripe webhook idempotency via stripe_event_id deduplication in pos_transactions table; duplicate webhook delivery returns 200 without reprocessing. POS transaction failures surfaced to staff with retry option — no silent failures.

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm per brewery group, OIDC JWT signed RS256, location_id custom claim enforced in Gin middleware for all /api/v1 routes
security.authorization_model: RBAC roles: taproom_staff (read keg lines, create POS transactions), taproom_manager (full keg and line CRUD, reservation approve/deny, run reports), ops_director (cross-location read, config writes)
security.secrets_management: PostgreSQL credentials and Stripe secret key in Kubernetes Secrets encrypted with Sealed Secrets; Keycloak client secrets in Sealed Secrets

data.entities: keg_inventory (id, brewery_location_id, beer_release_id, keg_size_liters, tapped_at, kicked_at, remaining_volume_pct, status ENUM in_storage/tapped/kicked), tap_line_assignments (id, location_id, line_number, current_keg_id FK, assigned_at, notes), tasting_reservations (id, location_id, party_size, requested_date, requested_time, guest_name, guest_email, status ENUM pending/confirmed/denied/cancelled, approved_by_staff_id, created_at), merchandise_items (id, location_id, name, sku, price, stock_count), pos_transactions (id, location_id, staff_id, stripe_payment_intent_id, stripe_event_id UNIQUE, items_json, total_amount, status ENUM pending/completed/refunded, created_at), brewery_locations (id, slug, name, timezone, keycloak_group_id), beer_releases (id, location_id, beer_name, style, abv, ibu, seasonal BOOL, active)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with Longhorn PVC on Kubernetes; Redis 7 for session cache and pub/sub; no object storage required for MVP

devops.deployment_topology: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes cluster: 3 control plane nodes + 3 worker nodes. MetalLB for load balancing, cert-manager with Let's Encrypt for TLS, Longhorn for distributed PVC storage, Nginx ingress controller. Per-location Kubernetes namespaces (e.g., taproom-portland, taproom-seattle) with location-specific ConfigMaps.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: Go unit tests and race condition detection, SvelteKit build and type-check, Docker image build, Helm chart lint and template validation, kubectl rollout to staging namespace, smoke tests, manual promotion to production namespace per location
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm release rollback via helm rollback {release} {revision}; PostgreSQL migrations use golang-migrate with down migrations available

best_plan.title: Brewery Taproom Operations Platform — Round 2 Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: SvelteKit and Go Gin fullstack app on Hetzner Kubernetes managing keg inventory, tap line assignments, tasting reservations, and Stripe-powered merchandise POS.
best_plan.architecture_overview: SvelteKit with Go Gin, PostgreSQL 16, Redis pub/sub, Keycloak OIDC, per-location Kubernetes namespaces.
best_plan.data_model: keg_inventory, tap_line_assignments, tasting_reservations, pos_transactions with stripe_event_id

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves the two remaining medium issues: SSE is the correct choice for keg board real-time updates (unidirectional, reconnectable, stateless, no WebSocket upgrade complications with Nginx ingress), and Stripe webhook idempotency is handled by a UNIQUE constraint on stripe_event_id in pos_transactions. Both fixes are concrete and implementable. The TTB 7-year POS transaction retention and Keycloak location-scoped RBAC remain the security pillars throughout.

fix_report.action_taken:
Committed to SSE via Go net/http chunked streaming for keg board real-time updates. Go handler subscribes to Redis pub/sub channel 'keg-events:{location_id}', writes events to SSE stream as 'data: {json}

'. SvelteKit client uses native EventSource API with auto-reconnect. Nginx ingress configured with proxy_buffering off and proxy_read_timeout 3600 for SSE connections. Documented in system_components, workflows, and development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Keg line board updates propagate to all connected taproom clients within 200ms of a keg tap or kick event without WebSocket upgrade complexity

fix_report.action_taken: Added UNIQUE constraint on pos_transactions.stripe_event_id. Stripe webhook handler in Go attempts INSERT of pos_transaction with stripe_event_id; on UNIQUE constraint violation (duplicate delivery), handler catches the PostgreSQL error code 23505, logs the duplicate event_id, and returns HTTP 200 to Stripe without reprocessing. Documented in api_design and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Duplicate Stripe webhook deliveries cannot double-count POS revenue; handler is idempotent at the database constraint level

target_output.title: Brewery Taproom Operations Platform — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A SvelteKit and Go Gin fullstack application deployed on Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes managing keg inventory, draft line assignments, tasting room reservations, and Stripe-powered merchandise POS for a regional multi-location brewery group. Real-time keg line board updates delivered via SSE from a Go handler subscribed to Redis pub/sub. Stripe webhook idempotency enforced via UNIQUE constraint on stripe_event_id in pos_transactions. Keycloak OIDC with location-scoped RBAC; TTB-compliant 7-year POS record retention.
target_output.architecture_overview: SvelteKit TypeScript frontend communicates via REST to a Go 1.22 Gin API backend, both deployed in per-location Kubernetes namespaces on Hetzner bare metal. Real-time keg board state is delivered via a dedicated SSE endpoint that subscribes to a Redis pub/sub channel keyed by location_id. POS payments use Stripe Elements (PCI SAQ-A scope) — card data never touches the Go backend. Keycloak OIDC issues JWTs with a location_id custom claim; the Gin middleware validates the claim on every API request, preventing cross-location data access. PostgreSQL 16 with Longhorn PVC provides relational persistence; TTB 7-year retention enforced via a PostgreSQL partial retention policy. GitLab CI builds and deploys independently to per-location Kubernetes namespaces using Helm charts with location-specific ConfigMap values.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: SvelteKit with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Svelte stores, native EventSource for SSE, Stripe.js for POS. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, GORM, golang-migrate, encoding/json for SSE formatting. Auth: Keycloak 24, OIDC, RS256 JWT. Payments: Stripe Elements, Stripe Go SDK, Stripe webhook signature verification. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with Longhorn PVC on Kubernetes. Cache/Pub-Sub: Redis 7 (session state, keg-events pub/sub channels). Infrastructure: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes, MetalLB, cert-manager (Let's Encrypt), Nginx ingress, Longhorn, Sealed Secrets. CI/CD: GitLab CI, Helm. Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, Loki.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Keg Inventory Management: log keg deliveries, tap a keg to a line, record kicks with remaining volume estimate, auto-generate restocking notification when keg_inventory.remaining_volume_pct falls below location_restock_threshold config. Draft Line Board: real-time visual grid of tap lines showing current beer name, style, ABV, and estimated pours remaining, updated via SSE on tap/kick events. Tasting Room Reservations: guest books via staff-entered request, manager receives in-app notification and approves or denies, confirmed reservations sent to guest email via SMTP relay. Merchandise POS: item selection with stock count display, Stripe Elements payment capture, receipt generation, pos_transactions recorded with stripe_event_id idempotency. Beer Release Management: manage active beer catalog per location for menu board and keg assignment. Cross-Location Analytics: ops director dashboard showing revenue by location, keg turnover rate, reservation acceptance rate.
target_output.system_components: SvelteKit frontend (per-location namespace), Go Gin API service (per-location namespace, 2 replicas), PostgreSQL 16 with Longhorn PVC (shared cluster-wide, per-location database schema), Redis 7 (shared, per-location pub/sub channels prefixed by location_id), Keycloak 24 (cluster-wide identity provider), Nginx ingress controller (TLS termination via cert-manager), MetalLB (bare-metal load balancer), Grafana + Prometheus + Loki (observability namespace), SSE endpoint /api/v1/keg/line-events?location_id=X (Go net/http chunked streaming, Redis SUBSCRIBE keg-events:{location_id}), Stripe webhook receiver /api/v1/webhooks/stripe (signature verification + idempotency via pos_transactions UNIQUE stripe_event_id).
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Keg Tap Event: Taproom manager clicks 'Tap Keg' on keg_inventory record, selects target tap line. Go handler updates keg_inventory.status=tapped, sets tap_line_assignments.current_keg_id, publishes JSON event to Redis channel keg-events:{location_id}. All connected SvelteKit clients with active EventSource on /api/v1/keg/line-events receive the SSE data event within 200ms, Svelte store updates the line board reactively without a page reload. Merchandise POS Transaction: Staff selects merchandise items, clicks Checkout. SvelteKit loads Stripe Elements iframe on POS route. On confirm, Stripe.js calls Stripe API directly to create PaymentIntent, returns payment_intent_id to client. Client POSTs to /api/v1/pos/transactions with items_json and payment_intent_id. Go handler confirms PaymentIntent via Stripe Go SDK, inserts pos_transaction with stripe_event_id. Stripe fires webhook to /api/v1/webhooks/stripe confirming payment.completed; Go handler verifies Stripe-Signature header, attempts INSERT — on duplicate stripe_event_id, PostgreSQL constraint violation 23505 triggers a 200 return without reprocessing.
target_output.data_model: keg_inventory (id UUID PK, brewery_location_id FK, beer_release_id FK, keg_size_liters DECIMAL, tapped_at TIMESTAMPTZ, kicked_at TIMESTAMPTZ, remaining_volume_pct DECIMAL, status ENUM in_storage/tapped/kicked). tap_line_assignments (id UUID PK, location_id FK, line_number SMALLINT, current_keg_id FK NULLABLE, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, notes TEXT). tasting_reservations (id UUID PK, location_id FK, party_size SMALLINT, requested_date DATE, requested_time TIME, guest_name, guest_email, status ENUM pending/confirmed/denied/cancelled, approved_by_staff_id FK NULLABLE, created_at). merchandise_items (id UUID PK, location_id FK, name, sku, price DECIMAL, stock_count INT). pos_transactions (id UUID PK, location_id FK, staff_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR, stripe_event_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, items_json JSONB, total_amount DECIMAL, status ENUM pending/completed/refunded, created_at — NOT NULL on stripe_event_id, UNIQUE INDEX). brewery_locations (id UUID PK, slug VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, timezone, location_restock_threshold DECIMAL, keycloak_group_id). beer_releases (id UUID PK, location_id FK, beer_name, style, abv DECIMAL, ibu INT, seasonal BOOL, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: All routes under /api/v1/, Keycloak JWT bearer required, location_id enforced by middleware. GET /api/v1/keg?location_id=X — list keg inventory with status. POST /api/v1/keg — create keg record (manager role). PATCH /api/v1/keg/{id}/tap {line_number} — tap keg to line, publishes SSE event. PATCH /api/v1/keg/{id}/kick {remaining_volume_pct} — record kick. GET /api/v1/keg/line-events?location_id=X — SSE stream, text/event-stream, chunked, proxy_buffering off. GET /api/v1/reservations?location_id=X&status=pending — reservation queue. POST /api/v1/reservations — create reservation. PATCH /api/v1/reservations/{id}/approve — manager approval. POST /api/v1/pos/transactions {items, stripe_payment_intent_id} — record POS transaction. POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe — Stripe webhook receiver with signature verification. GET /api/v1/analytics/revenue?location_ids=X,Y&period=30d — ops director cross-location view.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC RS256 JWT validation in Gin middleware; location_id custom claim extracted and compared to requested resource location_id — 403 on mismatch. POS transactions: Stripe Elements ensures no card data reaches Go backend (PCI SAQ-A). stripe_event_id UNIQUE constraint prevents double-processing of payment webhooks. PostgreSQL Row Level Security can supplement application-layer location scoping if multi-tenant schema is used. Sealed Secrets for PostgreSQL DSN, Stripe secret key, and Keycloak client secret in Kubernetes. TLS 1.3 via cert-manager Let's Encrypt on Nginx ingress. pos_transactions records are append-only — no UPDATE/DELETE on completed transactions enforced via PostgreSQL trigger raising exception on status=completed UPDATE. TTB compliance: pos_transactions retained 7 years via PostgreSQL data lifecycle policy; tasting_reservations purged after 12 months via scheduled Go maintenance job.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Hetzner bare-metal Kubernetes: 3 control plane nodes (CX21), 3 worker nodes (CX31). MetalLB assigns external IP to Nginx LoadBalancer service. cert-manager issues Let's Encrypt TLS certificates per hostname. Longhorn distributed storage for PostgreSQL PVCs with 2-replica replication. GitLab CI pipeline: Go unit tests with race detection (-race flag), SvelteKit type-check and build, Helm chart lint, Docker image build and push to GitLab Container Registry, Helm upgrade --install to staging namespace (taproom-staging), smoke tests via curl health checks, manual GitLab Environment promotion to per-location production namespaces (taproom-portland, taproom-seattle, taproom-spokane). Rollback: helm rollback {release} {revision}. golang-migrate runs at app startup for forward migrations; down migrations available for manual rollback.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Go /metrics endpoint (custom counters: keg_tap_events_total, pos_transactions_total, stripe_webhook_duplicates_total, reservation_approval_latency_seconds). Grafana dashboards: Keg Turnover Rate by location (kicks per week per line), POS Revenue by location (daily/weekly), SSE connection count, Stripe webhook error rate. Loki log aggregation: structured JSON logs from Go app with fields location_id, event_type, duration_ms. Alerting: Grafana alerts on Go API error rate >5% for 5 minutes, SSE handler connection drop spike (>50% connections lost in 60 seconds indicating Redis pub/sub failure), Stripe webhook error rate >10% in 15 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Hetzner CX21 x3 + CX31 x3 ~$120/month total compute. Longhorn storage 500GB ~$25/month. No cloud egress fees. Total infrastructure ~$150/month for 5-location deployment. Scales to 20 locations by adding CX31 worker nodes (~$10/node/month). PostgreSQL connection pooling via PgBouncer required beyond 10 locations to prevent connection exhaustion. Redis pub/sub scales to 20 locations on a single instance; beyond that, consider Redis Cluster with channel sharding by location_id.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Keycloak setup, keg inventory CRUD, tap line assignment board with SSE real-time updates, tasting room reservation workflow with email notification, basic merchandise POS with Stripe Elements, per-location Kubernetes namespace deployment at 3 locations, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Beer release catalog management, keg restocking notifications, cross-location analytics dashboard for ops director, POS transaction reporting with date range filters. Phase 3 (8 weeks): Guest loyalty program integration, digital menu board sync from active keg inventory, mobile app for manager reservation approval, SMTP relay for automated reservation confirmation emails.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: gofmt + golangci-lint in CI with -race flag on test runs. Gin route handlers must not contain business logic — delegate to service layer structs. Redis pub/sub event payloads must be versioned JSON structs — no raw string formatting. SvelteKit: ESLint + Prettier + TypeScript strict. EventSource SSE client must implement exponential backoff reconnection with max 5 retries before showing offline warning to staff. Stripe.js loaded only on POS route (/taproom/pos) via dynamic import to avoid loading payment SDK on non-payment pages. All Kubernetes Secrets created via Sealed Secrets — no plaintext Secret manifests committed to GitLab repository. Helm values files per location (values.portland.yaml, values.seattle.yaml) for location-specific ConfigMap overrides.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Nginx ingress SSE connection timeout terminates long-lived EventSource connections; mitigated by proxy_read_timeout 3600 and client-side auto-reconnect. Risk: Longhorn distributed storage adds replication overhead vs. cloud-managed PostgreSQL; mitigated by Longhorn's 2-replica configuration and automated backup to S3-compatible Hetzner Object Storage. Tradeoff: Hetzner bare-metal over cloud-managed Kubernetes — lower cost but higher operational burden for certificate management, load balancing, and storage. Risk: Keycloak as a self-managed identity provider requires high availability configuration — Keycloak must run with 2 replicas and shared PostgreSQL database to survive node failures. Tradeoff: per-location Kubernetes namespaces over a multi-tenant single-namespace deployment — config isolation and independent rollout justify added Helm chart complexity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SSE confirmed over WebSocket for keg board — unidirectional, no upgrade header complications with Nginx, native EventSource API in SvelteKit. stripe_event_id UNIQUE constraint confirmed as idempotency mechanism — simpler than a separate event deduplication table. golang-migrate at app startup confirmed over dedicated migration job — acceptable for expected migration frequency and Kubernetes rolling deployment restart. Longhorn confirmed for PostgreSQL PVC storage — 2-replica replication sufficient for 5-location deployment. MetalLB confirmed for bare-metal LoadBalancer IP assignment.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolution of SSE mechanism and Stripe webhook idempotency for brewery taproom operations platform on Hetzner Kubernetes.

project_goal.value: Fullstack web application for hotel F&B directors to manage banquet event orders (BEOs), track catering labor costs, and compare actual-versus-budgeted food costs across multi-day conferences, weddings, and corporate dinners with per-event costing worksheets
project_goal.rationale: Hotel F&B departments lack specialized software for BEO costing; current process is spreadsheet-based with no real-time labor or food cost visibility

target_users.value: Hotel F&B directors, banquet managers, and executive chefs at full-service hotels; 5-20 internal users per property
target_users.rationale: Primary operators creating events, managing menus, and reviewing cost performance

project_class.rationale: Requires full Next.js frontend with complex costing worksheets and a FastAPI backend for financial calculation logic

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Complex UI for event management, backend for cost calculations, financial data persistence, Auth0 authentication, cost analytics, and property admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: Complex per-event costing logic, multi-day conference handling, actual-vs-budget reconciliation, and labor burden calculation

risk_level.rationale: Financial data for hotel operations but no cardholder data; internal multi-tenant SaaS

data_sensitivity.rationale: Event costing, labor rates, vendor invoices, and food cost percentages are confidential financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Auth0 authentication required; internal hotel staff only; no public access

access_model.value: Auth0 multi-tenant with property_id claim; RBAC roles: f_and_b_director, banquet_manager, executive_chef, property_admin; property-scoped data isolation via row-level security
access_model.rationale: Multi-property SaaS requires tenant isolation; roles map to feature access (directors see cost analytics, chefs see menu management)

feature_scope.value: BEO creation and management with event space selection and guest count, configurable catering menu catalog with costed items, labor assignment with wage rate calculation, food cost percentage auto-calculation, post-event reconciliation with invoice import, actual-vs-budget variance reports, admin panel for menu and wage rate management
feature_scope.rationale: Full BEO lifecycle from initial creation through post-event financial reconciliation

mvp_scope.value: BEO creation with event space, date/time, guest count; static menu catalog selection; food cost percentage calculation; basic labor assignment; PDF BEO export; Auth0 login
mvp_scope.rationale: Core event creation and basic costing without post-event reconciliation for initial pilot

security_baseline.value: Auth0 MFA optional for directors; JWT validation on all API endpoints; PostgreSQL row-level security for property isolation; TLS 1.3; no financial data in client-side state beyond current session
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires transport security and tenant isolation; MFA for high-privilege roles

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and App Router
frontend_stack.rationale: App Router enables server components for initial BEO list renders; TypeScript for financial calculation type safety

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; SQLAlchemy ORM with Alembic migrations
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for high-performance cost calculation endpoints; SQLAlchemy for ORM with PostgreSQL RLS support

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies; Redis 7 for session cache and worksheet lock state
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces property isolation at DB layer; Redis for collaborative worksheet editing prevention

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot in us-central1; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private IP; Memorystore Redis
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot reduces cluster management burden; Cloud SQL for managed PostgreSQL with HA

privacy_retention_policy.value: Event and financial data retained 7 years per hotel accounting standards; deleted properties purged after 90-day grace period; no PII beyond staff names and emails
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Hotel financial records subject to standard accounting retention requirements

future_scope.value: Guest dietary tracking per event, vendor contract management, multi-property consolidated reporting, mobile app for day-of banquet execution
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP features once core BEO workflow is validated

constraints.value: Single GCP project deployment; budget under $2,000/month GCP spend; Next.js frontend and FastAPI backend must be separately deployable; no Excel file parsing dependency in MVP
constraints.rationale: Cost control and deployment flexibility constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on FastAPI backend; structured JSON logging; Datadog dashboards for API latency and error rate; GKE Autopilot metrics via Cloud Monitoring
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate Datadog standard for application monitoring

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI with 2-week sprints; feature branch workflow with merge request approvals
execution_preference.rationale: Team uses GitLab; sprint cadence aligns with hotel pilot schedule

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II controls required for SaaS deployment to hotel management companies: access logging, data encryption at rest via Cloud KMS, change management process, incident response plan; internal hotel financial controls for cost reporting integrity
compliance_context.rationale: Hotel management company procurement requires SOC 2 Type II certification for SaaS vendors

project.goal: BEO management with food cost and labor tracking for hotel F&B operations
project.target_users: F&B directors, banquet managers, executive chefs
project.timeline: Q3 MVP pilot at 2 hotel properties

frontend.state: React Query (TanStack Query) for server state; Zustand for worksheet lock UI

security.auth: Auth0 with property_id custom claim, RBAC roles

data.cache: Memorystore Redis 7 for session and lock state

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: The BEO creation flow is well-scoped for MVP. The most important UX challenge is the costing worksheet — F&B directors need to see food cost percentage updating live as they add menu items. This requires either a real-time API call pattern or client-side cost calculation with server-side validation on save. Given the FastAPI backend, recommend client-side calculation with server-side audit on POST /events/{id}/cost-worksheet to avoid excessive round-trips during item selection.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The plan correctly uses PostgreSQL RLS for property isolation, but the Round 1 plan did not specify the RLS policy implementation — specifically whether the app uses SET LOCAL app.current_property_id before each query (application-enforced) or a dedicated per-property DB user (DB-enforced). The distinction matters for SOC 2 audit evidence. Recommend application-level SET LOCAL with SQLAlchemy event listener setting property context on connection checkout.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 multi-tenant setup requires validating the property_id claim on every API request, not just at login. The Round 1 plan assumed property_id was validated only during Auth0 login, which allows a token with a tampered property_id claim to access cross-tenant data if the JWT is not re-validated server-side. FastAPI dependency should extract and validate property_id from JWT payload on every request.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GCP budget $2,000/month is achievable. GKE Autopilot with e2-standard-2 nodes ~$400/month, Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 ~$250/month, Memorystore Redis basic ~$100/month, Cloud Storage and CDN ~$50/month. Remaining $1,200 budget headroom covers staging environment and load spikes. The plan should explicitly size the GKE workloads.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router: (app)/events (BEO list and creation), (app)/events/[id] (BEO detail with costing worksheet), (app)/menus (menu catalog admin), (app)/reports (cost analytics), (app)/admin (property admin). Server components for BEO list and report pages; client components for costing worksheet (live cost calculation) and labor assignment grid.
frontend.pages_and_flows: BEO List: server-rendered table of banquet_events filtered by property_id from JWT, sortable by event_date and status. BEO Create: multi-step form: event_space + date/time + guest_count -> menu selection with live food_cost_pct calculation -> labor assignment grid -> review + BEO PDF preview. BEO Detail/Edit: full worksheet view with locked indicator if another user editing (Redis lock). Cost Analytics: chart.js bar charts for actual-vs-budget variance by event, monthly food cost % trend.
frontend.state_management: React Query for BEO CRUD and menu catalog fetches with 5-minute stale time. Zustand store for worksheet editing state: {isLocked, lockedByUser, localCostWorksheet} — prevents API round-trips on each item add during worksheet editing. Cost percentage recalculated client-side on item change, server-validated on save.
frontend.ui_modules: CostWorksheetEditor: item selector with per-item cost and extension, live food_cost_pct badge (green <28%, yellow 28-32%, red >32%). LaborAssignmentGrid: role x hours x wage_rate matrix with auto-calculated labor burden. EventSpaceSelector: availability calendar checking event_spaces.max_capacity against guest_count. VarianceReport: table with drill-down from event-level to category-level cost variance.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: /events (BEO CRUD), /events/{id}/cost-worksheet (cost calc), /events/{id}/labor (labor assignment), /events/{id}/reconciliation (post-event), /menus (catalog), /reports (analytics), /admin (property config). Dependency injection: get_current_property_id() extracts and validates property_id from Auth0 JWT on every request; DB session dependency sets SET LOCAL app.current_property_id before yielding connection.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 auto-generated by FastAPI. POST /v1/events — create BEO. GET /v1/events?status=&start_date=&end_date= — list with filters. GET /v1/events/{id}/cost-worksheet — full worksheet with calculated food_cost_pct. POST /v1/events/{id}/cost-worksheet — save worksheet, return validated cost summary. POST /v1/events/{id}/labor — save labor assignments, compute labor_burden_total. POST /v1/events/{id}/reconciliation — import actual invoices, compute variance. GET /v1/reports/cost-variance?start=&end= — aggregate variance by event and category.
backend.business_modules: CostCalculationService: food_cost_pct = sum(menu_item.unit_cost * quantity) / event_total_revenue * 100; validates against property.target_food_cost_pct threshold. LaborBurdenCalculator: labor_total = sum(hours * wage_rate * (1 + burden_rate)); burden_rate from property configuration (typically 0.28-0.35 for payroll taxes and benefits). ReconciliationEngine: matches vendor_invoices to cost_worksheet line items by category, computes variance_pct per line, flags items where abs(variance_pct) > property.variance_alert_threshold.
backend.failure_handling: Optimistic locking on cost_worksheets via version column; 409 Conflict if stale version on save. Redis worksheet lock: SET event_lock:{event_id} with 15-minute TTL and NX flag; 423 Locked response with locked_by_user if lock taken. PDF generation queued as background task via FastAPI BackgroundTasks.

security.auth_design: Auth0 RS256 JWT validation on every request via fastapi-auth0 dependency; property_id and role claims extracted from JWT payload; no session state server-side beyond Redis token cache for logout. Auth0 Management API used to sync user roles on property_admin actions.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency chain: validate_jwt -> extract_property_id -> check_role_permission(required_roles). F&B director: full read/write on all event and report endpoints. Banquet manager: create/edit BEOs, no cost analytics. Executive chef: read BEOs, edit menu items. Property admin: user management and property config only.
security.privacy_controls: No PII in financial data beyond staff names (Auth0-managed) and email. PostgreSQL RLS: CREATE POLICY property_isolation ON banquet_events USING (property_id = current_setting('app.current_property_id')::uuid) — applied to all financial tables. Audit log table (access_audit) records every GET/POST on /events with user_id, property_id, event_id, action, timestamp for SOC 2 compliance.

data.entities: banquet_events (event_id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, title TEXT, event_type ENUM conference/wedding/corporate_dinner/social, event_space_id FK, event_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, guest_count INT, status ENUM draft/confirmed/in_progress/completed/cancelled, created_by UUID), event_orders (order_id UUID PK, event_id FK, menu_item_id FK, quantity INT, unit_cost_cents INT, extended_cost_cents INT), catering_menus (menu_id UUID PK, property_id FK, name TEXT, is_active BOOL), menu_items (item_id UUID PK, menu_id FK, name TEXT, category ENUM appetizer/entree/dessert/beverage/staffing, unit_cost_cents INT, sale_price_cents INT, is_active BOOL), labor_assignments (assignment_id UUID PK, event_id FK, role TEXT, hours DECIMAL, wage_rate_cents INT, labor_burden_rate DECIMAL, total_labor_cost_cents INT), cost_worksheets (worksheet_id UUID PK, event_id FK UNIQUE, food_cost_pct DECIMAL, labor_cost_total_cents INT, revenue_total_cents INT, version INT, last_saved_at TIMESTAMP), vendor_invoices (invoice_id UUID PK, event_id FK, vendor_name TEXT, invoice_date DATE, amount_cents INT, category TEXT, status ENUM pending/matched/unmatched), budget_lines (line_id UUID PK, event_id FK, category TEXT, budgeted_amount_cents INT, actual_amount_cents INT, variance_cents INT), event_spaces (space_id UUID PK, property_id FK, name TEXT, max_capacity INT, base_rental_cents INT)
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies on all financial tables (banquet_events, event_orders, cost_worksheets, vendor_invoices, budget_lines) using SET LOCAL app.current_property_id per connection. SQLAlchemy event listener on connection checkout applies property context. Alembic manages schema migrations. Redis for worksheet locks (event_lock:{event_id}, 15-min TTL) and Auth0 token validation cache.
data.migration_notes: Alembic autogenerate with manual review before apply. All migrations backward-compatible for zero-downtime GKE rolling deploys. Sensitive financial columns (wage_rate_cents, unit_cost_cents) use INT (cents) to avoid floating point cost calculation errors.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1: Next.js deployment (2 replicas, 0.5 vCPU / 1 GB), FastAPI deployment (3 replicas, 1 vCPU / 2 GB), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-n1-standard-2 private IP, Memorystore Redis 7 basic 1 GB. Next.js served via Cloud Run (alternative: GKE Ingress with Cloud Armor WAF). Cloud Storage for BEO PDF artifacts.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: ruff lint + mypy typecheck (Python), ESLint + tsc (TypeScript), pytest 85% coverage gate, Playwright E2E for BEO creation flow, Docker build + Artifact Registry push, GKE rolling deploy via kubectl rollout. Staging: mirrors production topology in GKE, Cloud SQL separate instance. Alembic migrations run as Kubernetes Job before FastAPI rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: ddtrace on FastAPI with service:beo-api, resource names mapped to route paths. Custom metrics: beo.worksheet.save_duration_ms, beo.cost_calc.food_cost_pct distribution, beo.reconciliation.variance_count. Datadog dashboard: API p99 latency by endpoint, food cost % distribution across events, daily BEO creation rate.

ISS-001.title: property_id claim validated only at Auth0 login, not on every API request
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan assumed JWT property_id claim was validated during Auth0 login. A valid JWT with a tampered or stale property_id could access cross-tenant BEO data. FastAPI must re-validate property_id from JWT on every request.

history.note: Security review: property_id must be extracted from JWT on every request, not only at login

ISS-002.title: PostgreSQL RLS policy implementation not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan stated RLS would be used for property isolation but did not define the policy DDL or the mechanism for setting app.current_property_id per connection (SET LOCAL vs dedicated DB user). Required for SOC 2 audit evidence.

history.note: Architect review: RLS policy DDL and connection property context mechanism must be specified

focus_issues.title: property_id claim not validated on every API request
focus_issues.detail: Cross-tenant data access risk if JWT property_id not re-validated per request in FastAPI.

focus_issues.title: RLS policy implementation unspecified
focus_issues.detail: No DDL or connection context mechanism defined for PostgreSQL RLS property isolation.

latest_recommendations: Add FastAPI dependency get_current_property_id() that validates property_id from JWT RS256 payload on every request and passes to DB session context

Define RLS policy DDL: CREATE POLICY property_isolation ON banquet_events USING (property_id = current_setting('app.current_property_id')::uuid) and show SQLAlchemy event listener pattern for SET LOCAL before connection yield
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Hotel BEO Cost Management Platform v1

convergence_state.reason: One critical and one high issue unresolved from round 1; 6.8 score below passing threshold

previous_audits.summary: Well-structured fullstack BEO management design with appropriate GCP stack choices, but a critical cross-tenant data access vulnerability and unspecified RLS implementation undermine the security posture required for SOC 2 compliance.
strengths: PostgreSQL RLS mentioned as property isolation mechanism showing correct intent
Auth0 RBAC role mapping to F&B director/banquet manager/chef is appropriate
Cost calculation in cents avoids floating point errors
concerns: property_id validated only at login enables cross-tenant access with valid but reassigned JWTs
RLS policy DDL completely absent; SOC 2 auditor would reject this as unverified control
blocking_issues: Critical: property_id not re-validated from JWT on every FastAPI request
High: no RLS CREATE POLICY statement or SQLAlchemy connection context mechanism defined
recommendations: Define FastAPI dependency extracting and validating property_id from Auth0 JWT RS256 on every request
Provide RLS POLICY DDL and SET LOCAL connection context SQLAlchemy listener pattern

issue_updates.title: Cross-tenant JWT property_id not re-validated
issue_updates.detail: Plan described Auth0 login validation only; no per-request property_id extraction in FastAPI

issue_updates.title: RLS implementation absent
issue_updates.detail: RLS mentioned by name but no DDL, no connection context mechanism, no SQLAlchemy integration pattern

previous_plan.title: Hotel BEO Cost Management Platform v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Fullstack Next.js 14 + FastAPI application for hotel banquet event order management with food cost tracking and post-event reconciliation. Auth0 multi-tenant with property isolation. PostgreSQL RLS mentioned but not specified.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RS256 JWT, property_id validated at login. RLS for property isolation. SOC 2 Type II controls. TLS 1.3.
previous_plan.data_model: banquet_events, event_orders, catering_menus, menu_items, labor_assignments, cost_worksheets, vendor_invoices, budget_lines. Cents-based cost columns.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js App Router frontend, FastAPI backend on GKE Autopilot, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with RLS, Memorystore Redis for locks, Auth0 RBAC.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addresses a critical security issue (ISS-001: property_id not validated per request) and a high issue (ISS-002: RLS policy DDL unspecified). The critical fix requires defining a FastAPI dependency chain that extracts property_id from the RS256 JWT on every request and passes it to the database session via SET LOCAL. The RLS fix requires providing the actual CREATE POLICY DDL and the SQLAlchemy event listener pattern. This is a flawed quality row — the plan intentionally introduces a secondary inconsistency: the frontend stack section incorrectly references Remix instead of Next.js 14 despite the contract specifying Next.js 14, demonstrating a technology_stack mismatch that an auditor should flag. The critical and high issues are addressed, but the plan contains a contract violation.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined FastAPI dependency get_current_property_id() that decodes and validates Auth0 RS256 JWT on every request via python-jose, extracts property_id claim, raises 403 if absent or not matching any property the user is enrolled in. Injected into all event, menu, report, and reconciliation router dependencies. Updated security_and_compliance and api_design sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every API request independently validates the caller's property_id from the JWT; tampered or stale property_id claims are rejected before any DB query executes

fix_report.action_taken: Provided full RLS CREATE POLICY DDL for banquet_events, event_orders, cost_worksheets, vendor_invoices, and budget_lines tables. Defined SQLAlchemy event listener using @event.listens_for(engine, 'connect') to execute SET app.current_property_id = '{property_id}' via PRAGMA equivalent before connection checkout. Updated data_model and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce property isolation at DB layer independent of application logic; provides SOC 2 auditable control at database level

target_output.title: Hotel BEO Cost Management Platform v2
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack web application built with Remix and FastAPI on GKE Autopilot for hotel F&B directors to manage the full lifecycle of banquet event orders — from initial BEO creation with menu selection and live food cost percentage calculation through post-event reconciliation of actual invoices against budgeted line items. Auth0 multi-tenant with per-request JWT property_id validation and PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce complete data isolation between hotel properties. Designed for SOC 2 Type II compliance with comprehensive audit logging on all financial data access.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture on GCP: Remix SSR frontend on GKE Autopilot serving authenticated hotel staff, communicating via HTTPS to FastAPI REST backend also on GKE Autopilot, backed by Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security and Memorystore Redis. Auth0 RS256 JWT validated on every FastAPI request via get_current_property_id() dependency injecting property context into DB connections via SET LOCAL. PostgreSQL RLS policies on all financial tables enforce property isolation at the database layer. Datadog APM monitors food cost calculation latency and BEO creation throughput.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Remix with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui. Backend: Python 3.12 FastAPI with SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, python-jose for JWT validation. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with RLS. Cache: Memorystore Redis 7 for worksheet locks and Auth0 JWKS cache. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Cloud Armor WAF, Cloud SQL private IP, Cloud Storage for PDF artifacts. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: BEO Creation Wizard: event_space + date + guest_count selection, menu item picker with live food_cost_pct badge, labor assignment grid with wage rate auto-calculation, BEO PDF preview and confirm. Cost Worksheet Editor: client-side cost recalculation on item change (CostCalculationService client mirror), server-side validation on save with version conflict detection. Post-Event Reconciliation: vendor invoice import, line-item matching by category, variance report generation. Cost Analytics Dashboard: actual-vs-budget variance by event and category, monthly food cost % trend, labor burden distribution. Property Admin: menu catalog management, event space configuration, wage rate table, user role assignment.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot: Remix deployment (2 replicas, 0.5 vCPU / 1 GB), FastAPI deployment (3 replicas, 1 vCPU / 2 GB). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-n1-standard-2 private IP with RLS on all financial tables. Memorystore Redis 7 1 GB for worksheet lock keys (event_lock:{event_id} 15-min TTL NX) and JWKS key cache. Cloud Storage bucket for generated BEO PDF files. Auth0 tenant with property_id custom claim and RBAC extension. Datadog APM agent sidecar on FastAPI pods. Cloud Armor WAF on GKE Ingress.
target_output.workflows: BEO Creation and Cost Calculation: F&B director navigates to /events/new, selects event_space_id (availability checked via GET /v1/event-spaces?date=&guest_count=), enters guest_count and event dates. Proceeds to menu selection: React Query fetches GET /v1/menus/{id}/items, director adds items to worksheet; client-side CostCalculationService mirrors backend formula: food_cost_pct = sum(unit_cost_cents * quantity) / revenue_estimate_cents * 100; badge updates live without API call. On worksheet save, POST /v1/events/{id}/cost-worksheet validates server-side and returns authoritative food_cost_pct. Labor assignment: director fills LaborAssignmentGrid (role, hours, wage_rate_cents), LaborBurdenCalculator applies property burden_rate. BEO PDF generated as background task. Post-Event Reconciliation: banquet_manager uploads vendor invoices via POST /v1/events/{id}/invoices (multipart form, CSV or manual entry). ReconciliationEngine matches invoice line items to cost_worksheet categories by name similarity and category enum, computes variance_pct per line, flags lines where abs(variance_pct) > property.variance_alert_threshold (default 15%). Variance report available at GET /v1/reports/cost-variance/{event_id}.
target_output.data_model: banquet_events (event_id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK NOT NULL, title TEXT, event_type ENUM('conference','wedding','corporate_dinner','social'), event_space_id UUID FK, event_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, guest_count INT, status ENUM('draft','confirmed','in_progress','completed','cancelled'), created_by UUID, version INT DEFAULT 1). CREATE POLICY property_isolation ON banquet_events USING (property_id = current_setting('app.current_property_id')::uuid). ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on banquet_events. Same policy applied to: event_orders, cost_worksheets, vendor_invoices, budget_lines, labor_assignments. cost_worksheets (worksheet_id UUID PK, event_id UUID FK UNIQUE, food_cost_pct NUMERIC(5,2), labor_cost_total_cents BIGINT, revenue_total_cents BIGINT, version INT, last_saved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). vendor_invoices (invoice_id UUID PK, event_id FK, vendor_name TEXT, invoice_date DATE, amount_cents BIGINT, category TEXT, status ENUM('pending','matched','unmatched')). budget_lines (line_id UUID PK, event_id FK, category TEXT, budgeted_amount_cents BIGINT, actual_amount_cents BIGINT, variance_cents BIGINT). SQLAlchemy engine event listener: @event.listens_for(engine, 'before_cursor_execute') executes SET LOCAL app.current_property_id = :property_id before each DB operation within request context.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI routers under /v1 with Auth0 JWT dependency on all routes. POST /v1/events — create BEO, returns event_id. GET /v1/events?status=&start_date=&end_date= — list BEOs for current property. GET /v1/events/{id} — full BEO detail. POST /v1/events/{id}/cost-worksheet — save and validate worksheet, returns {food_cost_pct, labor_cost_total_cents, revenue_total_cents, version}. POST /v1/events/{id}/labor — save labor assignments. POST /v1/events/{id}/invoices — upload vendor invoices. GET /v1/events/{id}/reconciliation — variance report. GET /v1/reports/cost-variance?start=&end= — aggregate variance. GET /v1/menus/{id}/items — menu catalog items. POST /v1/admin/menus — create menu catalog. Auth dependency chain: validate_jwt() -> get_current_property_id() -> check_role(required_roles). 409 on worksheet version conflict. 423 if worksheet locked.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FastAPI dependency get_current_property_id(): decodes Auth0 RS256 JWT using JWKS URI (cached in Redis, 1-hour TTL), validates iss and aud claims, extracts property_id claim — raises HTTP 403 if absent. DB session dependency applies SET LOCAL app.current_property_id = {property_id} via SQLAlchemy @event.listens_for(engine, 'before_cursor_execute') ensuring RLS policy fires for every query in request context. PostgreSQL RLS CREATE POLICY on 6 financial tables (banquet_events, event_orders, cost_worksheets, vendor_invoices, budget_lines, labor_assignments). Auth0 RBAC: f_and_b_director has all permissions, banquet_manager has events:write but not reports:read, executive_chef has menus:write only, property_admin has admin:write. SOC 2 Type II: access_audit table logs all GET/POST on financial endpoints (user_id, property_id, resource, action, timestamp, ip_address); Cloud KMS CMEK on Cloud SQL for encryption at rest; change management via GitLab MR approvals; Datadog alert on error_rate > 1%.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff + mypy for FastAPI, ESLint + tsc for Remix (NOTE: plan incorrectly references Next.js build commands 'next build' — this is a contract violation as frontend_stack contract specifies Next.js 14 but plan executive_summary and architecture_overview reference Remix). GKE Autopilot deployments: Remix (2 replicas HPA min/max 2-6 on CPU 70%), FastAPI (3 replicas HPA min/max 3-8 on CPU 60%). Alembic migration as Kubernetes Job before FastAPI rollout. Cloud SQL private IP, no public access. Cloud Armor WAF rules: OWASP CRS level 2, rate limit 200 req/min per IP. Blue-green deploy strategy for zero-downtime. Secrets in GCP Secret Manager mounted as environment variables.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: ddtrace auto-instrumented on FastAPI; service:beo-api with resource names matching FastAPI route paths. Custom Datadog metrics: beo.worksheet.save_latency_ms (histogram), beo.food_cost_pct (distribution across events, alert if median > 35%), beo.reconciliation.unmatched_invoices_count (gauge). Grafana Cloud SQL dashboard via GCP Cloud Monitoring integration for DB connection pool saturation and replication lag. PagerDuty escalation: API error_rate > 2%, DB connection pool > 80%, worksheet lock contention > 5 simultaneous locks.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GCP estimate: GKE Autopilot e2-standard-2 equivalent for 5 pods ~$450/month, Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-2 ~$280/month, Memorystore Redis 1 GB ~$110/month, Cloud Storage ~$20/month, Cloud Armor ~$30/month, Cloud CDN + egress ~$50/month. Total ~$940/month for production, well within $2,000 budget. Staging environment ~$400/month. Scaling: GKE HPA handles event-day load spikes (pre-event PDF generation concurrent with BEO edits). Cloud SQL read replica for analytics queries if report load increases.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): BEO creation wizard with event space selector, static menu catalog with food cost % calculation, basic labor assignment, BEO PDF export via WeasyPrint background task, Auth0 login with property_id claim, PostgreSQL RLS policies, GitLab CI pipeline, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Post-event reconciliation with vendor invoice CSV import, variance report generation, cost analytics dashboard with chart.js, optimistic locking on worksheets, worksheet lock via Redis. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Multi-property consolidated reporting for hotel management companies, SOC 2 audit trail export, menu item cost history tracking, labor burden rate configuration per property.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff + mypy strict on FastAPI codebase; pytest with 85% coverage on CostCalculationService, LaborBurdenCalculator, ReconciliationEngine; parameterized tests for food_cost_pct boundary conditions. TypeScript: ESLint + tsc strict; no any types in cost calculation client mirror. Cents-only arithmetic for all financial values — no float cost fields anywhere. SQLAlchemy models use BIGINT for all cents columns. Alembic migrations reviewed for RLS policy preservation on table alterations. GitLab MR requires 1 senior approval + passing CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Auth0 custom claim propagation delay if property_admin changes user's property_id. Mitigation: force token refresh on role/property change via Auth0 Management API action; Redis JWKS cache TTL 1 hour limits stale token window. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS SET LOCAL context not applied in background tasks (PDF generation). Mitigation: background tasks receive property_id as explicit parameter and set connection context before any query. Tradeoff: client-side food_cost_pct calculation creates dual maintenance of formula — mitigated by shared validation constants module imported by both FastAPI and a TypeScript port. Risk: Cloud SQL connection exhaustion under peak concurrent BEO saves. Mitigation: pgBouncer sidecar on FastAPI pods for connection pooling with pool_size=10 per pod.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Auth0 property_id validated per request via FastAPI dependency, not only at login. RLS CREATE POLICY DDL specified for all 6 financial tables with SQLAlchemy connection context listener. Worksheet versioning uses optimistic locking with version column and 409 response on conflict. Food cost calculation uses cents arithmetic throughout — no float division except for final pct display. BEO PDF generated via WeasyPrint as FastAPI BackgroundTask writing to Cloud Storage.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision addressing critical auth and RLS issues; plan intentionally contains frontend stack inconsistency (Remix referenced in plan body vs Next.js 14 in contract) as a flawed training example.

profile.domain: Contract Food Service / AI Procurement Intelligence

project_goal.value: AI system for contract food service management companies to automatically analyze and benchmark supplier invoices against market price indices and historical spend data, flagging invoices where line-item prices deviate significantly from expected ranges and generating LLM-authored negotiation briefs per supplier to reduce procurement costs
project_goal.rationale: Core product: surface overpayment patterns at scale and equip procurement managers with specific negotiation ammunition
