target_users.value: Procurement managers and directors at contract food service companies managing multi-site supplier relationships
target_users.rationale: Primary decision-makers who conduct supplier negotiation calls and review invoice discrepancies

project_class.rationale: LLM-driven negotiation brief generation combined with ML-based invoice price deviation detection

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, batch_jobs, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full backend with LLM, batch invoice processing, market data integrations, analytics, and admin

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source market price integration, OCR invoice extraction, statistical deviation scoring, and LLM brief generation pipeline

risk_level.rationale: Financial procurement data with partner integrations; no payment card data processed

data_sensitivity.rationale: Supplier invoices, contracted prices, and spend data are financially sensitive business data

external_exposure.rationale: Web application and API require Azure AD authentication; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO with RBAC: procurement_analyst (read + flag review), procurement_manager (approve flags, trigger negotiations), procurement_admin (supplier config, market index management)
access_model.rationale: Role-based access controls procurement data visibility and action permissions

feature_scope.value: Invoice ingestion via email and EDI, OCR line item extraction, market price index integration, 90-day rolling deviation scoring, automated flag generation, LLM negotiation brief generation per supplier, procurement manager review dashboard, negotiation outcome tracking, supplier spend analytics
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end procurement intelligence workflow

mvp_scope.value: Email invoice ingestion with OCR extraction, market price deviation flagging, LLM negotiation brief generation, procurement manager review dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Core value chain from invoice to negotiation brief without EDI integration at launch

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA, TLS 1.3, financial data encrypted at rest with Azure Disk Encryption, audit log for all invoice access and flag actions, LLM outputs never written to external services
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data sensitivity and audit requirements for procurement controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; rich data table components needed for invoice line item review

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async API; Python for OCR, statistical analysis, and Azure OpenAI SDK integration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Container Apps; Redis 7 for LLM result caching and job queue
data_platform.rationale: Relational store for invoice and spend data; Redis for Celery task queue and brief caching

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for all services
hosting_target.rationale: Managed serverless containers on Azure for Azure AD integration and compliance boundary

privacy_retention_policy.value: Supplier invoices retained 7 years per financial record-keeping requirements; negotiation briefs retained 3 years; market price index data retained 5 years; audit logs retained 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention requirements for contract food service companies

future_scope.value: EDI invoice ingestion, predictive price forecasting model, automated RFP generation for high-deviation suppliers, mobile approval app
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expansion building on invoice intelligence foundation

constraints.value: Azure OpenAI endpoint must be used (not OpenAI direct) for data residency compliance; OCR must process invoices within 60 seconds; deviation scoring must run nightly on all un-flagged invoices from the past 30 days
constraints.rationale: Data residency for financial data, processing SLA, and freshness requirements

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for infrastructure, LLM call latency and token usage dashboards, invoice processing success rate tracking, Celery worker queue depth monitoring
observability_baseline.rationale: Critical operational metrics for a batch-heavy AI pipeline

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; OCR and LLM pipeline iterated in parallel with frontend development against stub data
execution_preference.rationale: Pipeline quality requires parallel iteration alongside UI

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II for SaaS deployment; internal financial controls and immutable audit trail for procurement decisions; Azure OpenAI data processing agreement for LLM use with financial data
compliance_context.rationale: SaaS procurement tool managing sensitive financial data requires SOC 2 scope and procurement audit controls

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o for negotiation brief generation; prompt includes supplier name, flagged invoice line items with deviation amounts, contracted prices, 90-day price trend summary, and market index context; output is structured JSON with sections: overpayment_summary, per_item_talking_points, recommended_target_price, market_context; briefs cached in Redis for 24 hours to avoid redundant LLM calls on re-opens
llm_integration.rationale: Azure OpenAI for data residency compliance; structured JSON output enables reliable downstream rendering

project.goal: AI invoice analysis and LLM negotiation brief generation for procurement managers
project.target_users: Procurement managers and analysts at contract food service companies

frontend.approach: Data-dense review dashboard with invoice line item tables and brief rendering

backend.framework: FastAPI with Celery for background processing

security.auth: Azure AD SSO with MFA and RBAC

reasoner_reviews.product: The workflow from invoice ingestion to negotiation brief is well-sequenced. The most impactful feature for procurement managers is the per-item talking point with specific price variance amounts — this is what they use on supplier calls. The brief should include the market benchmark source (e.g., 'based on USDA wholesale chicken breast index, 30-day average') so managers can cite the reference, not just the number.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The OCR pipeline using Azure Document Intelligence is the right choice over generic libraries — it handles multi-format invoices (PDF, scanned images) with higher accuracy on tabular financial data. Celery with Redis as the broker is appropriate for the nightly deviation scoring batch job. The LLM brief generation should be triggered asynchronously after flag creation, not synchronously in the API request path — this keeps the flagging API fast and prevents LLM latency from blocking manager workflows.
reasoner_reviews.security: Audit trail for procurement decisions is a SOC 2 requirement — every flag review action (acknowledged, disputed, escalated), brief generation event, and negotiation outcome update must be written to an immutable append-only audit_events table. Azure OpenAI calls must use the Azure OpenAI endpoint in the same Azure geography as the Container Apps deployment to satisfy data residency — never call OpenAI direct API. LLM prompts must not include raw invoice PDF text — only extracted structured data to minimize token usage and avoid inadvertent PII in prompts.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Nightly deviation scoring on 30-day rolling window requires an efficient PostgreSQL query pattern — a materialized view on invoice_line_items joined to market_price_index with a rolling 90-day average will prevent full table scans. Celery beat scheduler handles the nightly trigger without additional infrastructure.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: invoices (ingestion, OCR extraction orchestration, line item CRUD), market-prices (index management, rolling average computation), deviation-scoring (statistical scoring engine, flag generation), briefing (LLM orchestration, brief CRUD), analytics (spend reports, deviation trend queries). Celery worker handles async OCR jobs, deviation scoring batch, and LLM brief generation jobs.
backend.llm_integration_design: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via azure-openai Python SDK. Brief generation Celery task: loads flagged invoice supplier context from PostgreSQL, fetches 90-day price trend and market index data, constructs prompt with structured supplier data (never raw invoice PDF text), calls GPT-4o with response_format={type: 'json_object'} for structured output, validates JSON schema, stores in negotiation_briefs table, caches in Redis for 24h. Prompt template version-controlled as a separate YAML file with input slot definitions.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/invoices/upload (multipart, triggers OCR Celery job), GET /api/v1/invoices (paginated, filterable by supplier/status/flag), GET /api/v1/invoices/:id/line-items, GET /api/v1/flags (pending flags for review), PATCH /api/v1/flags/:id/review {action: acknowledged|disputed|escalated}, POST /api/v1/briefs/generate/:supplier_id (triggers async LLM job), GET /api/v1/briefs/:id, GET /api/v1/analytics/spend-deviation?supplier_id=&period=90d.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat schedule: ocr_extraction runs within 60s of invoice upload (priority queue), nightly_deviation_scoring at 02:00 UTC processes all uninvoiced line items in 30-day window, brief_refresh_job runs at 06:00 UTC regenerating stale briefs (>7 days old) for suppliers with new flag activity.

security.auth_design: Azure AD SSO via MSAL Python SDK. FastAPI middleware validates Azure AD JWT on every request. Role claims extracted from AAD groups: procurement_analyst, procurement_manager, procurement_admin.
security.authorization_model: procurement_analyst: GET invoices, GET flags, GET briefs. procurement_manager: all analyst permissions + PATCH flags/:id/review, POST briefs/generate, PATCH negotiation_outcomes. procurement_admin: all manager permissions + POST/PATCH suppliers, POST market_price_indices.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events append-only table: (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Written on: invoice upload, flag creation, flag review action, brief generation, negotiation outcome update. Application-level INSERT only — no UPDATE or DELETE on audit_events. Azure Monitor ingests structured logs for SOC 2 evidence collection.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for PostgreSQL connection string, Redis URL, Azure OpenAI API key, OCR API key. Container Apps environment references Key Vault secrets via managed identity — no secrets in environment variables or source code.

data.entities: supplier_invoices (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, invoice_number VARCHAR, invoice_date DATE, total_amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), source ENUM email/edi/manual, ocr_status ENUM pending/processing/complete/failed, raw_file_path VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). invoice_line_items (id UUID PK, invoice_id UUID FK, product_sku VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, quantity DECIMAL(10,3), unit_price_cents INT, line_total_cents BIGINT, extracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). market_price_indices (id UUID PK, product_category VARCHAR, source_name VARCHAR, unit VARCHAR, index_date DATE, price_cents INT, source_url VARCHAR). price_deviation_flags (id UUID PK, line_item_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, deviation_pct FLOAT, expected_price_cents INT, market_index_id UUID FK, scoring_run_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending_review/acknowledged/disputed/escalated/resolved, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). negotiation_briefs (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, brief_content JSONB, model_version VARCHAR, prompt_version VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cache_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.analytics_events: deviation_flag_created (supplier_id, deviation_pct, product_sku), brief_generated (supplier_id, flag_count, model_version), negotiation_outcome_recorded (supplier_id, outcome_type, savings_cents).

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment: api-svc (FastAPI), worker-svc (Celery), frontend-svc (React served via nginx). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Azure Cache for Redis. Azure Blob Storage for raw invoice files. Azure Document Intelligence (OCR). Azure OpenAI endpoint in same region.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: on push to main — pytest (80% coverage gate), mypy strict, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps rolling deploy. Nightly integration test run against staging environment with synthetic invoices.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor metrics: OCR job duration histogram, Celery queue depth by queue name, LLM call P95 latency, deviation flag creation rate, brief generation success rate. Application Insights for request tracing. Alerts: OCR queue depth >50 sustained 10 min, LLM error rate >10% in 30 min, nightly scoring job failure (PagerDuty P1).

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an AI procurement intelligence system where the core value is the LLM-generated negotiation brief backed by statistically sound deviation scoring. Key decisions: Azure OpenAI for data residency compliance — GPT-4o briefs reference USDA/market indices with source attribution so managers can cite benchmarks in supplier calls; OCR via Azure Document Intelligence for high accuracy on scanned invoice PDFs; Celery beat for nightly deviation scoring without adding a scheduler service; deviation scoring uses a materialized view with 90-day rolling market averages to keep the nightly batch performant; LLM briefs cached in Redis 24h to avoid regenerating on every dashboard open.
target_output.title: AI Supplier Invoice Intelligence and Negotiation Brief Generation Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An AI procurement intelligence system for contract food service companies built on Python 3.12 FastAPI with Azure OpenAI GPT-4o, Azure Container Apps, and PostgreSQL. Supplier invoices arrive via email, are OCR-extracted by Azure Document Intelligence, and line items are statistically scored against a 90-day rolling market price index to identify deviation flags. A Celery async pipeline generates structured negotiation briefs per supplier using GPT-4o, citing specific overpayment amounts and market benchmark sources. Procurement managers review flags and briefs in a React 18 dashboard, record negotiation outcomes, and track cost recovery. All financial data stays within Azure's geographic boundary per data residency requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven batch architecture on Azure Container Apps with three services: FastAPI API service, Celery worker service, and React frontend served via nginx. Invoice ingestion triggers an async Celery job that calls Azure Document Intelligence OCR and extracts structured line items into PostgreSQL. A Celery beat nightly job at 02:00 UTC runs deviation scoring across all uninvoiced line items in the 30-day window, joining against a materialized view of 90-day rolling market price averages to compute deviation percentages and generate price_deviation_flags. A separate Celery job generates negotiation briefs by loading all pending flags for a supplier, constructing an Azure OpenAI GPT-4o prompt with structured deviation data (never raw invoice text), and storing the structured JSON brief. React dashboard pulls flags and briefs via FastAPI REST endpoints. Azure AD SSO with RBAC gates all access; audit_events table captures every flag review and brief generation action for SOC 2 evidence.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery 5.4 with Redis broker, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg, pydantic v2. OCR: Azure Document Intelligence (azure-ai-formrecognizer SDK). LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via openai Python SDK (azure endpoint). Statistical scoring: pandas, numpy for 90-day rolling average deviation computation. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, TanStack Table for invoice line item grids, React Query. Auth: MSAL Python SDK (backend), MSAL React (frontend), Azure AD JWT validation. Infrastructure: Azure Container Apps, Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Azure Cache for Redis, Azure Blob Storage, Azure Container Registry. CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Observability: Azure Monitor, Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Invoice ingestion: procurement analyst uploads PDF/image invoice via drag-drop UI; POST /api/v1/invoices/upload stores raw file to Azure Blob Storage, creates supplier_invoices record, enqueues OCR Celery job. OCR extraction: Celery high-priority worker calls Azure Document Intelligence with prebuilt-invoice model, extracts line items (product_sku, description, quantity, unit_price), writes to invoice_line_items. Deviation scoring: nightly Celery beat job joins invoice_line_items against market_price_averages_mv (materialized view, refreshed nightly), computes deviation_pct = (actual - expected) / expected * 100, creates price_deviation_flags for items with deviation_pct > configurable threshold (default 8%). Brief generation: procurement_manager triggers POST /api/v1/briefs/generate/:supplier_id; Celery job constructs prompt with supplier flagged items summary, calls Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with json_object response format, stores structured brief with overpayment_summary, per_item_talking_points, recommended_target_price, market_context sections. Dashboard: manager views flags grouped by supplier with deviation severity heatmap, opens brief with talking points for each flagged item, records negotiation outcome (price_concession_pct, savings_cents). Analytics: spend deviation trend charts by supplier and product category over configurable period.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps: api-svc (FastAPI, 2 replicas, scales on HTTP requests), worker-svc (Celery, 3 replicas, scales on Redis queue depth via KEDA), frontend-svc (nginx serving React build, 1 replica). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: primary with read replica for analytics queries. Azure Cache for Redis: Celery broker, brief cache (24h TTL), market price snapshot cache. Azure Blob Storage: raw invoice file storage with private container, SAS URL generation for secure download. Azure Document Intelligence: invoice model endpoint. Azure OpenAI: GPT-4o deployment in same Azure region as Container Apps. Azure Key Vault: all secrets accessed via managed identity. Application Insights: request tracing and custom metric emission.
target_output.workflows: Invoice to Flag Workflow: Procurement analyst drops an invoice PDF on the upload panel. React calls POST /api/v1/invoices/upload (multipart). FastAPI streams file to Azure Blob Storage, creates supplier_invoices record with ocr_status=pending, enqueues ocr_extraction Celery task on high-priority queue. Celery worker calls Document Intelligence prebuilt-invoice, receives structured line item table, writes invoice_line_items rows, updates ocr_status=complete. At 02:00 UTC, Celery beat triggers nightly_deviation_scoring: queries market_price_averages_mv for relevant product categories, joins against unscored line items, creates price_deviation_flags for items exceeding the threshold with deviation_pct and expected_price_cents populated. Procurement manager sees new flags count badge in dashboard. Brief Generation and Negotiation Workflow: Manager selects supplier with pending flags, clicks 'Generate Negotiation Brief'. POST /api/v1/briefs/generate/SUPP-042 enqueues brief_generation Celery task. Worker loads all pending flags for SUPP-042, fetches 90-day trend data, constructs prompt: 'You are a procurement analyst. Supplier: [name]. Flagged items: [{item, contracted, actual, market_avg, market_source}]. Generate a negotiation brief.' Azure OpenAI GPT-4o returns JSON with overpayment_summary (total estimated overcharge), per_item_talking_points (specific price variance with market benchmark citation), recommended_target_price per item, market_context (trend commentary). Brief stored in negotiation_briefs, cached in Redis 24h. Manager uses talking points in supplier call, records outcome: supplier agreed to $0.12/lb reduction on chicken breast. savings_cents calculated and written to negotiation_outcomes.
target_output.data_model: supplier_invoices (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, invoice_number VARCHAR, invoice_date DATE, total_amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3) DEFAULT 'USD', source ENUM email/edi/manual, ocr_status ENUM pending/processing/complete/failed, ocr_confidence_avg FLOAT nullable, raw_blob_path VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). invoice_line_items (id UUID PK, invoice_id UUID FK, product_sku VARCHAR, description VARCHAR, quantity DECIMAL(10,3), unit_price_cents INT, line_total_cents BIGINT, scored BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, extracted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). market_price_indices (id UUID PK, product_category VARCHAR, commodity_code VARCHAR nullable, source_name VARCHAR, source_frequency ENUM daily/weekly/monthly, unit VARCHAR, index_date DATE, price_cents INT, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ). market_price_averages_mv — MATERIALIZED VIEW: SELECT product_category, AVG(price_cents) OVER (ORDER BY index_date ROWS 90 PRECEDING) as rolling_90d_avg_cents, MAX(index_date) as as_of_date GROUP BY product_category. price_deviation_flags (id UUID PK, line_item_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, deviation_pct FLOAT, actual_price_cents INT, expected_price_cents INT, market_index_id UUID FK, scoring_run_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending_review/acknowledged/disputed/escalated/resolved, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). negotiation_briefs (id UUID PK, supplier_id UUID FK, brief_content JSONB, total_flagged_items INT, estimated_overcharge_cents BIGINT, model_version VARCHAR, prompt_version VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cache_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ). negotiation_outcomes (id UUID PK, brief_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, negotiated_by UUID FK, outcome_type ENUM price_reduction/no_change/credit_issued/escalated, price_concession_pct FLOAT nullable, savings_cents BIGINT nullable, notes TEXT, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) — INSERT ONLY, no UPDATE/DELETE.
target_output.api_design: Invoices: POST /api/v1/invoices/upload (multipart), GET /api/v1/invoices (paginated, filter: supplier_id, status, date_range), GET /api/v1/invoices/:id/line-items. Flags: GET /api/v1/flags (filter: supplier_id, status, min_deviation_pct), PATCH /api/v1/flags/:id/review {action: 'acknowledged'|'disputed'|'escalated', note}. Briefs: POST /api/v1/briefs/generate/:supplier_id (enqueues async job, returns job_id), GET /api/v1/briefs/status/:job_id, GET /api/v1/briefs/:id. Outcomes: POST /api/v1/outcomes {brief_id, outcome_type, price_concession_pct, savings_cents}. Analytics: GET /api/v1/analytics/deviation-summary?supplier_id=&period=90d, GET /api/v1/analytics/savings-tracker. All endpoints require Azure AD Bearer JWT; role enforcement per endpoint.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO: MSAL React (frontend) and MSAL Python (backend) validate Azure AD JWT tokens. Role claims from AAD group membership: procurement_analyst, procurement_manager, procurement_admin mapped in FastAPI dependency middleware. Financial data at rest: Azure Database for PostgreSQL with Azure Disk Encryption; Azure Blob Storage with encryption at rest. TLS 1.3 on all Container Apps ingress. Azure Key Vault: PostgreSQL DSN, Redis URL, Document Intelligence key, Azure OpenAI key — all accessed via Container Apps managed identity (no secrets in environment variables). LLM data residency: Azure OpenAI deployment in Azure East US 2 matching Container Apps region; no calls to OpenAI direct API. LLM prompt policy: prompts contain only structured extracted data (product_sku, price_cents, deviation_pct, market_source) — never raw invoice PDF text or PII. Audit trail: audit_events INSERT-only table written on every material action; Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace ingests structured logs for SOC 2 Type II evidence. Access review: procurement_admin role membership reviewed quarterly.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps environment in East US 2. api-svc: python:3.12-slim Docker image, min-replicas 1, max-replicas 10, scale on HTTP requests via built-in KEDA scaler. worker-svc: same image with Celery entrypoint, min-replicas 1, max-replicas 6, scale on Redis queue depth via KEDA external scaler (lagThreshold 20 per queue). frontend-svc: nginx:alpine serving React build, min-replicas 1. Azure DevOps pipeline: on push to main — pytest (80% coverage), mypy strict, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps revision deploy with 10% traffic split for canary. On tag v* — full traffic promotion. PostgreSQL Flexible Server: Standard_D2ds_v4, zone-redundant HA, automated daily backups with 35-day retention, read replica for analytics queries. Redis Cache: C1 Standard (1GB). Azure Blob Storage: private container, lifecycle policy moving invoices to Cool tier after 90 days, Archive tier after 2 years. Celery beat scheduler runs as dedicated Container Apps job (not in worker-svc) to avoid duplicate scheduling on scale-out.
target_output.observability: Application Insights custom events: ocr_job_completed (duration_ms, confidence_avg, line_items_extracted), deviation_flag_created (supplier_id, product_sku, deviation_pct), brief_generated (supplier_id, flag_count, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, duration_ms, model_version), negotiation_outcome_recorded (supplier_id, outcome_type, savings_cents). Azure Monitor alerts: OCR Celery queue depth >50 sustained 10 minutes (PagerDuty P2), LLM error rate >10% in 30-minute window (PagerDuty P1), nightly scoring job failure (PagerDuty P1), Azure OpenAI token quota >80% (Slack P3). Dashboard: brief generation P95 latency, average deviation_pct by supplier (rolling 30 days), cumulative savings_cents by quarter, flag resolution rate by status.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure Container Apps: ~$80/month for api-svc + worker-svc at baseline (2 replicas each). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Standard_D2ds_v4 + read replica: ~$200/month. Redis Cache C1: ~$55/month. Azure Blob Storage 500GB: ~$10/month. Azure Document Intelligence: ~$15/month at 500 invoices/month (S0 tier). Azure OpenAI GPT-4o: ~$0.50 per supplier brief (8000 input + 1000 output tokens) — 200 briefs/month = ~$100/month. Total: ~$460/month at 500 invoices and 200 briefs per month. Scaling: worker-svc KEDA scaling handles burst invoice processing; read replica absorbs analytics query load independently.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Email invoice ingestion UI with Azure Blob Storage upload, Azure Document Intelligence OCR extraction pipeline, nightly deviation scoring against manual market price index entries, price_deviation_flags table and manager review UI, Azure OpenAI GPT-4o negotiation brief generation, procurement manager dashboard with React 18 TanStack Table, Azure AD SSO with RBAC, audit_events table, Azure DevOps CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Market price index automated pull integrations (USDA Agricultural Marketing Service API, Urner Barry price feed), EDI invoice ingestion, negotiation outcome recording with savings tracking, spend deviation trend analytics charts. Phase 3 (future): Predictive price forecasting model, automated RFP generation for high-deviation suppliers, mobile approval app via API-first design.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: mypy strict, ruff linting, pytest with >80% coverage; deviation scoring function covered by property-based tests (hypothesis) verifying that deviation_pct calculation is symmetric and bounded. Celery tasks must be idempotent: check for existing scored flag before creating duplicate. LLM prompt templates stored in prompts/ directory as YAML files with version numbers in filenames — never embedded in Python code. All Azure OpenAI calls log prompt_version and model_version to negotiation_briefs for reproducibility audits. React: TypeScript strict, React Testing Library for dashboard component tests. No direct SQL in API handlers — all queries via SQLAlchemy ORM or named query functions in a queries/ module.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Document Intelligence OCR accuracy on handwritten or non-standard supplier invoice formats. Mitigation: ocr_confidence_avg stored per invoice; invoices below 0.85 confidence flagged for manual line item review in dashboard before scoring. Risk: Market price index data latency — USDA weekly index means deviation scores are stale by up to 7 days during market volatility. Mitigation: index_date displayed on flag cards in dashboard; brief talks points cite specific benchmark date. Tradeoff: nightly batch deviation scoring over real-time streaming — significantly simpler architecture at the cost of up to 24-hour lag from invoice ingestion to flag creation; acceptable for procurement workflows where negotiation happens on weekly/monthly cycles. Tradeoff: Azure OpenAI over fine-tuned procurement-specific model — zero-shot GPT-4o brief quality is sufficient for MVP; fine-tuning evaluated in Phase 3 when negotiation outcome data accumulates for training signal.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Azure OpenAI over OpenAI direct API for data residency compliance — confirmed Azure East US 2 deployment matches Container Apps region. Chose nightly batch scoring over real-time streaming — procurement negotiation cadence is weekly so 24-hour flag latency is acceptable. Selected Celery beat as dedicated Container Apps job (not embedded in worker-svc) to prevent duplicate scheduler instances on scale-out. Confirmed structured JSON LLM output with json_object response format to enable reliable brief section rendering without output parsing heuristics.

metadata.notes: Greenfield AI invoice intelligence system for contract food service procurement; LLM negotiation brief generation with market index deviation scoring is the core value proposition.

project_goal.value: Migrate the national pizza chain franchisee portal from ASP.NET WebForms to React/Node.js, providing franchise owners with royalty fee statements, peer benchmarking, and corporate training module completions while preserving all existing data and user accounts
project_goal.rationale: Legacy WebForms intranet has reached end-of-support; franchisee expectations demand a modern responsive experience

target_users.value: Franchise owners and their designated staff, plus corporate L&D administrators and financial reporting teams
target_users.rationale: Multiple roles with different data access requirements across 800+ franchise locations

project_class.rationale: Server-rendered web application with authenticated portal UX, not a static site

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: React frontend, Node.js API, SQL Server data, Azure AD auth, analytics dashboards, corporate admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: Legacy data migration, multi-role authorization, financial benchmarking, and training tracking across 800+ franchisees

risk_level.rationale: Financial data (royalty statements) and proprietary benchmarking data; no health data; franchise contract sensitivity

data_sensitivity.rationale: Royalty fee statements and sales performance benchmarking constitute confidential financial data under franchise agreements

external_exposure.rationale: Portal accessible only to authenticated franchise owners and corporate staff

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO with RBAC: franchisee_owner, franchisee_staff, corporate_ld_admin, financial_reporting_admin roles; data scoped to franchisee_id for owner and staff roles
access_model.rationale: Azure AD alignment with corporate identity infrastructure

feature_scope.value: Royalty fee statement viewer with itemized breakdown, peer performance benchmarking with anonymized cohort comparison, corporate training module library with video and quiz, completion tracking dashboard, corporate announcement feed, admin tools for content and user management
feature_scope.rationale: Full franchisee portal feature set

mvp_scope.value: Azure AD SSO migration for all existing users, royalty statement viewer, training module completion tracking, corporate announcement feed
mvp_scope.rationale: Highest-value features for franchisee retention during migration

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO with MFA enforcement, TLS 1.3, franchisee_id-scoped data access in all API queries, no raw SQL with user-supplied parameters
security_baseline.rationale: Corporate security standards and financial data protection

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript on Azure App Service
frontend_stack.rationale: React team expertise; Azure App Service for Node.js runtime hosting

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Express on Azure App Service
backend_stack.rationale: JavaScript end-to-end with existing SQL Server database

data_platform.value: Microsoft SQL Server 2022 (existing schema preserved); Redis 7 for session caching
data_platform.rationale: Existing data in SQL Server; migration preserves schema to minimize risk

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service (P2v3 plan) with Azure SQL Server Managed Instance
hosting_target.rationale: Corporate Azure tenant; managed SQL Server with existing data

privacy_retention_policy.value: Royalty statements retained 7 years per FDD requirements; training completion records retained for franchisee contract duration; user accounts deactivated on contract termination with 90-day data retention then purge
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FDD data retention and franchise agreement requirements

future_scope.value: Mobile-responsive training module viewer, franchisee forum and community features, automated royalty payment integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 engagement features

constraints.value: Must preserve all existing SQL Server data without schema changes in Phase 1; Azure AD is the only allowed identity provider; deployment to corporate Azure tenant only
constraints.rationale: Data migration risk and corporate IT policy

observability_baseline.value: New Relic APM for Node.js, structured JSON logging to Azure Log Analytics, Azure Monitor alerts for API error rates and slow queries
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate observability standard

execution_preference.value: Parallel development: legacy WebForms remains live until new portal passes UAT; blue-green cutover
execution_preference.rationale: Zero franchisee downtime during migration

compliance_context.value: FTC Franchise Disclosure Document (FDD) data handling requirements; financial data confidentiality under franchise agreements; GDPR if applicable for any EU franchise locations
compliance_context.rationale: FDD and franchise agreement compliance are the primary constraints

project.goal: Migrate franchisee portal from ASP.NET WebForms to React/Node.js preserving all data and users
project.target_users: Franchise owners, franchisee staff, corporate L&D and finance admins

frontend.state: TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for UI state

backend.framework: Express with mssql driver (existing SQL Server schema)

security.auth: Azure AD SSO via MSAL.js and Passport-azure-ad
security.franchisee_scoping: franchisee_id WHERE clause in all financial queries

data.cache: Redis 7 Azure Cache for session state
data.migrations: Schema frozen in Phase 1; additive-only migrations in Phase 2

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

constraints.schema: No destructive SQL Server schema changes in Phase 1

reasoner_reviews.product: The royalty statement viewer is the highest-stakes feature — franchisees will scrutinize their statements immediately after launch. The anonymized peer benchmarking must guarantee that no individual franchisee's data can be reverse-engineered from the cohort view. Training module completion tracking needs to handle the existing completion records in SQL Server without losing any historical data during migration.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Node.js Express with the mssql driver against the existing SQL Server schema is the lowest-risk backend approach for Phase 1. TanStack Query handles the server state caching of royalty statements and completion records. The parallel deployment strategy (legacy WebForms runs alongside new portal until UAT) requires that both systems write to the same SQL Server database — the new portal must be read-only for financial data in Phase 1 to avoid write conflicts.
reasoner_reviews.security: franchisee_id scoping in all Express route handlers is the most critical security control. Every query that returns financial data must include WHERE franchisee_id = :current_user_franchisee_id regardless of what query parameters are passed. Azure AD token claims must be validated to extract the franchisee_id, and any discrepancy between the token claim and requested franchisee_id returns 403.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SQL Server schema freeze in Phase 1 means the new React frontend must adapt to legacy column names and data types. A thin transformation layer in the Express API should normalize the legacy schema to a clean API response shape without modifying the database.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with React Router v6 route groups: /auth (Azure AD callback), /statements (royalty), /benchmarks, /training, /announcements, /admin
frontend.pages_and_flows: Statements list with period selector and itemized fee breakdown table. Benchmark dashboard with peer cohort charts (anonymized). Training library grid with module cards and progress indicators. Announcement feed with pagination. Admin panel for L&D content upload and user role management.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for royalty statements (cache 5 minutes), training completions (cache 10 minutes). Zustand for UI state (active period filter, selected cohort). MSAL.js React provider for Azure AD token management.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA required — financial tables must have proper th scope attributes; training video player must support closed captions; color contrast ratios verified for royalty statement status indicators

backend.service_design: Express application with routers: /api/statements, /api/benchmarks, /api/training, /api/announcements, /api/admin. Passport-azure-ad BearerStrategy for JWT validation. Franchisee ID middleware extracts oid claim from Azure AD token, looks up franchisee_id in franchisee_accounts table.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. GET /api/statements?period=2026-Q1 returns royalty statement for authenticated franchisee. GET /api/benchmarks?period=2026-Q1 returns anonymized cohort data. GET /api/training/modules returns module catalog. POST /api/training/completions records quiz completion.
backend.failure_handling: SQL Server connection pool via mssql with retry on transient errors; statement queries cached in Redis for 5 minutes to reduce SQL Server load during peak login periods after statement release

security.auth_design: Azure AD tenant-scoped application registration, MSAL.js React provider for SPA token acquisition, Passport-azure-ad BearerStrategy on Express for JWT validation
security.authorization_model: Azure AD groups map to Express middleware roles: franchisee_owner and franchisee_staff have read-only access to financial data scoped by franchisee_id; corporate_ld_admin has training content write access; financial_reporting_admin has cross-franchise read access
security.secrets_management: SQL Server DSN, Redis connection string, and Azure AD client secret stored in Azure Key Vault, injected via Azure App Service Key Vault references

data.entities: franchisee_accounts (existing: franchisee_id, azure_ad_oid, tier, region_id, enrolled_date), royalty_statements (existing: statement_id, franchisee_id, period, gross_sales, royalty_fee, marketing_fee, net_amount, generated_at), training_modules (existing: module_id, title, video_url, quiz_json, required_by_role, active), completion_records (existing: completion_id, franchisee_id, module_id, staff_id, completed_at, quiz_score), peer_comparison_groups (existing: group_id, region_id, tier, member_franchisee_ids), corporate_announcements (existing: announcement_id, title, body, published_at, target_roles)
data.retention_and_deletion: Royalty statements read-only in Phase 1; no deletions. Completion records append-only. User account deactivation sets active=0 with deactivated_at; purge job runs 90 days post-deactivation.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service P2v3 plan with auto-scale 2-4 instances. Azure SQL MI for SQL Server. Azure Cache for Redis Basic C1. Azure CDN for static React bundle. Azure DevOps pipeline.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps: Node.js unit tests (Jest), React build and type-check, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, App Service deploy with deployment slot (staging) swap to production after smoke tests
devops.rollback_strategy: Azure App Service deployment slot swap rollback — swap staging back to production in <30 seconds. SQL Server: no destructive migrations in Phase 1, so no migration rollback required.

ISS-001.title: Peer benchmarking anonymization must prevent reverse-engineering individual franchisee data
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan described returning anonymized cohort averages but did not specify the minimum cohort size below which individual data could be inferred. A peer group of 3 franchisees has insufficient anonymization.

history.note: Initially flagged as legal risk
Plan added anonymized averages but no k-anonymity threshold specified

ISS-002.title: Legacy SQL Server schema normalization layer not defined
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned a transformation layer but round 2 still used raw SQL Server column names in API response shapes.

history.note: Flagged by architect_reasoner
Added Express DTO transformation layer mapping legacy columns to clean API schema

ISS-003.title: Parallel deployment write conflict risk during migration window
ISS-003.detail: Both legacy WebForms and new React portal will write to the same SQL Server during UAT overlap period. Training completions from both systems could conflict.

history.note: Raised by architect_reasoner in round 2 review

focus_issues.title: Peer benchmarking anonymization must prevent reverse-engineering individual franchisee data
focus_issues.detail: Round 2 plan described returning anonymized cohort averages but did not specify the minimum cohort size below which individual data could be inferred.

focus_issues.title: Parallel deployment write conflict risk during migration window
focus_issues.detail: Both legacy WebForms and new React portal write to the same SQL Server during UAT overlap; training completions could conflict.

latest_recommendations: Specify k-anonymity threshold for benchmarking: minimum peer_comparison_group size of 10 franchisees required before returning cohort averages; below threshold return suppressed response with explanation

Define new portal as read-only for completion_records during UAT overlap period; legacy WebForms retains write authority; new portal writes completions only after cutover flag is toggled in feature config
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Pizza Franchise Portal Migration — Round 2 Architecture

convergence_state.reason: High severity anonymization issue and medium write conflict risk unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Good migration strategy and Azure AD integration, but peer benchmarking anonymization gap is a legal risk under franchise agreements and FDD, and parallel deployment write conflict strategy is undefined.
strengths: Azure AD franchisee_id scoping middleware correctly prevents cross-franchisee data access
Express DTO transformation layer cleanly normalizes legacy SQL Server schema
Deployment slot swap strategy for zero-downtime cutover is well designed
concerns: Benchmarking cohort can expose individual franchisee data if peer group is small
New portal writing training completions alongside legacy system during overlap creates potential duplicate records
blocking_issues: Benchmarking anonymization threshold must be specified before financial data is exposed in new portal
recommendations: Add minimum_cohort_size=10 check in /api/benchmarks handler; if peer_comparison_group has fewer than 10 members, return 200 with {suppressed: true, reason: 'insufficient_cohort_size'} rather than potentially identifiable averages
New portal operates in read-only mode for training completions during UAT; a feature flag ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=false prevents dual writes; toggle to true at cutover

issue_updates.title: Benchmarking anonymization threshold
issue_updates.detail: No minimum cohort size specified in round 2

issue_updates.title: Parallel write conflict on training completions
issue_updates.detail: Dual-write prevention strategy not described

previous_plan.title: Pizza Franchise Portal Migration — Round 2 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and Node.js Express migration of the ASP.NET WebForms franchisee portal, reading from existing SQL Server 2022 via Express DTO transformation layer, Azure AD SSO with franchisee_id claim scoping, parallel deployment with legacy system during UAT.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA with TanStack Query, Node.js Express with Passport-azure-ad, SQL Server 2022 existing schema, Redis for session cache, Azure App Service, Azure DevOps CI.
previous_plan.data_model: franchisee_accounts, royalty_statements, training_modules, completion_records, peer_comparison_groups, corporate_announcements — all existing SQL Server schema, read-only in Phase 1
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO, franchisee_id WHERE clause on all financial queries, Azure Key Vault for secrets

best_plan.title: Pizza Franchise Portal Migration — Round 2 Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and Node.js Express migration of ASP.NET WebForms portal with Azure AD SSO and existing SQL Server schema.
best_plan.architecture_overview: React SPA, Node.js Express, SQL Server 2022, Redis, Azure App Service.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close two issues: the benchmarking anonymization gap (ISS-001) requires a k-anonymity check with minimum cohort size of 10 before returning peer comparison data, and the parallel write conflict (ISS-003) requires a feature flag to keep the new portal read-only for training completions until legacy cutover. Both are low-complexity fixes that primarily affect the Express API handler logic and are documented in security, api_design, and deployment sections. The FDD compliance constraint makes ISS-001 a blocking issue that cannot be deferred.

fix_report.action_taken: Added minimum_cohort_size check in GET /api/benchmarks handler: queries COUNT(franchisee_id) from peer_comparison_groups for the requesting franchisee's group; if count < 10, returns HTTP 200 {suppressed: true, reason: 'insufficient_cohort_size', message: 'Benchmark data requires a minimum of 10 peer franchisees in your comparison group'} without returning any financial averages. This threshold is configurable via BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE environment variable. Documented in api_design and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Individual franchisee financial performance cannot be reverse-engineered from peer benchmarks when cohort size is below the k-anonymity threshold

fix_report.action_taken: Defined ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag (Azure App Service application setting, default false during UAT overlap). When false, POST /api/training/completions returns 202 Accepted with {queued: false, message: 'Completion acknowledged; record will be written at cutover'} without inserting to SQL Server. Legacy WebForms retains sole write authority during overlap. At cutover, ops engineer sets ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=true in App Service config without a code deployment. Documented in deployment_and_operations and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No duplicate completion_records during UAT overlap period; clean handoff of write authority at cutover without requiring a code deploy

target_output.title: Pizza Franchise Franchisee Portal — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A React 18 and Node.js Express migration of a national pizza chain's ASP.NET WebForms franchisee portal, serving 800+ franchise owners with royalty fee statements, anonymized peer performance benchmarking (minimum cohort size 10 for FDD compliance), and corporate training module tracking. Azure AD SSO via MSAL.js with franchisee_id-scoped data access enforced in every Express route. Parallel deployment with legacy WebForms during UAT; training completion writes gated by feature flag until cutover. Deployed on Azure App Service against the existing SQL Server 2022 schema preserved intact in Phase 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates via REST to a Node.js 22 Express API on Azure App Service. The Express layer connects to the existing SQL Server 2022 schema via the mssql driver with a DTO transformation layer normalizing legacy column names to clean API response shapes. Azure AD SSO via MSAL.js provides token acquisition; Passport-azure-ad validates bearer tokens on all Express routes and extracts the franchisee_id claim. All financial data queries include WHERE franchisee_id = :claim_franchisee_id. Peer benchmarking returns suppressed responses when the cohort size is below 10. Redis Azure Cache stores session state and royalty statement response cache (5-minute TTL). During UAT overlap, ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=false prevents new portal from writing training completions alongside legacy WebForms. Azure DevOps CI/CD deploys to staging slot with automated swap to production.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Router v6, TanStack Query, Zustand, MSAL.js React provider. Backend: Node.js 22, Express, Passport-azure-ad BearerStrategy, mssql driver, DTO transformation layer. Database: SQL Server 2022 on Azure SQL MI (existing schema). Cache: Redis 7 Azure Cache for Redis Basic C1. Auth: Azure AD with MSAL.js and Passport-azure-ad. Hosting: Azure App Service P2v3 with deployment slots. CI/CD: Azure DevOps pipelines. Monitoring: New Relic APM for Node.js, Azure Log Analytics, Azure Monitor.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Royalty Statement Viewer: franchisee selects period from dropdown; Express queries royalty_statements WHERE franchisee_id = claim; DTO maps legacy columns to {period, gross_sales, royalty_rate, royalty_fee, marketing_fee, net_amount, statement_date}; TanStack Query caches 5 minutes. Peer Benchmarking: GET /api/benchmarks checks cohort size >= 10; if met, returns anonymized peer averages for gross_sales_avg, royalty_fee_avg, yoy_growth_avg for region+tier cohort; if not met, returns suppressed response. Training Module Tracking: module library with completion status per module, video player with quiz, POST /api/training/completions gated by feature flag. Corporate Announcements: paginated announcement feed filtered by target_roles matching authenticated user's role. Admin Panel: L&D admin uploads training modules (title, video URL, quiz JSON), manages module visibility; financial admin exports cross-franchisee royalty report.
target_output.system_components: React 18 SPA bundle served via Azure CDN, MSAL.js Azure AD token provider, TanStack Query cache layer (royalty statements 5min TTL, training modules 10min TTL), Express API on Azure App Service P2v3 (auto-scale 2-4 instances), Passport-azure-ad BearerStrategy middleware, franchisee_id claim enforcement middleware, DTO transformation layer (legacy SQL Server column name mapping), mssql connection pool (max 20 connections), SQL Server 2022 Azure SQL MI (existing schema, read-only for financial data), Redis Azure Cache (session state, statement response cache), ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag (Azure App Service application setting), New Relic APM agent.
target_output.workflows: Royalty Statement Access: Franchisee owner logs in via MSAL.js Azure AD redirect, receives bearer token with franchisee_id claim. Selects Q1 2026 statement period. TanStack Query checks local cache (miss), fires GET /api/statements?period=2026-Q1. Express middleware validates token, extracts franchisee_id=F-0043. Handler queries SELECT * FROM royalty_statements WHERE franchisee_id='F-0043' AND period='2026-Q1'. DTO maps legacy column names. Response cached in Redis for 5 minutes. Peer Benchmark Request: Franchisee navigates to benchmarks. GET /api/benchmarks?period=2026-Q1. Handler queries COUNT of peer_comparison_groups members for F-0043's region+tier group. Count=7 (<10 threshold). Returns {suppressed: true, reason: 'insufficient_cohort_size'}. Frontend displays 'Benchmark data not yet available for your cohort.' Training Completion During UAT: Franchisee staff completes training quiz on new portal. POST /api/training/completions {module_id, staff_id, quiz_score}. ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=false. Handler returns 202 {queued: false}. Staff completes same quiz in legacy WebForms. Legacy system writes completion_record to SQL Server. No duplicate. At cutover, ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=true — new portal writes completions directly.
target_output.data_model: All existing SQL Server 2022 tables, read as-is in Phase 1. franchisee_accounts (franchisee_id VARCHAR PK, azure_ad_oid VARCHAR, tier VARCHAR, region_id INT, enrolled_date DATE, active BIT). royalty_statements (statement_id INT PK, franchisee_id FK, period VARCHAR, gross_sales DECIMAL, royalty_fee DECIMAL, marketing_fee DECIMAL, net_amount DECIMAL, generated_at DATETIME). training_modules (module_id INT PK, title VARCHAR, video_url VARCHAR, quiz_json NVARCHAR(MAX), required_by_role VARCHAR, active BIT). completion_records (completion_id INT PK, franchisee_id FK, module_id FK, staff_id VARCHAR, completed_at DATETIME, quiz_score DECIMAL). peer_comparison_groups (group_id INT PK, region_id INT, tier VARCHAR, member_franchisee_ids NVARCHAR(MAX) — JSON array of franchisee_ids). corporate_announcements (announcement_id INT PK, title VARCHAR, body NVARCHAR(MAX), published_at DATETIME, target_roles VARCHAR). DTO transformation: Express mappers convert snake_case SQL columns to camelCase API responses without touching the database schema.
target_output.api_design: All routes /api/v1/, Azure AD bearer token required, franchisee_id scoped. GET /api/v1/statements?period=YYYY-Q# returns {period, gross_sales, royalty_fee, marketing_fee, net_amount, statement_date} for authenticated franchisee. GET /api/v1/benchmarks?period=YYYY-Q# — returns cohort averages if cohort_size >= BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE (default 10), else {suppressed: true, reason, message}. GET /api/v1/training/modules returns catalog with per-module completion status for authenticated staff. POST /api/v1/training/completions {module_id, quiz_score} — writes if ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES=true, returns 202 {queued:false} otherwise. GET /api/v1/announcements?page=1&limit=20 paginated, filtered by token role claim. GET /api/v1/admin/franchisees (financial_reporting_admin only) cross-franchise royalty export. POST /api/v1/admin/training/modules (corporate_ld_admin only) module content upload.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MFA enforced via Conditional Access policy for all portal users. franchisee_id extracted from Azure AD token oid claim lookup in franchisee_accounts table — client-supplied franchisee_id parameters ignored. All financial queries parameterized with mssql PreparedStatement — no string interpolation. Benchmarking k-anonymity: GET /api/benchmarks suppresses response if peer_comparison_groups member count < BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE (default 10); FDD compliance. ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag prevents dual writes during parallel deployment. Azure Key Vault references for SQL Server DSN, Redis connection string, Azure AD client secret. HTTPS enforced via Azure App Service TLS settings; HTTP redirect to HTTPS. FDD compliance: royalty statements append-only in SQL Server; no UPDATE or DELETE on royalty_statements via new portal API.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps pipeline: npm test (Jest unit tests for DTO mappers, route handlers, and benchmarking anonymization logic), npm run build (React production build), Docker image push to Azure Container Registry, App Service deployment slot deploy to staging, smoke test suite (login flow, statement retrieval, benchmark suppression check), manual approval gate, production slot swap. Blue-green parallel deployment: legacy WebForms IIS servers remain live until franchisee UAT sign-off. New portal at portal-v2.franchise.chain.com; legacy at portal.franchise.chain.com. DNS cutover after UAT. Feature flag ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES toggled in App Service application settings without redeployment. Azure App Service auto-scale: 2-4 P2v3 instances on CPU 70% threshold. SQL Server Azure MI: Business Critical tier for read replica used by reporting queries.
target_output.observability: New Relic APM: Node.js agent tracing Express route handlers; alert on p99 latency >1.5s, error rate >2%. Custom New Relic events: BenchmarkSuppressed (franchisee_id, period, cohort_size) for monitoring cohort growth. Azure Log Analytics: structured JSON logs with fields franchisee_id, route, duration_ms, sql_query_ms. Azure Monitor alert: SQL Server DTU >80% for 5 minutes (read replica query load), Redis cache hit rate <60% (royalty statement caching ineffective), App Service error rate >5%. Weekly report: training module completion rate by franchisee tier for L&D team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Azure App Service P2v3 plan (4 vCPU, 14GB) x2 instances ~$350/month. Azure SQL MI Business Critical (existing, amortized). Redis Basic C1 ~$55/month. Azure CDN for static bundle <$20/month. New Relic Pro ~$200/month. Total new infrastructure ~$625/month. SQL Server MI is existing cost. Scales to 1200 franchisees without tier change; above 1200 concurrent users, App Service scales to 4 instances. Read replica on SQL MI handles reporting admin cross-franchise queries without impacting franchisee statement read latency.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Migration (18 months, parallel): Azure AD SSO migration for all 800+ existing user accounts, React portal with royalty statement viewer, training completion tracking with ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag, announcement feed, DTO transformation layer for legacy schema, Azure DevOps CI/CD, parallel deployment alongside legacy WebForms, blue-green DNS cutover after UAT. Phase 2 Content (4 months post-cutover): Peer benchmarking dashboard with cohort size enforcement, admin panel for L&D content management and financial reporting, additive SQL Server schema migrations to support new fields. Phase 3 Engagement (6 months): Mobile-responsive training video player, franchisee community forum, automated royalty payment status integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in React frontend and Node.js API. DTO mappers tested with Jest unit tests — each SQL Server column mapping must have a corresponding test case. BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE must never be hardcoded — read from environment variable only. Parameterized queries only with mssql PreparedStatement — any raw string query is a CI lint violation. Azure Key Vault references documented in .env.example with no default values. ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag behavior documented in runbook accessible to ops engineers. PR checklist: benchmark suppression test, statement scoping test, and SQL query parameterization review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legacy SQL Server column naming inconsistencies cause DTO mapper bugs. Mitigation: comprehensive Jest tests for every DTO mapper with fixture data from actual SQL Server schema. Risk: Azure AD claim propagation delay after user account changes causes franchisee_id mismatch. Mitigation: franchisee_accounts lookup caches Azure AD oid-to-franchisee_id mapping in Redis for 5 minutes. Risk: Peer comparison group membership data (member_franchisee_ids as JSON in SQL Server) is denormalized — querying cohort size requires JSON_VALUE or deserialization. Mitigation: add a computed column or indexed view for peer_comparison_groups member count in Phase 2 schema migration. Tradeoff: ASP.NET WebForms parallel deployment increases operational complexity but eliminates migration downtime for 800+ franchisee owners. Risk: BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE set too low by ops engineer disables FDD protection; mitigated by minimum value enforcement in Express startup validation rejecting values below 5.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Minimum benchmarking cohort size set at 10 (configurable via BENCHMARK_MIN_COHORT_SIZE env var, minimum 5 enforced at startup). ENABLE_COMPLETION_WRITES feature flag confirmed as App Service application setting — no code deploy required for cutover. mssql npm package confirmed over Prisma for SQL Server — Prisma's SQL Server support is less mature for the legacy schema column types. Azure AD oid-to-franchisee_id mapping cached in Redis to avoid per-request SQL lookup. React Router v6 confirmed over Next.js — existing Azure App Service Node.js deployment model does not require SSR for this portal.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolves franchisee benchmarking k-anonymity and parallel-write conflict for pizza franchise portal WebForms migration.

project_goal.value: Migrate a multi-tenant restaurant reservation platform from legacy on-premises bare-metal to containerized AWS ECS Fargate, migrating MySQL 5.7 to Aurora MySQL 8 with zero-downtime cutover and automated Terraform infrastructure provisioning
project_goal.rationale: Current bare-metal deployment causes 4-hour maintenance windows for updates and lacks horizontal scaling for peak weekend reservation load

target_users.value: Platform engineering team executing the migration; end users (restaurant operators and diners) must experience zero disruption during cutover
target_users.rationale: Migration is transparent to end users; engineering team is the primary operator of the migration tooling

project_class.rationale: Platform and environment migration — Terraform, networking, data migration tooling, not a new application

capabilities.rationale: Infrastructure provisioning and database migration; no frontend work required

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant data migration, zero-downtime cutover, CDC replication, and Terraform state management at scale

risk_level.rationale: Any data loss or extended downtime during cutover directly impacts restaurant reservations and revenue

data_sensitivity.rationale: Guest profiles contain names, phone numbers, email addresses, and dining preference history

external_exposure.rationale: Reservation platform serves public internet traffic from diners and restaurant partners

access_model.value: AWS Cognito for restaurant operator authentication; diner sessions via short-lived JWT tokens; Terraform backend access restricted to CI/CD service role via IAM
access_model.rationale: Existing auth stack migrated to AWS Cognito; Terraform state protected by IAM boundary

feature_scope.value: Terraform modules for ECS task definitions, ALB routing, security groups, RDS parameter groups; DMS CDC replication job for MySQL to Aurora MySQL migration; cutover runbook with automated rollback; GDPR data handling during transit
feature_scope.rationale: Complete infrastructure migration scope

mvp_scope.value: Terraform infrastructure provisioning, DMS replication job to Aurora MySQL 8, staging validation environment, cutover runbook with < 5 minute window and automated rollback
mvp_scope.rationale: Core migration deliverables required for go-live

security_baseline.value: All ECS tasks in private subnets, ALB with WAF, TLS 1.3 termination at ALB, Aurora MySQL encrypted at rest with KMS, VPC flow logs enabled, DMS replication instance in isolated subnet
security_baseline.rationale: AWS security baseline for a public-internet reservation platform handling personal data

frontend_stack.value: None — infrastructure project; no frontend work in scope
frontend_stack.rationale: Existing Laravel PHP frontend migrated as-is via containerization

backend_stack.value: PHP 8.2 with Laravel (existing application, containerized); Terraform 1.7 for infrastructure; Python 3.12 migration validation scripts
backend_stack.rationale: Existing Laravel app containerized without code changes; Terraform for IaC

data_platform.value: Amazon Aurora MySQL 8 (target); MySQL 5.7 on bare-metal (source); AWS Database Migration Service for CDC replication
data_platform.rationale: Aurora MySQL 8 provides serverless auto-scaling, automated failover, and MySQL compatibility for the existing Laravel app

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with multi-AZ deployment
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container hosting with no EC2 instance management; multi-AZ for reservation platform availability

privacy_retention_policy.value: Guest profile PII (name, phone, email) retained per existing privacy policy (3-year default); GDPR deletion requests honored within 72 hours on the new platform
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR Article 17 compliance for EU diner data; no extension of retention period during migration

future_scope.value: ECS service auto-scaling based on reservation volume, Aurora read replicas for reporting queries, WAF rule expansion
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration optimization roadmap

constraints.value: Zero data loss during cutover; maximum 5-minute DNS cutover window; Terraform state must be auditable; GDPR data handling during DMS transit must maintain encryption
constraints.rationale: Business continuity and regulatory requirements for the migration

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for Laravel containers post-migration; Datadog synthetic monitoring for reservation API health during cutover; CloudWatch for DMS replication lag metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog is existing corporate APM standard; CloudWatch DMS metrics for replication health during migration

execution_preference.value: Terraform module-first development with staging environment validation before any production apply; DMS replication tested in staging for 2 weeks before cutover window
execution_preference.rationale: Risk mitigation through staging validation for a high-risk migration

compliance_context.value: GDPR for guest personal data handling during migration; PCI-DSS applicability if any payment tokens are co-located with reservation data in the MySQL database
compliance_context.rationale: EU guest PII in scope for GDPR; PCI-DSS scope assessment required for migrated database contents

project.goal: Zero-downtime migration of reservation platform from bare-metal MySQL 5.7 to AWS ECS Fargate + Aurora MySQL 8
project.target_users: Platform engineering team; end users transparent

security.vpc: Private subnets for ECS tasks and DMS
security.dms: DMS replication instance in isolated subnet, encryption in transit

data.replication: DMS CDC with ongoing replication until cutover

devops.terraform_state: S3 backend with DynamoDB lock table
devops.environments: staging (AWS), production (AWS target)

reasoner_reviews.product: The zero-downtime cutover constraint is the most complex part of this migration. The DMS CDC replication must run for at least 2 weeks in staging to validate replication lag and data integrity before any production attempt. The 5-minute cutover window requires a precise runbook with pre-validated rollback steps — not a general plan.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Key design decisions: Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 is preferable over provisioned for the reservation platform's highly variable weekend peak load. DMS full-load plus CDC replication is the correct approach; the critical path is validating schema compatibility between MySQL 5.7 and Aurora MySQL 8 — Laravel migration compatibility must be tested in staging before any production replication. Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB locking is mandatory.
reasoner_reviews.security: DMS replication instance must be in a dedicated isolated subnet with no internet access. DMS source endpoint connection to bare-metal MySQL 5.7 requires a Secrets Manager stored credential, not a hardcoded connection string in Terraform. Guest PII traverses the DMS replication pipeline — DMS task must use SSL/TLS for the source endpoint connection. GDPR: no additional copies of guest PII may be retained in DMS task logs beyond what is needed for replication.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5-minute cutover window is achievable only if DMS replication lag is under 30 seconds at cutover time. Monitoring DMS replication lag via CloudWatch metric ReplicationTaskStats is mandatory during the cutover window. Rollback plan: if lag exceeds 2 minutes during cutover, abort and revert DNS to bare-metal.

backend.service_design: Laravel PHP 8.2 Dockerized in multi-stage build: composer install --no-dev in build stage, php-fpm in runtime stage. ECS task definition with php-fpm container and nginx sidecar for static assets.
backend.api_patterns: Existing Laravel API routes migrated as-is. Aurora MySQL 8 connection via Laravel DB config pointing to Aurora cluster endpoint.
backend.failure_handling: ECS rolling deployment: minimum healthy percent 100, maximum percent 200. Laravel health check endpoint /api/health validates Aurora MySQL connectivity before ECS marks task healthy.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool migrated from existing auth system. Cognito hosted UI endpoint updated in Laravel .env. DMS source credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
security.secrets_management: Terraform reads DMS source/target credentials from Secrets Manager using data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version. No credentials in Terraform state or GitHub.
security.security_risks: DMS replication logs may contain PII fragments. Mitigation: DMS task log level set to ERROR only; DMS CloudWatch log group with 7-day retention; no DMS task log export to S3.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: VPC flow logs for all subnets. CloudTrail for all AWS API calls. Terraform state versioned in S3 with object versioning enabled.

data.entities: reservations (id, restaurant_id, guest_profile_id, party_size, reservation_datetime, status, table_id, special_requests, created_at), guest_profiles (id, first_name, last_name, email_encrypted, phone_encrypted, dietary_preferences, visit_count, last_visit_at), restaurant_venues (id, name, address, cuisine_type, capacity, owner_cognito_id), table_configurations (id, restaurant_id, table_number, capacity, location_zone), waitlist_entries (id, restaurant_id, guest_profile_id, party_size, joined_at, notified_at, status), migration_checkpoints (id, phase ENUM schema_test/full_load/cdc_running/cdc_stable/cutover_ready/cutover_complete, dms_task_arn, lag_seconds, validated_at, validated_by)
data.storage_design: Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 with auto-scaling 0.5-16 ACUs. Multi-AZ enabled. KMS customer-managed key for encryption at rest. DMS full-load task followed by CDC replication task. Nightly Aurora automated backups retained 7 days.
data.migration_notes: MySQL 5.7 to Aurora MySQL 8 compatibility check required: utf8mb3 to utf8mb4 charset upgrade, strict mode differences, JSON function compatibility. Python migration_validator.py script compares row counts and checksums for top 10 tables post full-load.

devops.deployment_topology: Terraform root module composes vpc, ecs, alb, rds, dms, cognito, waf child modules. ECS cluster with Fargate capacity provider. ALB with two target groups (blue/green for future deploys). Route53 weighted routing for gradual traffic shift during cutover.
devops.environments: Staging: fully provisioned AWS environment in us-east-1 (separate VPC, Aurora MySQL 8, DMS replicating from a mysqldump restore of production). Production: bare-metal current, ECS Fargate target. Cutover: Route53 TTL reduced to 60 seconds 24h before window.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: terraform fmt → terraform validate → tflint → terraform plan (output to PR comment) → on merge: terraform apply for staging. Production apply requires manual approval gate in GitHub Actions.
devops.devops_risks: Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 cold start during off-peak may add latency on first request. Mitigate with minimum ACU=0.5 (never fully cold). DMS replication lag spike during high-write periods could delay cutover readiness.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass plan for a high-risk restaurant reservation platform migration from bare-metal MySQL 5.7 to AWS ECS Fargate + Aurora MySQL 8. The plan is intentionally flawed: it specifies Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 correctly in the data section but the deployment_and_operations section references 'Aurora PostgreSQL' — a technology mismatch between contract (Aurora MySQL) and plan. Additionally, the compliance_context requires GDPR and PCI-DSS assessment but the security section does not address PCI-DSS scope determination for the migrated database, leaving a gap. These flaws reflect plan_quality of flawed.
target_output.title: Restaurant Reservation Platform AWS Migration Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Infrastructure migration plan moving a multi-tenant restaurant reservation platform from legacy bare-metal MySQL 5.7 to containerized AWS ECS Fargate with Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2. Terraform 1.7 provisions all AWS resources; AWS DMS with CDC replication enables a sub-5-minute zero-downtime DNS cutover. GDPR-compliant handling of guest PII during DMS transit. The migration is transparent to restaurant operators and diners.
target_output.architecture_overview: Terraform-managed AWS environment: ECS Fargate cluster in private subnets behind an ALB with WAF, serving the existing Laravel PHP 8.2 application in Docker containers. Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 replaces bare-metal MySQL 5.7. AWS DMS full-load plus CDC replication runs for a minimum 2-week staging validation period before the production cutover window. Route53 weighted routing with 60-second TTL enables the DNS flip with rollback capability if DMS lag exceeds threshold. VPC flow logs, CloudTrail, and Terraform S3 state with DynamoDB locking provide full auditability.
target_output.technology_stack: IaC: Terraform 1.7 with S3 backend and DynamoDB lock table. Containerization: Docker multi-stage build for PHP 8.2 Laravel + nginx sidecar. Database target: Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2 (auto-scaling 0.5-16 ACUs). Migration: AWS DMS full-load + CDC replication task. Validation: Python 3.12 row-count and checksum scripts. Auth: AWS Cognito user pool (migrated from legacy auth). WAF: AWS WAF v2 on ALB. Monitoring: Datadog APM, CloudWatch DMS metrics. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with terraform plan PR comments and manual approval gate for production apply.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Terraform Infrastructure Module: provisions VPC (3 AZs), ECS Fargate cluster, ALB with WAF rules, ECS task definitions and service, Aurora MySQL 8 cluster, DMS replication instance and tasks, Cognito user pool, Route53 records, KMS keys, S3 state bucket. DMS Migration Pipeline: full-load task migrates all tables from MySQL 5.7 source to Aurora MySQL 8 target; CDC task streams ongoing changes maintaining < 30 second replication lag. Migration Validator: Python script comparing row counts and MD5 checksums for reservations, guest_profiles, restaurant_venues, table_configurations, waitlist_entries between source and target. Cutover Runbook: step-by-step procedure with go/no-go checkpoints at DMS lag < 30s, validator pass, health check pass, with rollback procedure at each step.
target_output.system_components: Terraform child modules: vpc (3 private + 3 public subnets, NAT gateways), ecs (cluster, task definitions, services, capacity provider), alb (ALB, target groups, WAF association), rds (Aurora MySQL 8 Serverless v2, parameter group, subnet group), dms (replication instance in isolated subnet, source/target endpoints, replication tasks), cognito (user pool, app clients), waf (managed rule groups: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet). AWS services: ECS Fargate, Aurora MySQL 8, DMS, ALB, WAF v2, Cognito, Route53, KMS, Secrets Manager, CloudWatch, CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, S3 (Terraform state + Laravel assets), ECR (Docker images).
target_output.workflows: Pre-Cutover Validation: Run terraform apply on staging → restore mysqldump of production to bare-metal staging source → start DMS full-load task → run migration_validator.py (row counts + checksums for top 10 tables) → start CDC replication → monitor CloudWatch ReplicationTaskStats.CDCLatencySource for 2 weeks → confirm lag < 30s consistently → run Laravel integration test suite against Aurora MySQL 8 staging → sign off on cutover readiness. Production Cutover Window: T-24h: reduce Route53 TTL to 60s. T-0: confirm DMS lag < 30s → set Laravel DB config to Aurora cluster endpoint in ECS task definition → trigger ECS rolling deploy → wait for 100% healthy ECS tasks → run migration_validator.py against production → confirm health check /api/health returns 200 → flip Route53 weighted routing to 100% ECS Fargate → monitor Datadog synthetic check on reservation API. Rollback: if health check fails or DMS lag > 2 minutes during window → revert Route53 to bare-metal weight 100 → rollback ECS deploy → page on-call SRE.
target_output.data_model: reservations (id BIGINT PK, restaurant_id BIGINT FK, guest_profile_id BIGINT FK, party_size INT, reservation_datetime DATETIME, status ENUM confirmed/cancelled/seated/no_show, table_id BIGINT FK, special_requests TEXT, created_at DATETIME). guest_profiles (id BIGINT PK, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, email_encrypted VARCHAR, phone_encrypted VARCHAR, dietary_preferences JSON, visit_count INT, last_visit_at DATETIME). restaurant_venues (id BIGINT PK, name VARCHAR, address TEXT, cuisine_type VARCHAR, seating_capacity INT, owner_cognito_id VARCHAR). table_configurations (id BIGINT PK, restaurant_id BIGINT FK, table_number VARCHAR, seating_capacity INT, location_zone ENUM indoor/outdoor/bar/private). waitlist_entries (id BIGINT PK, restaurant_id BIGINT FK, guest_profile_id BIGINT FK, party_size INT, joined_at DATETIME, notified_at DATETIME, status ENUM waiting/notified/seated/cancelled). migration_checkpoints (id INT PK, phase ENUM schema_test/full_load/cdc_running/cdc_stable/cutover_ready/cutover_complete, dms_task_arn VARCHAR, lag_seconds INT, validated_at DATETIME, validated_by VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: Existing Laravel API routes preserved unchanged. Migration-specific endpoints: GET /api/health (validates Aurora MySQL 8 connection, returns 200 with {db: 'aurora', status: 'ok', lag_ms: N} — used by ECS health check and cutover monitoring). POST /admin/cutover/start (internal endpoint, protected by IAM policy, triggers ECS blue/green deploy). No new public API endpoints added by the migration project. Terraform outputs: alb_dns_name, aurora_cluster_endpoint, ecs_cluster_arn published to GitHub Actions outputs for pipeline automation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: VPC: ECS tasks in private subnets, NAT gateway for outbound, no direct public IP on tasks.
