target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must close two gaps: ISS-001 requires a concrete mTLS certificate rotation plan using AWS Private CA with a 60-day CloudWatch alarm and delivery platform notification protocol, and ISS-003 requires a Go response middleware that enforces FDA calorie disclosure at serialization time with a data_quality_flag mechanism. Both are operational and compliance requirements that must be specified before production launch given the partner_facing exposure and FDA regulatory obligation.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified mTLS certificate rotation using AWS Certificate Manager Private CA: leaf certificates issued with 365-day validity, stored in Secrets Manager. A CloudWatch alarm fires 60 days before expiry (certificate_days_until_expiry < 60 metric from a Lambda that checks the cert every 24 hours). Rotation runbook: ops engineer requests new cert from Private CA, stores in Secrets Manager new version, triggers ECS service update to pick up new cert, sends email + out-of-band API notification to delivery platform partner contacts 30 days before old cert expires. Old cert remains valid during overlap. Documented in security_and_compliance and deployment_and_operations sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: mTLS certificates are rotated before expiry with delivery platform partners notified in advance; no webhook delivery outage due to certificate expiry

fix_report.action_taken: Added MenuResponseValidator Go middleware in the Gin response chain that iterates menu_items in the response payload after serialization and checks calories_per_serving != 0 for each item. Items with missing calories have data_quality_flag: 'missing_calorie_disclosure' appended to their response object. A Datadog custom metric missing_calorie_data_count is incremented per flagged item. A PagerDuty alert fires if missing_calorie_data_count > 0 for any item in a response served to a consumer_channel with type=pos or type=digital_board. FDA compliance is maintained by surfacing the gap immediately; a data quality runbook guides ops to update the nutritional_facts table.
fix_report.expected_outcome: FDA 21 CFR 101.11 calorie disclosure is enforced at response time; missing calorie data is flagged and alerted rather than silently omitted from consumer displays

target_output.title: Restaurant Menu Management Go Microservice — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin API service on AWS ECS Fargate serving as the authoritative menu management microservice for a restaurant group, replacing a Rails monolith. Exposes menu items with FDA-compliant calorie disclosure and structured Top 9 allergen declarations to POS terminals, digital menu boards, mobile backends, and delivery platforms. Redis 7 pub/sub invalidation ensures menu changes propagate within 30 seconds. Delivery platform webhooks use mTLS with AWS Private CA-issued leaf certificates rotated on a 365-day cycle with 60-day CloudWatch pre-expiry alerting. OAuth 2.0 client credentials for delivery platforms, bcrypt-hashed API keys for POS terminals. Gradual ALB traffic migration from Rails service at 10/50/100% increments.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go 1.22 Gin service deployed on ECS Fargate reads from and writes to PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ for menu item persistence. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster caches menu item responses keyed by menu:item:{id}:v{cache_version}; a Redis pub/sub subscriber goroutine per ECS task instance listens to the menu-invalidation channel and clears affected keys within milliseconds of a write operation. Delivery platform webhooks are delivered via mTLS using leaf certificates from AWS Private CA; a CloudWatch alarm at 60 days before expiry triggers the rotation runbook. A Gin middleware chain validates OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens (delivery platforms) or bcrypt API keys (POS/boards), enforces consumer_channel scope, and runs MenuResponseValidator to check calorie disclosure completeness. ALB with weighted target groups routes POS traffic 10% Go / 90% Rails during initial shadow phase, increasing to 100% Go after validation.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, GORM, golang-migrate, encoding/json. Auth: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (Gin middleware), bcrypt API key hashing, mTLS with AWS Private CA leaf certificates. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode (3 shards, r7g.medium). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (2-8 tasks), ALB with weighted target groups, AWS Private CA, AWS Secrets Manager, CloudWatch (custom metric: certificate_days_until_expiry, missing_calorie_data_count). CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Menu Item Read API: GET /v1/menu/items?location_id=X returns full menu payload with name, base_price, calories_per_serving, nutritional_facts, allergen_declarations (Top 9 structured enum), available flag, and data_quality_flag per item if calorie data missing. Cache-first reads from Redis; miss populates from PostgreSQL. Item Availability Toggle: PATCH /v1/menu/items/{id}/availability triggers PostgreSQL update, Redis PUBLISH to menu-invalidation channel, and webhook fan-out to registered delivery platforms via mTLS. Pricing Rule Management: CRUD for pricing_rules per menu item (fixed/percentage/time_based). Delivery Platform Webhook Registration: POST /v1/webhooks/register for delivery platforms to register event endpoint URL and mTLS client cert thumbprint. FDA Calorie Validation: MenuResponseValidator middleware flags missing calorie data in real time.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate service (Go Gin binary, Redis subscriber goroutine per task instance, MenuResponseValidator middleware), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (menu_items, item_variants, nutritional_facts, allergen_declarations, pricing_rules, consumer_channels), Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (menu item cache keys, menu-invalidation pub/sub channel), ALB with weighted target groups (Go service + Rails service during migration), AWS Private CA (mTLS leaf certificate issuer), Secrets Manager (mTLS leaf cert, PostgreSQL DSN, API key table, OAuth client secrets), Lambda daily cert expiry checker (publishes certificate_days_until_expiry metric to CloudWatch), Datadog APM agent (trace sampling, custom metrics: cache_hit_rate, webhook_delivery_success_rate, missing_calorie_data_count).
target_output.workflows: Menu Item Availability Change: Manager toggles availability via PATCH /v1/menu/items/{id}/availability {available: false}. Go handler validates OAuth token scope (menu:write), updates menu_items.available in PostgreSQL, publishes event {item_id, location_id, available: false, updated_at} to Redis channel menu-invalidation. All ECS task instances running a Redis subscriber goroutine receive the PUBLISH message and execute DEL menu:item:{id}:* in Redis. Within seconds, next POS poll hits Redis miss, fetches fresh data from PostgreSQL. Webhook fan-out goroutine iterates consumer_channels with webhook_url set, sends POST {item_id, available: false, updated_at} to each registered endpoint over mTLS, retries up to 3 times on failure. Full propagation completes within 30 seconds for all connected systems. FDA Calorie Validation: MenuResponseValidator runs after GORM query populates menu item slice. For each item, checks calories_per_serving != 0. Items with zero or NULL calories get data_quality_flag: 'missing_calorie_disclosure' added. Datadog metric missing_calorie_data_count incremented. PagerDuty alert fires if any item in a pos or digital_board response has data_quality_flag set.
target_output.data_model: menu_items (id UUID PK, location_id FK, name VARCHAR, description TEXT, category_id FK, base_price DECIMAL(10,2), calories_per_serving INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, available BOOL, created_at, updated_at — CHECK calories_per_serving >= 0). item_variants (id UUID PK, menu_item_id FK, variant_name, price_delta DECIMAL, calories_delta INT). pricing_rules (id UUID PK, menu_item_id FK, rule_type ENUM fixed/percentage/time_based, value DECIMAL, conditions_json JSONB, active BOOL). nutritional_facts (id UUID PK, menu_item_id FK UNIQUE, total_fat_g DECIMAL, saturated_fat_g DECIMAL, sodium_mg INT, total_carbs_g DECIMAL, dietary_fiber_g DECIMAL, total_sugars_g DECIMAL, protein_g DECIMAL). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, menu_item_id FK, allergen ENUM wheat/milk/eggs/fish/shellfish/tree_nuts/peanuts/soybeans/sesame, present BOOL, may_contain BOOL). menu_categories (id UUID PK, location_id FK, name, display_order INT, active BOOL). consumer_channels (id UUID PK, name, auth_type ENUM oauth/apikey, webhook_url VARCHAR NULLABLE, webhook_cert_thumbprint VARCHAR NULLABLE, oauth_client_id VARCHAR NULLABLE). api_keys (id UUID PK, consumer_channel_id FK, key_hash VARCHAR, key_prefix VARCHAR, issued_at, expires_at, active).
target_output.api_design: All routes /v1/, TLS 1.3. GET /v1/menu/items?location_id=X&category_id=Y — returns {items: [{id, name, base_price, calories_per_serving, available, allergens: [{allergen, present, may_contain}], data_quality_flag?: 'missing_calorie_disclosure', variants: [...]}], cached_at, cache_ttl_seconds}. PATCH /v1/menu/items/{id}/availability {available: bool} — triggers Redis invalidation and webhook fan-out. POST /v1/menu/items — create item (menu:write scope). PUT /v1/menu/items/{id} — update item. GET /v1/menu/items/{id}/nutritional-facts — detailed nutrition panel. POST /v1/webhooks/register {url, event_types: ['availability_change'], cert_thumbprint} — delivery platform registration (menu:webhook scope). POST /v1/admin/api-keys {consumer_channel_id} — issues API key, returns raw key once. GET /v1/health — liveness probe returning {status, db_ping_ms, redis_ping_ms}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: OAuth 2.0 client credentials: delivery platforms exchange client_id/client_secret (stored hashed in consumer_channels) for 1-hour bearer token via POST /v1/auth/token; token includes scope claim. Gin middleware validates token, checks scope. API keys: POS terminals use ApiKey header; Gin middleware bcrypt-compares against api_keys.key_hash. mTLS: Go webhook sender loads leaf certificate from Secrets Manager, presents in TLS handshake. AWS Private CA issues leaf certs with 365-day validity. Lambda checks daily and publishes certificate_days_until_expiry to CloudWatch. CloudWatch alarm fires at <60 days remaining, triggering PagerDuty. Rotation runbook: new cert requested from Private CA, stored in Secrets Manager new version, ECS service updated (picks up new Secrets Manager version), delivery platform partners notified via partner contact email and out-of-band API call 30 days before old cert expires. FDA 21 CFR 101.11: MenuResponseValidator middleware enforces calorie_per_serving != 0 at response time; missing items flagged with data_quality_flag. Top 9 allergens returned as structured enum array, not free text.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: 2 tasks minimum (multi-AZ, us-east-1a and us-east-1b), auto-scale to 8 tasks on CPU 60%. ALB weighted target groups: rails-tg (weight 90) and go-menu-tg (weight 10) for initial shadow phase. GitHub Actions CI: go test ./... -race, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Migration traffic schedule: Week 1-2: 10% Go; Week 3-4: 50% Go with metric comparison to Rails; Week 5-6: 100% Go, Rails scaled to 0. golang-migrate runs at ECS task startup. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ with automated backups. Secrets Manager secrets referenced via ECS task role — no secrets in task definition environment variables. Certificate rotation runbook documented in ops playbook and linked from PagerDuty alert.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: trace sampling for GET /v1/menu/items and PATCH /v1/menu/items/{id}/availability. Custom Datadog metrics: cache_hit_rate (Redis hits / total reads), webhook_delivery_success_rate (successful deliveries / attempts per delivery platform), missing_calorie_data_count (items flagged per response), certificate_days_until_expiry (from Lambda via CloudWatch→Datadog sync). Grafana/Cloudwatch dashboards: menu item read latency by consumer_channel type, cache hit rate trend, webhook delivery failure rate by platform, ALB request split (Go vs Rails) during migration. PagerDuty alerts: API error rate >3% for 5 min, webhook_delivery_success_rate <90% for any delivery platform for 15 min, certificate_days_until_expiry <60, missing_calorie_data_count >0 for pos or digital_board channel responses.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2 tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB each) ~$60/month at baseline. Redis ElastiCache r7g.medium cluster (3 shards) ~$180/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.r6g.large ~$250/month. AWS Private CA ~$400/month (one CA) + $0.75/cert. Datadog APM Pro ~$200/month. Total ~$1100/month. High-volume read path scales via ECS task count and Redis cluster sharding. At 1000+ menu item reads/second, consider Redis read replicas per shard. Rails migration reduces dual-running infrastructure cost after cutover.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Go microservice with GET /v1/menu/items and PATCH availability, Redis cache with pub/sub invalidation, OAuth 2.0 and API key auth, MenuResponseValidator middleware with calorie flagging, mTLS webhook delivery with AWS Private CA certs, ALB 10% traffic shadow, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 Migration (8 weeks): ALB traffic increase to 50% then 100%, Rails service scaled to zero, full menu CRUD (POST/PUT), pricing rule management, delivery platform webhook registration API, Datadog alerts fully configured including cert expiry. Phase 3 Advanced (8 weeks): GraphQL API layer for mobile app consumers, A/B pricing rule testing, multi-region menu replication for international locations, per-location menu inheritance (base menu + location overrides).
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: gofmt + golangci-lint -race in CI. MenuResponseValidator must be unit-tested with table-driven tests covering missing calories, zero calories, and fully populated items. All PostgreSQL queries use GORM named scopes — no raw SQL string interpolation. Redis cache keys follow strict naming: menu:item:{uuid}:v{cache_version} — cache version is a per-location counter incremented on any write. mTLS certificate path loaded from Secrets Manager at startup, not hardcoded. OAuth token validation uses the github.com/golang-jwt/jwt library with Keyfunc loading the public key from Secrets Manager. All Secrets Manager secret names are environment-variable driven — no hardcoded ARNs. OpenAPI spec generated from Go route annotations and committed to repository as api/openapi.yaml.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AWS Private CA monthly cost ($400+) is significant for certificate issuance. Mitigation: single CA issues certs for all delivery platform integrations; cost amortized over multiple partners. Risk: Redis cluster shard imbalance if all menu item cache keys for a popular location hash to the same shard. Mitigation: key includes location_id hash prefix to distribute shards. Tradeoff: Redis cluster mode over single-instance — higher cost but required for >10M cache keys at scale. Risk: mTLS rotation notified delivery partners via out-of-band channels — partner may not act on notification. Mitigation: 60-day notice window and 30-day active follow-up; old cert valid until expiry. Tradeoff: per-item data_quality_flag approach for FDA compliance over blocking the response — allows consumer systems to continue operating while data gaps are surfaced and alerted.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: AWS Private CA confirmed for mTLS leaf certificate issuance — ACM public certs do not support client certificate issuance. MenuResponseValidator runs as a Gin AfterFunc middleware on the response writer to validate calorie fields post-serialization. Cache key versioning uses per-location write counter in Redis to avoid stale key collisions across service deployments. golang-migrate at startup confirmed over separate migration job — acceptable startup latency for ECS rolling deployments. Go 1.22 confirmed over Go 1.21 for improved HTTP/2 server performance for concurrent POS read connections.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolution of mTLS certificate rotation lifecycle and FDA calorie disclosure enforcement for restaurant menu management API microservice migration.

project_goal.value: Research prototype using edge-deployed CV and NLP models to detect customer facial expression and vocal tone at a restaurant counter, streaming aggregated satisfaction scores to a back-of-house dashboard for staff coaching without storing raw biometric data
project_goal.rationale: Investigates real-time sentiment analysis feasibility for restaurant service quality improvement

target_users.value: Restaurant managers and corporate service quality researchers; staff are subjects not users
target_users.rationale: Managers use the coaching dashboard; research team validates model accuracy

security.auth: Custom JWT (local, no external IdP)
security.biometric: No raw video/audio stored; only aggregated scores

reasoner_reviews.product: The staff coaching report must be framed carefully — it should show interaction satisfaction trends, not individual interaction scores that could feel punitive. Aggregate scores per shift avoid singling out specific customer interactions. The consent display at the counter is not just a legal requirement — it needs to be visually prominent enough that customers actually see it.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The AffectNet-pretrained CNN running on Jetson Orin's CUDA cores can achieve <200ms inference per frame at 5 FPS. The interaction session detection (customer steps up, transaction ends) should use a simple motion detection trigger to start and stop scoring — no continuous frame capture when no customer is present. InfluxDB time-series with 5-second aggregation buckets suits the dashboard polling cadence.
reasoner_reviews.security: The critical privacy control is that the inference pipeline writes ONLY aggregated emotion scores to InfluxDB — the raw face detection bounding boxes, landmarks, and intermediate AU values must be discarded immediately after scoring, never written to disk or memory beyond the inference pass. A secondary control: InfluxDB must be bound to localhost only — not exposed on the local network to prevent unauthorized access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Jetson Orin Nano (8GB) can run AffectNet CNN and FastAPI simultaneously. Whisper small (244MB) for Phase 2 voice adds GPU memory pressure — must be profiled before Phase 2 launch. GitHub Actions CI builds Docker container image deployable to Jetson via USB or local network copy — no Jetson-native CI runner needed.

frontend.app_structure: Single-page React 18 Vite app served by FastAPI StaticFiles; route: /dashboard for real-time score display, /reports for per-shift coaching summaries, /admin for JWT login
frontend.pages_and_flows: Live Sentiment Display: real-time satisfaction gauge showing rolling 5-minute average score with color-coded zones (positive/neutral/negative). Session Activity Feed: recent interaction sessions with duration and score. Per-Shift Report: bar chart of avg satisfaction per 30-minute block across the shift, top positive and negative interaction durations. Model Performance (researcher view): inference latency histogram, frame skip rate.
frontend.state_management: EventSource to /v1/stream/sentiment for real-time SSE score updates; React useState for gauge and session feed; no global state manager needed for prototype

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with modules: inference (AffectNet model loader, frame processor), stream (SSE score publisher), api (REST endpoints for reports and health), auth (custom JWT). Inference pipeline runs as a separate Python thread from FastAPI event loop.
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/stream/sentiment (SSE, text/event-stream) — pushes {session_id, score, timestamp} every 5 seconds. GET /v1/reports/shift?date=2026-03-25&staff_id=X returns per-shift coaching summary. GET /v1/health returns {inference_latency_ms, influxdb_write_rate, model_version}.
backend.llm_integration_design: AffectNet CNN loaded via ONNX Runtime on Jetson GPU at startup; inference called per video frame captured by OpenCV VideoCapture; output 8-class emotion probabilities mapped to satisfaction_score = (happy_prob + neutral_prob) - (angry_prob + disgust_prob + fear_prob); score written to InfluxDB measurement sentiment_scores with tags location_id, session_id

security.auth_design: Custom JWT signed with HMAC-SHA256 using a secret key stored in Jetson local environment file; tokens issued on POST /v1/auth/login with manager/researcher username+password from a local bcrypt-hashed credentials file; 8-hour token expiry
security.privacy_controls: AffectNet inference pipeline discards all intermediate tensors immediately after writing scalar score to InfluxDB; no bounding boxes, landmarks, or raw emotion probability arrays written to disk; InfluxDB bound to 127.0.0.1 not 0.0.0.0
security.incident_response_notes: If unauthorized access to InfluxDB is detected, purge all sentiment_scores data immediately and notify the IRB research committee

data.entities: interaction_sessions (InfluxDB measurement: session_id tag, location_id tag, start_time field, end_time field, duration_seconds field, avg_satisfaction_score field, frame_count field). sentiment_scores (InfluxDB measurement: session_id tag, staff_id tag, score FLOAT field, timestamp). staff_coaching_events (InfluxDB: coach_date tag, staff_id tag, shift_start field, avg_satisfaction field, interaction_count field). No relational tables — InfluxDB only.
data.retention_and_deletion: InfluxDB retention policy: 30d on sentiment_scores measurement, 90d on staff_coaching_events; TTL enforced by InfluxDB retention policy configuration; no manual purge scripts needed

ISS-001.title: Consent display mechanism and customer opt-out procedure not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned a consent display at the counter but did not specify the physical display format, what happens if a customer explicitly objects, or how opt-out is logged for BIPA compliance.

history.note: Flagged by compliance reviewer as required for BIPA and GDPR Art. 9

ISS-002.title: Inference pipeline memory leak risk for continuous operation on Jetson
ISS-002.detail: Running AffectNet ONNX inference in a Python thread alongside FastAPI for 8-hour shifts creates risk of GPU memory accumulation if inference sessions are not properly closed and tensors released.

history.note: Flagged by architect_reasoner

ISS-003.title: InfluxDB bound address exposure risk on local network
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify that InfluxDB should be bound to localhost — if it defaults to 0.0.0.0 it would be accessible to anyone on the restaurant Wi-Fi network.

history.note: Flagged by security reviewer
InfluxDB configured to bind 127.0.0.1 only; documented in security section

focus_issues.title: Inference pipeline memory leak risk for continuous 8-hour shift operation
focus_issues.detail: AffectNet ONNX inference Python thread alongside FastAPI — GPU memory accumulation risk if tensors not released.

latest_recommendations: Specify BIPA-compliant consent: a 10-inch tablet or digital display at counter shows a consent notice; staff can toggle 'Pause Sensing' mode from the dashboard to disable inference for a customer who objects; opt-out events logged to InfluxDB with session_id for IRB audit trail

Add explicit tensor cleanup in inference pipeline: after each frame inference call, call del tensor and ort_session.clear_io_binding(); implement a session context manager that restarts the ONNX Runtime session every 500 frames to prevent GPU memory accumulation

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must address the BIPA/GDPR consent mechanism (ISS-001) with a concrete physical consent display plus a 'Pause Sensing' staff control that logs opt-out events to InfluxDB, and the ONNX Runtime GPU memory management (ISS-002) with a session restart every 500 frames using a Python context manager. The research prototype is high-risk due to biometric processing — these two gaps must be closed before the prototype can be operated at any real restaurant location. ISS-003 (InfluxDB bind address) was resolved in round 1 cleanup.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified BIPA-compliant consent mechanism: a customer-visible 10-inch digital display mounted prominently at counter showing consent notice ('This counter uses facial expression analysis for service quality research. No video is recorded or stored. You may request this be paused at any time.'). A 'Pause Sensing' toggle is added to the manager dashboard; when activated, the inference pipeline calls session.stop() and logs an opt_out_event to InfluxDB with timestamp and session_id for IRB audit. consent_display_active flag in FastAPI startup config defaults to true — prototype cannot run without consent display. Documented in security_and_compliance, system_components, and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: BIPA written consent satisfied by visible digital notice; GDPR Art. 9 explicit consent supported by Pause Sensing mechanism; opt-out events auditable for IRB compliance

fix_report.action_taken: Added InferenceSessionManager Python context manager that wraps the ONNX Runtime session. The context manager tracks frame_count per session; every 500 frames, it calls del ort_session, gc.collect(), and torch.cuda.empty_cache() before reinitializing the ONNX Runtime InferenceSession from the model file path. Prometheus metric jetson_gpu_memory_used_mb is exported via the existing Prometheus endpoint and tracked in Grafana; a Grafana alert fires if GPU memory exceeds 6.5GB (80% of 8GB Jetson Orin). Documented in system_components and development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ONNX Runtime GPU memory accumulation prevented over 8-hour shifts; memory usage monitored and alerted before Jetson OOM condition

target_output.title: Counter Sentiment Analysis Research Prototype — Round 2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype on a NVIDIA Jetson Orin edge device deploying an AffectNet-pretrained CNN via ONNX Runtime to score customer facial expression satisfaction at a restaurant counter in real time, with no raw video stored or transmitted. Aggregated sentiment scores stream via SSE to a React 18 back-of-house dashboard for staff coaching. BIPA compliance enforced via a customer-visible consent display and staff 'Pause Sensing' control with opt-out event logging. GPU memory managed via InferenceSessionManager restarting ONNX Runtime every 500 frames. All biometric processing and data storage remain local to the Jetson device.
target_output.architecture_overview: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano (8GB) runs the complete stack: OpenCV VideoCapture captures frames from the counter camera at 5 FPS; InferenceSessionManager feeds frames to AffectNet CNN via ONNX Runtime GPU, discarding all intermediate tensors immediately after writing the scalar satisfaction_score to InfluxDB. FastAPI serves REST endpoints and an SSE stream; a React 18 Vite dashboard connects via EventSource and displays the rolling 5-minute satisfaction gauge and session feed. InfluxDB is bound to 127.0.0.1 only. A customer-visible digital consent display at the counter shows the research notice; a 'Pause Sensing' toggle in the manager dashboard halts inference and logs the opt-out event to InfluxDB for IRB audit. Prometheus scrapes Jetson system metrics including GPU memory utilization; Grafana dashboard visualizes inference latency and memory health.
target_output.technology_stack: Edge Inference: Python 3.12, ONNX Runtime 1.18 with CUDA execution provider on Jetson GPU, OpenCV 4.9 VideoCapture, AffectNet CNN model (ONNX format). Backend: FastAPI, asyncio, InfluxDB-client 3.x Python SDK. Frontend: React 18, Vite, EventSource for SSE, Recharts for coaching reports. Data: InfluxDB 2.7 (local, 127.0.0.1:8086). Observability: Prometheus exporter (custom Python metrics), Grafana on Jetson. Auth: custom JWT HMAC-SHA256 with local bcrypt credential store. CI: GitHub Actions (Docker image build for ARM64 Jetson target). Deployment: manual Docker pull to Jetson via local network.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Consent and Privacy Controls: customer-visible digital consent display at counter; staff 'Pause Sensing' toggle halts CV inference and logs opt_out_event to InfluxDB; consent_display_active startup check prevents prototype from running without the display configured. Facial Expression Scoring: AffectNet CNN processes OpenCV frames at 5 FPS; 8-class emotion probabilities mapped to satisfaction_score = (happy_prob + neutral_prob) - (angry_prob + disgust_prob + fear_prob); InferenceSessionManager restarts ONNX session every 500 frames. Real-Time Dashboard: SSE score stream updates rolling 5-minute satisfaction gauge; session activity feed shows recent interaction durations and scores. Per-Shift Coaching Report: 30-minute block average satisfaction chart, top positive interaction count, session duration distribution. Model Performance (researcher): inference latency histogram, frame skip rate, GPU memory utilization trend.
target_output.system_components: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB (edge device), OpenCV VideoCapture (5 FPS frame capture from USB or CSI camera), InferenceSessionManager (ONNX Runtime CUDA execution provider, 500-frame session restart, tensor lifecycle management), AffectNet CNN ONNX model (emotion probability inference), FastAPI application (inference thread bridge, SSE stream /v1/stream/sentiment, REST /v1/reports/*, /v1/health, /v1/auth, Pause Sensing endpoint), InfluxDB 2.7 bound to 127.0.0.1 (sentiment_scores and interaction_sessions measurements, 30-day retention policy), React 18 Vite dashboard (served as FastAPI StaticFiles, EventSource SSE client), Consent Display Controller (10-inch digital display at counter, always-on notice), Prometheus metrics exporter (jetson_gpu_memory_used_mb, inference_latency_ms, influxdb_write_rate), Grafana dashboard (local).
target_output.workflows: Interaction Session and Consent Flow: Customer approaches counter. Consent display is visible, showing research notice. If customer objects, staff clicks 'Pause Sensing' in dashboard. FastAPI sets inference_active=False flag, calls session.stop() in InferenceSessionManager, writes {event: opt_out, session_id: current, timestamp} to InfluxDB opt_out_events measurement. Inference halts until manager re-enables. Normal session flow: motion trigger from OpenCV frame delta activates session_id generation. OpenCV captures frame at 5 FPS. Frame passed to InferenceSessionManager.infer(frame): ONNX Runtime runs AffectNet inference in GPU thread; output emotion probabilities computed to satisfaction_score; del intermediate_tensor; ort_io_binding.clear_binding_inputs(); score written to InfluxDB. Every 500 frames, InferenceSessionManager reinitializes ONNX session (del ort_session, gc.collect(), torch.cuda.empty_cache()). When customer leaves (motion absence for 3 seconds), session ends; InfluxDB aggregation writes avg_satisfaction_score to interaction_sessions measurement. Per-Shift Report Generation: At shift end, manager requests GET /v1/reports/shift?date=X&staff_id=Y. FastAPI queries InfluxDB for sentiment_scores in shift window, computes 30-minute block averages, returns JSON. React renders Recharts bar chart. No individual customer interaction scores exposed in staff report — only block averages.
target_output.data_model: InfluxDB measurements only. sentiment_scores: measurement name 'sentiment_scores', tags: session_id (String), location_id (String), fields: score FLOAT, frame_count INT, timestamp DateTime — retention policy 30d. interaction_sessions: tags: session_id, staff_id (shift assignment), location_id, fields: start_time INT (unix), end_time INT, duration_seconds INT, avg_satisfaction_score FLOAT, frame_count INT, paused BOOL — retention policy 30d. opt_out_events: tags: session_id, location_id, fields: paused_by_staff_id String, timestamp DateTime — retention policy 90d (IRB audit). staff_coaching_events: tags: coach_date, staff_id, location_id, fields: shift_start INT, avg_satisfaction FLOAT, interaction_count INT, pause_count INT — retention policy 90d. No relational database; no personal identifiers in any measurement.
target_output.api_design: All routes /v1/, custom JWT bearer. GET /v1/stream/sentiment — SSE text/event-stream, pushes {session_id, score, timestamp, active: bool} every 5 seconds. GET /v1/sessions/current — returns {session_id, duration_seconds, rolling_5min_avg_score, pause_active}. POST /v1/sensing/pause {reason: string} — manager halts inference, logs opt_out_event. POST /v1/sensing/resume — manager re-enables inference. GET /v1/reports/shift?date=YYYY-MM-DD&staff_id=X — shift coaching report with 30-min block averages. GET /v1/reports/model-performance?date=YYYY-MM-DD — researcher report: inference latency p50/p95, frame_skip_rate, GPU memory peak. GET /v1/health — {inference_active, gpu_memory_mb, influxdb_write_rate, onnx_session_restarts_today}. POST /v1/auth/login — issues JWT from local bcrypt credential file.
target_output.security_and_compliance: BIPA compliance: customer-visible consent display always active during prototype operation; FastAPI startup validator checks consent_display_active=true in config — exits with error if not set. Staff 'Pause Sensing' control provides opt-out mechanism; opt_out_events retained 90 days for IRB audit. GDPR Art. 9: biometric data minimization — only scalar satisfaction scores written to InfluxDB; no facial images, bounding boxes, or emotion probability arrays stored. InfluxDB bound to 127.0.0.1 — not accessible via local network. Custom JWT HMAC-SHA256; bcrypt-hashed local credentials; 8-hour token expiry. No data transmitted off-device. Research IRB protocol requires quarterly review of opt_out_events rate; if opt-out rate >5% per shift, prototype paused for consent mechanism review. Staff coaching reports never expose individual customer interaction scores — only 30-minute block aggregates to prevent indirect customer identification.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Docker buildx for linux/arm64 target (Jetson architecture), pytest for FastAPI routes and InferenceSessionManager context manager unit tests, mock ONNX Runtime for unit test isolation. Deploy: Docker image copied to Jetson via USB or local network scp, docker compose up starts FastAPI + InfluxDB + Grafana. consent_display_active=true must be set in .env before docker compose up — enforced by FastAPI startup event check. Model ONNX file deployed with Docker image (baked in — not downloaded from cloud). Jetson power mode set to MAXN for full GPU utilization. Log rotation on Jetson to prevent disk fill over multi-week deployments. Daily cron purges InfluxDB data beyond retention policy using InfluxDB CLI delete commands.
target_output.observability: Prometheus custom metrics from FastAPI /metrics endpoint: inference_latency_ms (histogram), jetson_gpu_memory_used_mb (gauge, from nvidia-smi subprocess call every 30s), influxdb_write_rate (counter), onnx_session_restarts_total (counter), pause_sensing_events_total (counter). Grafana dashboard panels: inference latency p50/p95, GPU memory over shift, satisfaction score rolling average, pause event count. Grafana alerts: jetson_gpu_memory_used_mb >6500 (>80% of 8GB) fires local notification; inference_latency_ms p95 >200 fires alert (SLA breach); influxdb_write_rate = 0 for 60 seconds fires alert (inference pipeline stalled).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: One-time cost: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB dev kit ~$499. Ongoing: $0 cloud infrastructure (local-only). Consent display: standard 10-inch Android tablet ~$150. Research prototype operates at single-location scale by design. Multi-location expansion requires additional Jetson units per counter; aggregated cloud analytics require a new consent and data governance framework — out of scope for this prototype.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): AffectNet ONNX inference pipeline on Jetson, InferenceSessionManager with 500-frame restart, consent display configuration, 'Pause Sensing' toggle with opt-out logging, InfluxDB local storage with 30-day retention, FastAPI SSE stream, React dashboard with live gauge and session feed, Prometheus + Grafana health monitoring. Phase 2 Vocal Tone (pending legal review): Whisper small model on Jetson for vocal tone analysis (GPU memory profiling required), composite satisfaction score weighting facial expression 70% + vocal tone 30%, updated consent notice for audio capture. Phase 3 Multi-Location (pending consent framework): cloud analytics aggregation of anonymized satisfaction aggregates across locations — requires new BIPA consent notices per location and IRB amendment.
target_output.development_guidelines: InferenceSessionManager must be unit-tested with a mock ONNX Runtime session that simulates 1000 frame calls and verifies session restart at frame 500 and 1000. FastAPI startup event must validate consent_display_active=True — no silent bypass. All InfluxDB write calls use the async write_api to avoid blocking the FastAPI event loop. nvidia-smi subprocess call in Prometheus metric collection must have a 2-second timeout — Jetson subprocess hangs can block the metrics endpoint. No logging of individual session_id values to application logs in production — session IDs are pseudonymous but could aid correlation. Research milestone checklist: after each 2-week sprint, IRB compliance officer reviews opt_out_events rate before proceeding.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AffectNet emotion classification accuracy on restaurant counter customers (varying lighting, counter height angle, masks) may be poor. Mitigation: establish ground truth accuracy baseline with researcher review of satisfaction score distributions before any staff coaching use. Risk: Jetson Orin Nano thermal throttling under sustained GPU load during peak service hours reduces inference throughput. Mitigation: Jetson cooling fan at full speed in MAXN power mode; monitor inference latency in Grafana with alert on p95 >200ms. Risk: BIPA litigation risk even with consent display — Illinois BIPA has been interpreted broadly. Mitigation: written legal opinion obtained before any deployment; opt-out mechanism reduces exposure; 30-day data deletion limits harm window. Tradeoff: 5 FPS frame capture rate over 30 FPS — reduces GPU load and memory pressure at acceptable accuracy cost for session-level aggregation. Tradeoff: facial expression-only MVP over combined audio/visual — audio adds GDPR and BIPA complexity that delays launch; defer to Phase 2 with separate legal review.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Consent display confirmed as customer-visible 10-inch tablet at counter — not a wall sign (must be interactive per some BIPA interpretations). InferenceSessionManager restart at 500 frames confirmed as primary memory management mechanism — tested to prevent OOM over simulated 8-hour shift of 144,000 frames at 5 FPS. InfluxDB confirmed over TimescaleDB — lighter resource footprint on Jetson. ONNX Runtime CUDA execution provider confirmed over TensorRT for faster model loading (TensorRT engine build adds 2-minute startup time). Staff coaching reports confirmed to show only 30-minute block aggregates, not individual session scores.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolution of BIPA consent mechanism and GPU memory management for edge AI counter sentiment research prototype.

reasoner_reviews.product: The seasonal tasting menu is the centerpiece — dish photography must be stunning and fast. The round 1 build's poor LCP was caused by a 2MB hero image served without srcset and a render-blocking Google Fonts call. Both must be fixed. The press coverage archive should show publication logo, pull quote, and link — not a wall of text. The reservation widget must not degrade the Lighthouse score on mobile.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4's astro:assets pipeline with Sharp handles WebP/AVIF generation at build time with responsive srcset. The hero image needs to be width=1200 WebP under 150KB — achievable with Sharp quality=80 on restaurant photography. Google Fonts must be replaced with system font stack or self-hosted WOFF2. The reservation widget (OpenTable or Resy iframe) must be loaded with loading=lazy and behind the GDPR consent gate to prevent it from blocking LCP.
reasoner_reviews.security: Content Security Policy in Cloudflare Pages _headers file restricts frame-ancestors and script-src to trusted origins. The reservation widget domain (OpenTable or Resy) must be explicitly allowlisted in the CSP. Plausible's script is loaded from plausible.io and must be in script-src. No inline scripts allowed — any reservation widget initialization JS moved to external .js file.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse CI enforces LCP <2.5s and CLS <0.1 as required PR checks — any regression blocks deployment. Image budget of 150KB enforced by a custom check in GitHub Actions that fails if any image in dist/assets exceeds the limit. Google Fonts removal eliminates the 300ms render-blocking resource that caused the round 1 FCP regression.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 pages: index.astro (home), menu.astro (seasonal tasting menu), chef.astro (biography), press.astro (coverage archive), gallery.astro (photo gallery), contact.astro (reservation widget). Astro content collections in src/content/: menu_sections (MDX), press_mentions (YAML), chef_profiles (MDX).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home: full-bleed hero with WebP chef photo, seasonal menu teaser, reservation CTA. Menu: tasting menu sections with dish name, description, and dish photography grid (webP srcset). Chef: biography with portrait, philosophy quote, accolades. Press: press mention cards with publication logo, date, pull quote, external link. Gallery: masonry photo grid with lazy-loaded WebP images. All pages: GDPR consent banner for reservation widget in footer.
frontend.performance_notes: Hero image: preload link in <head> for LCP candidate image; WebP 1200px wide, quality 80, max 120KB. All gallery images: loading=lazy, decoding=async, srcset with 400/800/1200 variants. Google Fonts removed — system font stack (Georgia for headings, system-ui for body). Reservation widget: loading=lazy on the iframe, only rendered after GDPR consent confirmed via localStorage flag.

security.abuse_prevention: No form inputs on the site in Phase 1 — no attack surface beyond the reservation widget iframe
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics loaded unconditionally (cookieless, GDPR-exempt). Reservation widget iframe only rendered after user clicks 'Accept' in GDPR consent banner. Consent stored in localStorage reservation_consent_granted.

ISS-001.title: Hero image LCP candidate not preloaded causing poor LCP score
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 Lighthouse CI showed LCP 4.2s on mobile due to the hero image not being marked as an LCP candidate with a <link rel=preload> in the document <head>. Astro astro:assets generates srcset but does not automatically add preload for the above-the-fold hero.

history.note: Lighthouse CI mobile LCP 4.2s — failing <2.5s gate

ISS-002.title: Reservation widget iframe causes CLS due to unset dimensions
ISS-002.detail: The OpenTable reservation iframe was inserted without explicit width and height attributes, causing layout shift as the iframe content loads and the container resizes.

history.note: Lighthouse CI CLS 0.18 — failing <0.1 gate; identified as iframe resize

ISS-003.title: Google Fonts render-blocking call in document head
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 included a Google Fonts stylesheet link in the <head> causing a render-blocking network request adding ~300ms to FCP.

history.note: Lighthouse audit flagged render-blocking resource
Replaced with system font stack (Georgia/system-ui) in global CSS

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 closes two focused performance issues. ISS-001 requires adding a <link rel=preload fetchpriority=high> hint in the Astro head for the hero image WebP URL, which is available at build time through astro:assets. ISS-002 requires adding explicit height to the reservation widget container div to reserve layout space before the iframe content loads, preventing the CLS from the undimensioned iframe resize. Both are targeted, low-risk changes that should bring LCP below 2.5s and CLS below 0.1 to pass the Lighthouse CI gates.

fix_report.action_taken: Added <link rel='preload' as='image' href={heroImage.src} type='image/webp' fetchpriority='high'> in the Astro BaseLayout.astro <head> slot for pages that include the hero component. The hero image URL is resolved at build time via astro:assets getImage() returning the optimized WebP path. This places the hero image in the browser's preload scanner immediately, reducing LCP candidate fetch delay from 4.2s to an expected <2.0s on mobile 4G. Documented in system_components and workflows sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Lighthouse mobile LCP drops below 2.5s target; hero WebP fetched during head parsing rather than after render tree construction

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit dimensions to the reservation widget container: a wrapper div with min-height: 420px (matching OpenTable widget rendered height at 375px viewport) set in the component CSS. When GDPR consent is not yet given, the container shows a placeholder 'Reserve a Table' CTA button at the same height. When consent is given, the iframe renders within the pre-sized container without resizing the layout. Documented in system_components and workflows.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CLS drops below 0.1 target; reservation widget container does not shift adjacent content when iframe loads or when consent state changes

target_output.title: Michelin-Recognized Fine Dining Restaurant Website — Round 2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A performance-optimized Astro 4 static website for a Michelin-recognized fine dining restaurant deployed on Cloudflare Pages, resolving round 1 Core Web Vitals failures: hero image preloaded via <link rel=preload fetchpriority=high> in the document head bringing mobile LCP to <2.0s, and reservation widget container dimensioned with min-height: 420px preventing CLS from iframe load. Seasonal tasting menu, chef biography, press archive, and photo gallery use WebP/AVIF images under 150KB generated by Sharp at build time. Plausible cookieless analytics; GDPR consent gate for the OpenTable reservation widget.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 with zero-JavaScript-by-default architecture generates a fully static HTML/CSS output deployed to Cloudflare Pages edge CDN. The astro:assets image pipeline invokes Sharp to produce WebP and AVIF variants with responsive srcset at build time; the hero image is preloaded via a <link rel=preload fetchpriority=high> tag in BaseLayout.astro's <head> slot, ensuring it enters the browser preload scanner before the render tree is constructed. Gallery images use loading=lazy decoding=async. The OpenTable reservation widget iframe is rendered inside a pre-sized container (min-height: 420px) behind a GDPR consent gate that reads a reservation_consent_granted flag from localStorage; the container shows a placeholder CTA at the same height before consent, eliminating layout shift. Content (seasonal menu sections, press mentions, chef profiles) managed in Astro content collections as MDX and YAML files committed to the repository. Deployment on every git push to main via Cloudflare Pages Git integration; Lighthouse CI on pull requests enforces LCP <2.5s and CLS <0.1 as required status checks.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, astro:assets with Sharp for WebP/AVIF image optimization, CSS custom properties for typography (Georgia serif + system-ui sans-serif, no external font loading), minimal Tailwind for layout utilities. Content: Astro content collections (MDX for menu_sections and chef_profiles, YAML for press_mentions and dish_showcases). Deployment: Cloudflare Pages with global edge CDN. Analytics: Plausible Analytics (cookieless, loaded unconditionally). Performance gating: Lighthouse CI (GitHub Actions, lighthouse-ci/action). Image CI: custom Node.js script validating dist/assets image file sizes. Security: Content Security Policy in Cloudflare Pages _headers file.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section with LCP-optimized preloaded WebP chef or signature dish photograph (1200px, <120KB, preloaded in <head>). Seasonal Tasting Menu: MDX content collection rendering course sections with dish name, description, and WebP dish photography grid with responsive srcset (400/800/1200 variants). Chef Biography: portrait photograph (lazy-loaded), name, culinary philosophy statement, Michelin recognition callout, press quote highlight. Press Coverage Archive: sorted list of press_mentions with publication name, publication logo (inline SVG or tiny PNG), date, pull quote text, and link to article. Photo Gallery: masonry grid of restaurant interior and food photography, all loading=lazy decoding=async WebP with AVIF fallback. Reservation Widget: OpenTable iframe in pre-sized container (min-height 420px); rendered only after GDPR consent granted; placeholder 'Reserve a Table' button at same height shown before consent.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 build pipeline (Sharp image processing, content collection type generation, HTML/CSS/JS static output), BaseLayout.astro ( with for hero image WebP, CSP meta tag, Plausible script), HeroSection.astro (preloaded WebP hero image with fetchpriority=high on tag and preload in head), ReservationWidget.astro (GDPR consent gate, placeholder CTA div with min-height: 420px, lazy iframe render after consent), Cloudflare Pages (global CDN, HTTPS, _headers CSP file), Lighthouse CI GitHub Action (LCP/CLS/FID gates on PRs), image-size-check.js (Node.js script failing CI if any dist/assets image >150KB), Plausible Analytics script (loaded unconditionally, cookieless).
target_output.workflows: Hero Image Preload Flow: Astro build processes src/assets/hero-chef.jpg through astro:assets getImage() with format: webp, width: 1200, quality: 80 — output is a content-hashed URL like /_astro/hero-chef.AbCd1234.webp. BaseLayout.astro renders <link rel='preload' as='image' href='/_astro/hero-chef.AbCd1234.webp' type='image/webp' fetchpriority='high'> in the <head> slot when the hero prop is passed. Browser parses the HTML, encounters the preload hint during head scan, fetches the hero WebP immediately in parallel with CSS parsing — LCP image is available before layout is complete. GDPR Consent and Reservation Widget: Page loads with placeholder div at min-height: 420px showing 'Reserve a Table →' CTA button. User scrolls to reservation section. If reservation_consent_granted in localStorage is false/absent, consent banner asks 'This reservation widget uses cookies. Accept to continue.' User clicks Accept → localStorage.setItem('reservation_consent_granted', 'true') → ReservationWidget.astro client:idle island renders the OpenTable iframe inside the pre-sized container. Container maintains min-height: 420px throughout — no layout shift occurs. Build and CI Flow: PR opened → GitHub Actions runs astro build, Lighthouse CI captures LCP/CLS/FID from built output, image-size-check.js scans dist/assets for files >150KB — all three checks must pass as required status checks before merge. Merge to main → Cloudflare Pages webhook triggers build and deploy to edge CDN globally in <90 seconds.
target_output.data_model: No server-side data model. Astro content collections: menu_sections (MDX schema: {course_name: string, dishes: [{name, description, photo_src}]}). press_mentions (YAML schema: {publication, date, pull_quote, article_url, logo_svg_path}). chef_profiles (MDX schema: {name, portrait_src, philosophy, accolades: []}). dish_showcases (YAML schema: {dish_name, photo_src, menu_section}). photo_galleries (YAML schema: {image_src, alt, category}). reservation_widget_configs (YAML schema: {provider: 'opentable'|'resy', widget_url, widget_height_px}). All content is static build-time data — no runtime queries.
target_output.api_design: No API endpoints. Static HTML output only. The Reservation widget is a third-party iframe with a src URL from OpenTable or Resy — no custom API. Plausible Analytics is a passive third-party script with no API calls from the site. Content updates deployed by committing MDX/YAML changes to the repository and pushing to main — Cloudflare Pages rebuilds the static output automatically.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content Security Policy in Cloudflare Pages _headers file: script-src 'self' https://plausible.io https://www.opentable.com (or Resy domain); frame-src https://www.opentable.com; default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:. HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare; HTTP redirected automatically. GDPR: Plausible Analytics is cookieless and does not require consent; reservation widget cookies require consent banner — consent stored in localStorage reservation_consent_granted. No user personal data collected or stored on site infrastructure. Content updates do not require a deployment pipeline approval — any commit to main triggers Cloudflare Pages build and is live within 90 seconds.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repository; every push to main triggers automatic build (astro build) and global deployment. GitHub Actions CI on PRs: astro build, @lhci/cli run with .lighthouserc.json config (preset: desktop and mobile assertions: lcp <2500, cls <0.1, fid <100), image-size-check.js (Node.js script: glob dist/assets/*.{webp,avif}, fail if any >153600 bytes). All three CI checks are required status checks — PRs cannot merge until LCP, CLS, FID, and image budget all pass. Cloudflare Pages preview deployments generated for each PR for visual review. Rollback: Cloudflare Pages dashboard allows instant rollback to any previous deployment. Content updates (seasonal menu changes, new press mentions) made by editing MDX/YAML files and pushing to main — no deployment gate required for content-only changes.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics: page views, unique visitors, referrer sources (organic search, direct, social), bounce rate by page — visible in Plausible dashboard. Cloudflare Web Analytics: real user Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) sampled from actual visitors — tracks performance in production separate from Lighthouse CI lab scores. Lighthouse CI reports stored as GitHub Actions artifacts per PR run — historical performance trend visible in CI artifacts. No alerting infrastructure required for a static site. Cloudflare Pages build notifications via Slack webhook if configured.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier covers unlimited static sites with unlimited bandwidth. Plausible Analytics $9/month for up to 10,000 monthly pageviews. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI minutes for static site builds. Total: $9/month. Scales to millions of page views at no additional cost — Cloudflare edge CDN handles traffic spikes (e.g., press coverage going viral) without configuration changes. Phase 2 private dining enquiry form adds Formspree $0-16/month depending on submission volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Performance Fix (4 weeks): Hero image preload with <link rel=preload fetchpriority=high>, reservation widget container min-height reservation eliminating CLS, WebP/AVIF image pipeline for all existing photography under 150KB budget, system font stack (Georgia + system-ui), astro:assets integration, Lighthouse CI gates on GitHub Actions, Plausible Analytics, GDPR consent banner for reservation widget, Cloudflare Pages deployment. Phase 2 Content Expansion (4 weeks): Seasonal menu MDX content collection with dish photography grid, press coverage archive with publication logos, photo gallery with masonry layout, Astro content collection type generation. Phase 3 Engagement (6 weeks): Private dining enquiry form via Formspree (serverless form handling), chef's notes blog using Astro content collections, multilingual content support for Japanese and French via Astro i18n routing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Every image added to src/assets must be run through astro:assets — no raw with unoptimized images. The hero image must always have the fetchpriority='high' attribute on the tag in addition to the preload hint in . The reservation widget container must always specify a height that matches the reservation_widget_configs.widget_height_px — if the widget provider changes height, update the YAML config, not the component. Lighthouse CI assertions in .lighthouserc.json must not be loosened without team agreement — any assertion relaxation requires a PR comment explaining the reason. Image size check script must be run locally before pushing large image batches: node scripts/image-size-check.js after astro build. Content collection schema changes require updating both the Zod schema in src/content/config.ts and any template components that consume the collection.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OpenTable or Resy updates their iframe height, breaking the min-height reservation and reintroducing CLS. Mitigation: widget_height_px in reservation_widget_configs YAML makes this a content change, not a code change; monitored via Cloudflare Web Analytics real-user CLS. Risk: Astro content collection schema changes break existing MDX files during seasonal menu updates. Mitigation: Zod schema validation in src/content/config.ts catches schema violations at build time — CI fails before deployment. Tradeoff: system font stack over custom typography — eliminates render-blocking font loading but reduces design distinctiveness; acceptable given Michelin-level photography is the visual differentiator, not typography. Tradeoff: Plausible over Google Analytics — GDPR-exempt cookieless tracking but lower feature depth (no funnel analysis, no audience demographics); acceptable for a restaurant website with simple page view analytics needs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Hero image preload added in BaseLayout.astro <head> slot using the astro:assets getImage() URL — not hardcoded path, so it tracks content-hash updates automatically. min-height: 420px confirmed for OpenTable widget container — measured from OpenTable embed documentation. Plausible confirmed over Google Analytics 4 — eliminates consent requirement for analytics, removing the GA4 consent complexity from round 1. Cloudflare Pages confirmed over Netlify — better global edge PoP coverage and free bandwidth tier. _headers file confirmed as the CSP delivery mechanism for Cloudflare Pages (no server-side header injection available on static hosting).

metadata.notes: Round 2 performance optimization fixes for fine dining restaurant static website — hero preload and reservation widget CLS correction.

project.goal: Showcase ML demand forecasting product for restaurant groups, capture demo request leads
project.target_users: Restaurant group ops directors and F&B tech buyers

security.headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options via CloudFront response headers policy

data.cms: Contentful for product pages, case studies, blog posts

devops.environments: dev preview on S3 staging bucket, production CloudFront distribution

reasoner_reviews.product: The three case study pages are the highest-value conversion content -- they must present anonymized restaurant group names, actual forecast accuracy improvement percentages, and waste reduction dollar figures to resonate with operations directors. Generic 'improved efficiency' claims will not convert B2B buyers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro islands are the correct choice for limiting JavaScript to the Recharts components only. The rest of the site should be zero-JS HTML. Pay attention to Contentful webhook integration to trigger S3/CloudFront invalidation on content publishes -- the previous round left this mechanism underspecified.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP must explicitly allowlist the Recharts bundle and Formspree endpoint. The GA4 consent banner needs to block GA4 script loading until explicit opt-in, not just set a flag. CloudFront response headers policy should include Permissions-Policy to disable camera and microphone.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 60-second build constraint is achievable with Contentful content caching at build time. Verify Recharts bundle size does not push the homepage JS payload above 50KB -- consider lazy-loading charts below the fold.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan underspecified the Contentful webhook-to-CloudFront invalidation flow and left the CSP allowlist vague. Both must be addressed in this revision.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with islands architecture: product page, case-studies collection, blog collection, demo-request page. Recharts components loaded as Astro islands with client:visible directive to defer JS until chart enters viewport.
frontend.pages_and_flows: / (hero + product value props), /forecasting (product capability deep-dive), /case-studies (index + [slug] detail pages from Contentful), /demo (form page), /blog/[slug]
frontend.performance_notes: Images served as WebP via Astro Image integration with width/height attributes to prevent CLS. Recharts lazy-loaded with client:visible. Total homepage JS budget: 50KB compressed. Preconnect hints for Contentful CDN and Formspree.
frontend.frontend_risks: Recharts bundle size risk if chart count grows; mitigate with dynamic import per chart type.

security.auth_design: No authentication required; public site
security.abuse_prevention: Formspree rate-limiting on demo request endpoint; honeypot field on form to block bot submissions
security.privacy_controls: GA4 consent banner blocks analytics script until explicit opt-in; cookie-less GA4 measurement mode for non-consenting visitors; Permissions-Policy header disabling camera, microphone, geolocation
security.security_risks: CSP misconfiguration could block Recharts or Formspree; maintain explicit allowlist in CloudFront response headers policy.

data.entities: product_pages (slug, title, hero_copy, feature_bullets, cta_label), case_studies (slug, restaurant_group_alias, forecast_accuracy_before, forecast_accuracy_after, waste_reduction_usd, implementation_weeks), blog_posts (slug, title, author, publish_date, body_rich_text), demo_request_submissions (name, company, role, email, restaurant_count) -- last entity lives in HubSpot, not in self-managed storage
data.storage_design: Contentful as source of truth for all content; Astro build fetches via Contentful Delivery API at build time; no self-hosted database
data.data_risks: Contentful API rate limits could fail builds if content fetch is not cached between incremental rebuilds.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions builds Astro static output, syncs to S3 bucket, triggers CloudFront invalidation. Contentful webhook POST to GitHub Actions dispatch endpoint triggers rebuild on content publish.
devops.environments: Staging: separate S3 bucket with CloudFront distribution, preview URL shared with marketing team for review. Production: primary CloudFront distribution with custom domain and ACM certificate.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: install deps (bun install), run Lighthouse CI against staging, enforce score > 90 as required check, sync to S3 on pass, invalidate CloudFront.
devops.devops_risks: Contentful webhook reliability -- implement retry logic in GitHub Actions dispatch handler; stale CloudFront cache if invalidation is skipped after partial builds.

ISS-001.title: Contentful webhook to CloudFront invalidation flow not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan referenced Contentful-triggered deploys but did not define the webhook endpoint, GitHub Actions dispatch mechanism, or CloudFront invalidation scope.
