previous_plan.title: ML Demand Forecasting Marketing Site Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Static Astro 4 marketing site for a B2B food-tech ML forecasting product, hosted on CloudFront and S3, with Contentful CMS for marketing-team-controlled content updates and GA4 analytics.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site with islands architecture served from CloudFront. Recharts components loaded as Astro islands. Contentful drives all content. Demo request form submits to Formspree.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions builds on push to main and on Contentful content publish. Syncs to S3 then invalidates CloudFront. Staging environment on separate bucket. [Contentful webhook mechanism not specified]
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by CloudFront. CSP headers configured. [Allowlist values not specified] GA4 with consent banner.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision addressing two specific gaps from the round 1 audit: the Contentful webhook-to-CloudFront invalidation flow and the CSP allowlist specification. The architecture is a simple static site -- the fixes are targeted at the deployment and security sections. Astro 4 islands architecture is correct for limiting JS bundle to Recharts components. The key design decisions are the GitHub Actions repository dispatch webhook endpoint for Contentful triggers and explicit CSP directive values for the Recharts bundle origin and Formspree connect-src.

fix_report.action_taken: Fully specified the Contentful webhook-to-GitHub-Actions-dispatch-to-S3-to-CloudFront invalidation pipeline in deployment_and_operations, including the repository dispatch event name, invalidation path scope, and error alerting.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Marketing editors can publish content in Contentful and see changes live within 3-4 minutes without engineering intervention.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced vague CSP placeholder with explicit directive values: script-src allowlisting the Recharts bundle CDN hash and Formspree domain, connect-src allowlisting Formspree submission endpoint and GA4 collector, and Permissions-Policy disabling camera/microphone/geolocation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP blocks all unexpected script execution while allowing Recharts rendering and Formspree form submission without console errors.

target_output.title: ML Demand Forecasting Marketing Site Architecture -- Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 marketing website for a B2B food-tech ML demand forecasting product, hosted on AWS CloudFront and S3 with zero server-side runtime costs. Contentful headless CMS gives the marketing team autonomous content control. Interactive forecast accuracy charts use Recharts as Astro islands, loaded client-side only when scrolled into view. Demo request form submissions flow to HubSpot CRM via Formspree. This revision resolves the Contentful webhook invalidation flow and CSP allowlist gaps identified in round 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture: Astro 4 build output (HTML + selective JS islands) deployed to an S3 bucket fronted by a CloudFront distribution with a custom domain and ACM certificate. Content is authored in Contentful and fetched via the Contentful Delivery API at build time. On content publish, a Contentful webhook fires a POST to a GitHub Actions repository dispatch endpoint, triggering a rebuild-and-deploy workflow. Recharts components are the only JavaScript on the site, loaded with Astro client:visible directive to defer until the chart enters the viewport. Formspree handles demo request form submission and routes to HubSpot without self-hosted infrastructure.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Recharts 2.x (chart islands), Astro Image integration (WebP output). CMS: Contentful Delivery API v2 with Contentful webhook for build triggers. Hosting: AWS S3 (static origin) + CloudFront (CDN with custom domain, ACM certificate, response headers policy). CI/CD: GitHub Actions with bun install, Lighthouse CI, S3 sync, CloudFront invalidation. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with gtag.js consent mode. Form: Formspree with HubSpot integration.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Product Capability Pages: /forecasting presents the ML demand forecasting feature set with demand accuracy stats pulled from Contentful fields. Case Study Pages: /case-studies/[slug] renders anonymized restaurant group profiles with Recharts line charts showing forecast accuracy before/after implementation and waste reduction dollar figures. Demo Request: /demo renders a Formspree-powered form capturing name, company, role, email, and number of locations; on submit, lead is created in HubSpot. Blog: /blog/[slug] renders Contentful rich-text body with author and date. Analytics: GA4 conversion event fires on successful demo form submission.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution (custom domain, ACM wildcard cert, HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect, response headers policy with CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/Permissions-Policy). S3 bucket (static origin, versioning enabled, public access via CloudFront OAC only). Contentful space (product_pages, case_studies, blog_posts content types). GitHub Actions runner (build, Lighthouse CI gate, S3 sync, CF invalidation). Formspree endpoint (demo request form handler with HubSpot CRM destination). Google Analytics 4 property (consent-gated via gtag.js consent mode v2).
target_output.workflows: Content Publish Flow: Marketing editor publishes or updates content in Contentful -- Contentful webhook fires POST to GitHub Actions repository dispatch endpoint with event type 'contentful_publish' -- GitHub Actions workflow triggers Astro build with CONTENTFUL_DELIVERY_TOKEN env var -- bun run build completes in under 60 seconds -- aws s3 sync uploads changed files to origin bucket -- aws cloudfront create-invalidation --paths '/*' clears CDN cache -- site reflects new content within 3-4 minutes of publish. Demo Request Flow: Visitor fills /demo form with company name, role, and location count -- Formspree validates and forwards payload to HubSpot via native integration, creating a contact and deal -- GA4 fires 'demo_request_submitted' conversion event -- visitor sees inline confirmation message; no redirect required.
target_output.data_model: product_pages (Contentful entry: slug, page_title, hero_headline, hero_subtext, feature_bullets[], cta_label, cta_url). case_studies (Contentful entry: slug, restaurant_group_alias, industry_segment, forecast_accuracy_before_pct, forecast_accuracy_after_pct, waste_reduction_usd_annual, implementation_weeks, quote_text, quote_attribution_role). blog_posts (Contentful entry: slug, title, author_name, publish_date, summary_text, body_rich_text, tags[]). demo_request_submissions -- stored in HubSpot CRM only, not in self-managed storage: contact (email, first_name, last_name, company, job_title, num_locations).
target_output.api_design: No self-hosted API. External integrations only: Contentful Delivery API GET /spaces/{space_id}/entries?content_type=case_study (called at build time by Astro data fetching). Formspree POST https://formspree.io/f/{form_id} (called client-side on demo form submit). GA4 Measurement Protocol POST https://www.google-analytics.com/mp/collect (gtag.js, consent-gated). GitHub Actions repository dispatch POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dispatches with event_type 'contentful_publish' (called by Contentful webhook on content publish).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via CloudFront HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect; HSTS max-age=31536000 in response headers policy. CSP header: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.googletagmanager.com https://formspree.io; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https://images.ctfassets.net; connect-src 'self' https://formspree.io https://www.google-analytics.com; frame-ancestors 'none'. Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). GA4 loaded via gtag.js consent mode v2 -- analytics_storage and ad_storage default to 'denied' until visitor explicitly accepts the cookie consent banner. Formspree honeypot field blocks automated bot submissions. CloudFront OAC prevents direct S3 bucket access.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow 'deploy.yml' triggers on: push to main branch AND on repository_dispatch event with event_type 'contentful_publish'. Steps: checkout -- bun install -- bun run build (Astro fetches Contentful content at build time using CONTENTFUL_DELIVERY_TOKEN secret) -- Lighthouse CI runs against locally served build output, enforcing LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, performance score > 90 as required gate -- aws s3 sync dist/ s3://prod-forecasting-site --delete --cache-control 'public,max-age=31536000,immutable' for hashed assets, 'public,max-age=300' for HTML -- aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id $CF_DIST_ID --paths '/*'. Contentful webhook: configured in Contentful space settings to POST to https://api.github.com/repos/foodtech-co/marketing-site/dispatches with Authorization: Bearer $GH_DISPATCH_TOKEN and body {event_type: 'contentful_publish'}. Staging environment: separate S3 bucket and CloudFront distribution, deployed from main branch PRs with a preview URL shared with marketing team. Rollback: re-run prior GitHub Actions workflow run targeting production.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4: pageview tracking, 'demo_request_submitted' conversion event on form success, scroll depth events on case study pages to measure engagement. CloudFront access logs streamed to S3 bucket with 90-day retention; Athena queries available for traffic analysis and referral source reporting. Build health: GitHub Actions workflow status badges in repo README; Slack notification on deploy failure via GitHub Actions Slack action. No APM required for a static site.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated monthly cost under $15: S3 storage ~$0.50/month for static assets, CloudFront data transfer ~$8-12/month at 50K monthly visitors, ACM certificate free. GA4 and Formspree free tier sufficient for expected lead volume. No compute costs. Scaling is handled transparently by CloudFront edge caching globally.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Launch (3 weeks): Product page, three initial case study pages with Recharts accuracy charts, demo request form with HubSpot integration, GA4 with consent banner, CloudFront deployment with CSP headers, Contentful webhook build trigger. Phase 2 -- Content Expansion (ongoing): Additional case studies published by marketing team via Contentful with no engineering changes required. Blog section with thought leadership posts. Phase 3 -- Conversion Optimization: A/B test hero headline variants via CloudFront Functions; gated ROI calculator widget with email capture; localized /eu route with GDPR-specific consent flows.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Astro project. Tailwind config scoped to content collections to prevent purge misses. Recharts components isolated in src/components/charts/ -- each chart is a standalone Astro island. Contentful content types versioned in src/contentful/types.ts generated via Contentful CLI. Lighthouse CI score thresholds enforced in ci config, not overridable. Image optimization via Astro Image component only -- no raw img tags. PR preview deployments to staging CloudFront distribution required before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful API outage blocks site rebuilds during content publish. Mitigation: build caches last successful Contentful fetch; site remains live on CloudFront even if rebuild fails. Risk: Recharts bundle growth as chart count increases. Mitigation: Lighthouse CI JS budget check blocks deploys exceeding 50KB compressed JS. Tradeoff: Static build means no personalization without client-side JS. Accepted for B2B marketing site where personalization is not a requirement. Tradeoff: Formspree dependency for form handling. Mitigation: Formspree endpoint can be swapped for a CloudFront-fronted Lambda URL without frontend changes if volume requires.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Contentful over Sanity for the marketing team's existing familiarity. Confirmed Formspree over self-hosted Lambda form handler to eliminate backend infrastructure. Chose CloudFront OAC over legacy S3 bucket policy for improved security posture. GA4 consent mode v2 selected to maintain measurement continuity for non-consenting EU visitors via modeled data.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving Contentful webhook invalidation flow and CSP allowlist gaps for a B2B food-tech ML forecasting marketing site.

project_goal.value: AI system using ML to forecast daily demand per menu item by location for a large restaurant franchise group, accounting for weather, local events, and historical sales to automate replenishment orders and prep quantity recommendations
project_goal.rationale: Reduces food waste and stockouts at scale across franchise network

target_users.value: Operations managers at franchise locations and corporate supply chain team
target_users.rationale: Primary users of forecast dashboards and replenishment approval workflow

project_class.rationale: Core value is ML-based demand forecasting with automated recommendation generation

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, batch_jobs, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI backend, ClickHouse warehouse, SageMaker ML models, nightly batch pipeline, POS integration, Cognito auth, admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: Per-location per-item models across hundreds of locations, nightly retraining, external signal ingestion

risk_level.rationale: Internal business data; incorrect forecasts have financial impact but no safety or regulatory risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sales data and demand forecasts are proprietary business intelligence

external_exposure.rationale: System accessible only to authenticated restaurant group staff; no public or partner access

access_model.value: Cognito with RBAC: location_manager (view forecasts for assigned location, approve replenishment), corporate_analyst (cross-location forecast review), supply_chain_admin (replenishment config, model retraining triggers)
access_model.rationale: Location-scoped access with corporate overlay

feature_scope.value: Nightly per-location per-item demand forecast generation, weather and local event signal enrichment, forecast accuracy dashboard, one-click replenishment order approval converting AI recommendations to purchase orders, prep quantity daily briefing per kitchen station
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope for demand-driven operations management

mvp_scope.value: Nightly batch forecast for top 50 menu items at pilot 20 locations, basic forecast dashboard, replenishment recommendation display
mvp_scope.rationale: Pilot scope to validate forecast accuracy before full rollout

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA for all roles, TLS 1.3, IAM roles for SageMaker and ClickHouse access, no raw POS transaction data exposed via API
security_baseline.rationale: Internal system with sensitive business data requiring controlled access

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript on ECS Fargate
frontend_stack.rationale: React dashboard for forecast visualization and replenishment approval

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI on ECS Fargate
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for REST API; Python ecosystem for SageMaker model integration

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for time-series sales analytics warehouse; S3 for model artifacts and raw POS ingestion; Redis 7 for forecast cache
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar storage for fast OLAP queries over millions of sales records per day

hosting_target.value: AWS SageMaker for model training and batch inference; ECS Fargate for FastAPI and React; ClickHouse on EC2
hosting_target.rationale: SageMaker managed ML lifecycle; ECS for application services

privacy_retention_policy.value: Daily sales data retained 3 years in ClickHouse for model training; forecast outputs retained 6 months; model artifacts retained for 5 most recent model versions per location-item pair
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 3-year historical data required for seasonal demand pattern capture

future_scope.value: Real-time intraday forecast updates, catering and event demand spikes, franchisee-level model customization
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 forecast fidelity improvements

constraints.value: Nightly batch must complete by 5am local time for each location cluster; SageMaker training budget under $3000/month; models must be interpretable enough for operations managers to understand why a forecast changed
constraints.rationale: 5am deadline enables morning prep briefings before kitchen opens

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for FastAPI, custom metrics for forecast accuracy (MAPE per item per location), SageMaker training job monitoring, PagerDuty alerts on batch job failure or forecast accuracy degradation
observability_baseline.rationale: Forecast accuracy degradation is a leading indicator of operational problems

execution_preference.value: Nightly batch pipeline with EventBridge scheduler; SageMaker Pipelines for model training orchestration
execution_preference.rationale: Managed AWS orchestration for reliable nightly cadence

compliance_context.value: No specific regulatory compliance; internal data governance policy for proprietary sales data access controls and audit trail of who accessed forecast and replenishment data
compliance_context.rationale: Internal governance for proprietary business intelligence

llm_integration.value: LightGBM gradient boosting models per location-item pair for demand forecasting; Prophet for seasonal decomposition baseline; feature engineering from weather API (OpenWeatherMap) and local events API (PredictHQ); no external LLM API -- models trained and hosted on SageMaker
llm_integration.rationale: Gradient boosting outperforms LLMs for tabular time-series forecasting; keeps model costs predictable and data internal

project.goal: Nightly AI demand forecasting with replenishment recommendations for franchise restaurant group
project.target_users: Location ops managers, corporate analysts, supply chain admins
project.timeline: MVP pilot at 20 locations in 16 weeks

frontend.state: TanStack Query for forecast data, Recharts for time-series visualizations

security.auth: Cognito user pools with MFA
security.iam: SageMaker and ClickHouse access via IAM roles (no static credentials)

data.warehouse: ClickHouse on EC2 c6a.4xlarge cluster (3 nodes)
data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache for forecast response cache

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: The replenishment approval workflow is the highest-value touch point -- managers need to trust the recommendation before clicking approve. Forecast explainability is critical: each recommendation should show the key drivers (e.g., 'Saturday + local sports event + weather 85F = +34% predicted demand for chicken sandwiches'). The morning prep briefing delivered by 5am must cover the top 10 high-variance items per kitchen station.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: LightGBM per location-item pair is the right approach -- thousands of lightweight models rather than one large model. SageMaker Pipelines can orchestrate the nightly training and batch inference steps. ClickHouse columnar queries over daily sales aggregates will be fast enough for the dashboard. The feature engineering pipeline needs to join POS sales history with weather API responses and PredictHQ event data before model training.
reasoner_reviews.security: IAM roles for SageMaker execution and ClickHouse access -- no static credentials. Cognito location-scoped data access: location_manager role JWT must be validated against the location_id in every ClickHouse query executed from the FastAPI backend. Audit log in DynamoDB for every replenishment approval action.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SageMaker training at $3000/month constrains how often models retrain. LightGBM training for 50 items x 200 locations = 10,000 models nightly. Using SageMaker Training Jobs with spot instances reduces cost by ~70%. EventBridge schedule must stagger location cluster batches to complete by 5am across time zones.

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: /forecasts (read forecast data), /replenishment (read recommendations, POST approval), /admin (model retraining, config management). Async ClickHouse queries via clickhouse-driver.
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/forecasts?location_id=X&date=2026-03-25&item_ids=[] returns 7-day demand forecasts with confidence intervals and feature importance top-3 drivers. POST /v1/replenishment/{recommendation_id}/approve triggers PO generation to procurement API.
backend.background_jobs: EventBridge triggers nightly orchestration Lambda at 9pm UTC, which starts SageMaker Pipeline for batch inference and feature enrichment; FastAPI has no background jobs -- all batch work in SageMaker.
backend.llm_integration_design: LightGBM models loaded from S3 model artifacts registry; each model is a location-item pair. Feature vector at inference time: day_of_week, week_number, days_until_next_holiday, weather_temp_forecast, weather_precip_probability, predicted_event_attendance_within_5mi, rolling_7d_avg_sales, rolling_28d_avg_sales, yoy_same_day_sales. SageMaker Batch Transform for nightly inference across all location-item pairs.

data.entities: historical_sales (ClickHouse: date, location_id, item_id, quantity_sold, revenue, day_of_week, week_number -- partitioned by toYYYYMM(date)), demand_forecasts (ClickHouse: forecast_date, location_id, item_id, predicted_quantity FLOAT, confidence_lower FLOAT, confidence_upper FLOAT, model_version, generated_at, top_features_json), replenishment_orders (PostgreSQL: id, location_id, recommended_quantity, approved_quantity, status ENUM draft/approved/transmitted, approved_by, approved_at, forecast_id FK), forecast_accuracy_metrics (ClickHouse: date, location_id, item_id, mape FLOAT, mae FLOAT, model_version, computed_at), external_signals (S3 + ClickHouse: date, location_id, weather_temp, weather_precip, event_name, event_attendance_estimate, signal_source)
data.storage_design: ClickHouse 3-node EC2 cluster for sales analytics and forecast storage; S3 for POS CSV ingestion, model artifacts, and external signal raw data; PostgreSQL on RDS for transactional replenishment records; Redis for forecast API cache

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: FastAPI service (2-4 tasks), React frontend (2 tasks); SageMaker Pipelines for nightly training and inference; ClickHouse EC2 cluster (c6a.4xlarge x3); RDS PostgreSQL for replenishment; EventBridge for scheduling; S3 for artifacts.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. SageMaker Pipeline definition versioned in repository; pipeline updates deployed separately via AWS CLI.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on FastAPI; custom metrics: forecast_mape_by_item (from nightly accuracy computation), batch_job_duration_minutes, replenishment_approval_rate; PagerDuty on batch job failure or MAPE >25% degradation on any item

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a high-scale AI demand forecasting system for a restaurant franchise group. Key decisions: LightGBM per location-item pair (interpretable, fast to train in parallel on spot instances) over a single global model; SageMaker Pipelines for nightly orchestration within the $3000/month training budget; ClickHouse as the OLAP warehouse for fast multi-dimensional sales queries. The replenishment approval workflow needs SHAP-based feature importance drivers for manager trust. Forecast accuracy (MAPE) as a first-class observable is critical to detect model drift before it causes operational problems at franchise scale.
target_output.title: Restaurant Franchise Demand Forecasting and Replenishment AI System
target_output.executive_summary: An AWS-hosted AI system generating nightly per-location per-item demand forecasts for a large restaurant franchise group using LightGBM gradient boosting models enriched with weather and local event signals via OpenWeatherMap and PredictHQ APIs. SageMaker Pipelines orchestrate nightly feature engineering, model training on spot instances, and batch inference, completing before 5am for morning prep briefings. FastAPI serves forecast and replenishment recommendation data to a React 18 dashboard; operations managers one-click approve AI-generated replenishment orders transmitted to the procurement system. ClickHouse stores 3 years of daily sales data for model training and OLAP analytics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Nightly EventBridge trigger at 9pm UTC starts a SageMaker Pipeline that ingests POS sales CSVs from S3, enriches them with OpenWeatherMap 7-day forecasts and PredictHQ event data for each location, builds feature matrices per location-item pair, and runs LightGBM training jobs on SageMaker spot instances (10,000 models for 50 items x 200 locations). Model artifacts stored in S3 with version tagging. A second SageMaker Batch Transform job runs inference across all location-item pairs, writing 7-day demand_forecast records with confidence intervals and top-3 SHAP-derived feature importance drivers to ClickHouse. By 5am local time, FastAPI reads from ClickHouse (Redis-cached for 1 hour) to serve forecast dashboards and replenishment recommendations. React 18 dashboard displays Recharts time-series forecasts and a replenishment approval queue; approved orders POST to the procurement system API. Cognito RBAC with location_id scoping protects all data access paths.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, Recharts for time-series visualization, TanStack Query. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, clickhouse-driver async, Pydantic v2. ML: LightGBM 4.x per location-item pair, Prophet for seasonal baseline features, SHAP for feature importance. Data: ClickHouse EC2 cluster (3x c6a.4xlarge), PostgreSQL 16 RDS for replenishment records, S3 for POS ingestion and model artifacts, Redis 7 ElastiCache. Orchestration: SageMaker Pipelines, EventBridge. External: OpenWeatherMap Forecast API, PredictHQ Events API. Auth: Cognito MFA. Monitoring: Datadog APM, custom MAPE metrics. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Nightly Demand Forecast Pipeline: ingests POS daily sales CSVs from S3, enriches with 7-day weather forecasts and local event attendance estimates per location, trains LightGBM on rolling 365-day history, runs batch inference generating 7-day demand forecast with confidence bands and SHAP top-3 feature importance per item. Forecast Dashboard: location manager views item-level 7-day demand charts with confidence intervals and feature importance panel showing top drivers. Replenishment Recommendation Queue: AI-generated recommended order quantities with predicted demand basis and feature drivers; one-click approve converts to purchase order. Prep Quantity Briefing: 5am morning summary per kitchen station of top 10 high-variance items with recommended prep quantities. Forecast Accuracy Dashboard: corporate analyst view of 30-day rolling MAPE by location and item with model drift detection.
target_output.system_components: EventBridge rule (nightly 9pm UTC), SageMaker Pipeline (POS ingestion, external signal enrichment, LightGBM training on spot instances, batch inference steps), S3 (POS CSV landing zone, model artifacts), ClickHouse EC2 cluster (historical_sales, demand_forecasts, external_signals, forecast_accuracy_metrics), RDS PostgreSQL (replenishment_orders), FastAPI ECS Fargate service (forecast, replenishment, admin routers), React 18 ECS Fargate frontend, Redis ElastiCache (forecast response cache 1-hour TTL), Cognito user pool (location-scoped JWT), Datadog APM + custom metrics.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Forecast Pipeline: EventBridge triggers SageMaker Pipeline at 9pm UTC. POS Ingestion step copies franchise CSV files from S3 drop zones, validates row counts. External Signal Enrichment step calls OpenWeatherMap and PredictHQ for all active locations, writes enriched features to ClickHouse external_signals. Model Training step fans out LightGBM Training Jobs across location-item pairs on ml.m5.large spot instances with S3 checkpoint support; each job trains on 365-day rolling feature matrix and saves artifact to S3 model_artifacts/{location_id}/{item_id}/{version}. Batch Inference step runs SageMaker Batch Transform reading model artifacts, writes demand_forecast records with SHAP top_features_json to ClickHouse. Pipeline completes by 4:30am UTC. Replenishment Approval: Manager opens approval queue at 5am showing items with predicted demand delta and recommended quantities. Feature importance panel shows driver explanation. Clicks Approve; FastAPI POSTs to procurement API; replenishment_orders record set to transmitted.
target_output.data_model: historical_sales (ClickHouse: date DATE, location_id LowCardinality(String), item_id LowCardinality(String), quantity_sold UInt32, revenue Decimal(10,2), day_of_week UInt8, week_number UInt8 -- ENGINE MergeTree() PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date) ORDER BY (location_id, item_id, date)). demand_forecasts (ClickHouse: forecast_date DATE, location_id LowCardinality(String), item_id LowCardinality(String), predicted_quantity Float32, confidence_lower Float32, confidence_upper Float32, model_version String, top_features_json String, generated_at DateTime -- ENGINE MergeTree() ORDER BY (location_id, item_id, forecast_date)). replenishment_orders (PostgreSQL: id UUID PK, location_id, item_id, forecast_id, recommended_quantity INT, approved_quantity INT NULLABLE, status ENUM draft/approved/transmitted/cancelled, approved_by_user_id, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, po_reference, created_at). forecast_accuracy_metrics (ClickHouse: computed_date DATE, location_id, item_id, mape FLOAT, mae FLOAT, model_version). external_signals (ClickHouse: signal_date DATE, location_id, weather_temp_f FLOAT, weather_precip_probability FLOAT, event_name String, event_attendance_estimate UInt32).
target_output.api_design: All routes /v1/, Cognito JWT bearer, location_id scoped. GET /v1/forecasts?location_id=X&start_date=2026-03-25&end_date=2026-04-01 returns {items: [{item_id, item_name, forecasts: [{date, predicted_quantity, confidence_lower, confidence_upper, top_features: [{feature, contribution_pct}]}]}], model_version, generated_at}. GET /v1/replenishment?location_id=X&date=2026-03-25 returns {recommendations: [{item_id, recommended_quantity, forecast_predicted, vs_last_week_delta_pct, top_drivers}]}. POST /v1/replenishment/{id}/approve {approved_quantity}. GET /v1/accuracy?location_id=X&item_id=Y&period=30d. GET /v1/prep-briefing?location_id=X&station=grill. POST /v1/admin/pipeline/trigger {location_ids: []}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA enforced. location_manager JWT claim location_id validated in FastAPI dependency for all ClickHouse queries. IAM roles for ECS task and SageMaker execution -- no static AWS credentials. Audit log in DynamoDB for every replenishment approval action (user_id, location_id, item_id, approved_quantity, timestamp). No raw POS transaction-level data exposed via API -- only aggregated daily sales totals. Model artifacts versioned in S3 with 5-version retention per privacy_retention_policy. SageMaker training jobs run in VPC private subnet with S3 VPC endpoint.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest (unit tests for FastAPI routes and feature engineering), Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. SageMaker Pipeline definition in sagemaker_pipeline.py versioned in repository; pipeline ARN stored in SSM Parameter Store. EventBridge rules created via Terraform per location cluster time zone group. ClickHouse EC2 cluster with ZooKeeper coordination; daily backups to S3. SageMaker spot instance interruption handling with S3 checkpointing. AWS Budgets alarm for SageMaker spend >$3000/month.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI with route tracing. Custom metrics: forecast_mape per item (daily accuracy), batch_pipeline_duration_minutes, replenishment_approval_rate, procurement_transmission_success_rate. ClickHouse 30-day rolling MAPE surfaced in Datadog dashboard. PagerDuty: SageMaker Pipeline failure, batch duration >300 minutes, MAPE >25% for any high-volume item for 3 consecutive days, AWS Budgets SageMaker spend >$2500/month.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SageMaker spot instances ~$1800/month for 10,000 nightly training jobs (ml.m5.large spot ~$0.018/hour). SageMaker Batch Transform ~$400/month. ClickHouse EC2 c6a.4xlarge x3 reserved ~$800/month. FastAPI + React ECS ~$150/month. Total ~$3150/month including Datadog. Scales to 500 locations with increased SageMaker spot parallelism; ClickHouse scales horizontally by adding shards.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): LightGBM pipeline for top 50 items at 20 pilot locations, weather and PredictHQ enrichment, SageMaker Pipelines nightly orchestration, ClickHouse historical_sales and demand_forecasts, FastAPI forecast and replenishment endpoints, React dashboard with Recharts and approval queue, Cognito auth, Datadog MAPE metrics, EventBridge scheduling. Phase 2 Rollout (8 weeks): Expand to 200 locations and full menu items, forecast accuracy dashboard, prep briefing API, procurement system API integration. Phase 3 Advanced (8 weeks): Real-time intraday forecast updates, catering event spike detection, franchisee-level model customization.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: black + isort + mypy strict in CI. FastAPI endpoints return typed Pydantic v2 response models. LightGBM training tested with synthetic 30-day sales fixtures -- no live SageMaker calls in unit tests. ClickHouse queries parameterized via clickhouse-driver execute() -- no f-string query construction. SageMaker Pipeline steps defined as Python functions. Model artifact version format: {location_id}/{item_id}/{YYYYMMDD}_{pipeline_run_id} -- never overwrite existing artifacts.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SageMaker spot interruptions delay nightly batch past 5am. Mitigation: S3 checkpoint every 50 epochs; interrupted jobs resume automatically; 30-minute buffer in schedule. Risk: OpenWeatherMap or PredictHQ API outages cause missing features. Mitigation: feature imputation fills missing weather with 7-day rolling average; missing event data treated as zero-event day. Tradeoff: per-location-item LightGBM models over a single global model -- location-specific seasonality captured at higher training cost. Risk: ClickHouse EC2 management burden versus ClickHouse Cloud. Mitigation: daily backups to S3; EC2 auto-recovery enabled. Tradeoff: SHAP over gain-based importance -- more expensive per inference but required for manager-facing explainability.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: LightGBM confirmed over XGBoost for faster categorical feature handling. SHAP confirmed for per-prediction driver explanations in replenishment recommendations. ClickHouse over BigQuery for AWS data residency. SageMaker spot instances with checkpointing for $3000/month budget compliance. OpenWeatherMap One Call API 3.0 confirmed for 7-day forecasts at per-call pricing.

metadata.notes: High-scale LightGBM demand forecasting with SageMaker Pipelines, ClickHouse warehouse, and SHAP-based replenishment recommendations for restaurant franchise group.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for catering company event managers to plan and cost custom event menus by dragging ingredients into recipe cards, calculating per-head costs with live ingredient price lookups, generating client proposal PDFs, and tracking proposal acceptance status
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet-based menu costing with a specialized desktop tool

target_users.value: Catering company event managers who build menus and proposals for clients
target_users.rationale: Single user persona -- internal staff at catering company

project_class.rationale: Self-contained desktop application with local SQLite storage -- no server required

capabilities.rationale: Tauri React frontend, Rust backend commands, SQLite local data -- no auth or payment capabilities needed

complexity_level.rationale: Drag-and-drop menu builder, per-head cost calculation, PDF generation, price list import

risk_level.rationale: Local desktop app with no network exposure; internal business data only

data_sensitivity.rationale: Client names and event details are company business data; no regulated personal data

external_exposure.rationale: Fully local desktop application; no network endpoints exposed

access_model.value: Local user accounts managed by the application; no external identity provider
access_model.rationale: Desktop app used by a small team -- local account management is sufficient

feature_scope.value: Drag-and-drop ingredient catalog to recipe cards, per-head cost calculation with ingredient price history, supplier price sheet CSV import, PDF proposal generation with branded template, proposal status tracking (sent/accepted/declined/pending)
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set for event menu planning workflow

mvp_scope.value: Recipe card builder with manual ingredient entry, per-head cost calculation, basic PDF proposal output, proposal status tracker
mvp_scope.rationale: Core workflow for immediate use -- price import and drag-and-drop polish in Phase 2

security_baseline.value: Local user accounts with bcrypt password hashing; SQLite file stored in OS app data directory with OS-level access controls; no network exposure
security_baseline.rationale: Desktop app minimum security for multi-user workstation use

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 with React 18 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native desktop shell with React UI; smaller bundle than Electron

backend_stack.value: Rust with Tauri backend commands (tauri::command)
backend_stack.rationale: Rust Tauri commands handle SQLite queries and PDF generation via backend commands invoked from React

data_platform.value: SQLite via rusqlite in Tauri backend
data_platform.rationale: Embedded SQLite suits single-user desktop data persistence without a server

hosting_target.value: Desktop installer for Windows and macOS via Tauri bundler
hosting_target.rationale: Tauri produces .msi and .dmg installers via GitHub Actions build

privacy_retention_policy.value: All data stored locally by the user; no retention policy enforced by the application; client data managed per company policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local desktop app -- data lifecycle managed by the company, not the application

future_scope.value: Supplier price sheet CSV import for live price updates, drag-and-drop ingredient catalog UI polish, cloud sync for multi-workstation teams
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 feature polish and team collaboration capabilities

constraints.value: Must run offline on Windows 10+ and macOS 12+; PDF generation must not require internet; installer under 50MB
constraints.rationale: Catering company workstations have inconsistent internet access

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for Rust panics and React errors; local error log file in app data directory
observability_baseline.rationale: Basic crash reporting for a desktop application

execution_preference.value: Ship MVP installer builds via GitHub Actions; test on both Windows and macOS runner environments
execution_preference.rationale: CI-built installers ensure cross-platform compatibility

project.goal: Desktop menu planning and cost calculation tool for catering event managers
project.target_users: Catering event managers (internal)

security.auth: Local bcrypt password file
security.storage: SQLite in OS app data dir

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with windows-latest and macos-latest runners

reasoner_reviews.product: The per-head cost calculation must update in real time as ingredients are added or quantities changed. The proposal PDF should include the full menu with per-dish descriptions, per-head price, and catering company branding. The proposal status board needs to show total pipeline value by status so managers can see their proposal pipeline at a glance.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri 2 invoke() from React to Rust commands is the correct pattern for all data access. SQLite via rusqlite with rusqlite_migration handles schema evolution cleanly. PDF generation with printpdf crate should write to a temp file, then invoke the OS file open dialog via Tauri shell API. Drag-and-drop with dnd-kit is well-supported in React for the ingredient catalog to recipe card UX.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local user accounts with bcrypt hashing via bcrypt crate in Rust, password hash stored in local_users SQLite table. For a small catering team this is adequate -- no full auth system needed. Sentry DSN configured at build time in the Tauri app.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: printpdf crate operates fully offline -- no internet dependency. Tauri bundler with Windows NSIS and macOS .dmg targets from GitHub Actions runners. Total bundle with Tauri 2 + React + Tailwind under 20MB, well within 50MB constraint.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2 React app with React Router: /login, /dashboard (proposal list with status), /proposals/:id (proposal builder and menu editor), /ingredients (catalog management), /settings (user accounts, branding)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Proposal Builder: left panel shows ingredient catalog grouped by category; right panel shows recipe cards for each course; drag ingredient from catalog onto recipe card adds it with default quantity; per-head cost updates in real time via Tauri invoke('calculate_proposal_cost'). Proposal Dashboard: list proposals with status badge, total value, event date, client name.
frontend.state_management: React useState for proposal builder local state; TanStack Query for SQLite-backed data fetching via Tauri commands; dnd-kit DndContext wrapping ingredient catalog and recipe card drop targets

backend.service_design: Tauri Rust commands: get_proposals, get_proposal, create_proposal, update_proposal_status, get_ingredient_catalog, add_ingredient_to_recipe, calculate_proposal_cost, generate_proposal_pdf, import_price_sheet, authenticate_user
backend.api_patterns: All data access via Tauri invoke() IPC. SQLite accessed only from Rust Tauri commands, never from React directly. PDF generation returns temp file path; React opens via shell.open().
backend.failure_handling: Rust Tauri commands return Result<T, String> -- errors surfaced to React as toast notifications

data.entities: event_proposals (id INTEGER PK, client_name TEXT, event_name TEXT, event_date TEXT, guest_count INTEGER, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('pending','sent','accepted','declined')), total_cost_per_head REAL, created_at TEXT). custom_menus (id INTEGER PK, proposal_id INTEGER FK, course_name TEXT, display_order INTEGER). recipe_cards (id INTEGER PK, menu_id INTEGER FK, dish_name TEXT, description TEXT). recipe_ingredients (id INTEGER PK, recipe_card_id INTEGER FK, ingredient_id INTEGER FK, quantity_per_serving REAL, unit TEXT). ingredient_catalog (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT, category TEXT, unit TEXT, current_price_per_unit REAL, last_price_updated TEXT). ingredient_prices (id INTEGER PK, ingredient_id INTEGER FK, price_per_unit REAL, effective_date TEXT, source TEXT). client_contacts (id INTEGER PK, client_name TEXT, email TEXT, phone TEXT). local_users (id INTEGER PK, username TEXT UNIQUE, password_hash TEXT, role TEXT)
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file at OS app data path (AppData/Roaming on Windows, ~/Library/Application Support on macOS). rusqlite_migration for schema versioning.

devops.deployment_topology: Local desktop application; no server infrastructure. GitHub Actions CI with windows-latest and macos-latest runners.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo test, vitest for React components, tauri build on Windows runner (produces .msi), tauri build on macOS runner (produces .dmg). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases.
devops.rollback_strategy: Users download prior GitHub Release installer; SQLite migrations are additive-only in Phase 1 to support rollback

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a self-contained Tauri 2 desktop app for catering event menu costing. Key architectural decisions: all data access via Tauri invoke() IPC from React to Rust commands (never direct SQLite from React), printpdf crate for fully offline PDF generation, dnd-kit for ingredient drag-and-drop in Phase 2. The data model captures the proposal-menu-course-recipe-ingredient hierarchy. Per-head cost is a Rust command that queries the ingredient price and recipe quantities. The app is local_only and low-risk, appropriate for a weak-quality training sample.
target_output.title: Catering Event Menu Planner Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 desktop application for catering event managers to plan custom event menus, calculate per-head food costs, and generate client proposal PDFs offline. Rust Tauri commands handle SQLite reads and writes via rusqlite, and PDF generation via printpdf crate. Proposal lifecycle tracked as pending, sent, accepted, or declined. Installs on Windows 10+ and macOS 12+.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 application with React 18 TypeScript frontend communicating with a Rust backend via Tauri invoke() IPC. All business logic runs in Rust Tauri commands. React handles the UI with dnd-kit drag-and-drop for ingredient assignment and TanStack Query for Tauri command data fetching. SQLite via rusqlite stores proposals, recipes, ingredients, and clients in a single file at the OS app data path. PDF proposals generated via printpdf crate write to a temp file opened by Tauri shell.open(). Sentry captures Rust panics and React errors.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Tauri 2, Rust 1.78, React 18 TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, dnd-kit, TanStack Query. Rust crates: rusqlite, rusqlite_migration, printpdf, bcrypt, serde_json. Build: GitHub Actions tauri-action on windows-latest and macos-latest. Crash reporting: Sentry. Installers: .msi (Windows NSIS) and .dmg (macOS). Tauri updater plugin configured against GitHub Releases API for silent background update checks on app launch. vitest with @testing-library/react for drag-and-drop interaction tests using dnd-kit's test utilities.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Ingredient Catalog: browse ingredients by category, add with price per unit, CSV price sheet import. Recipe Builder: course cards (appetizers, mains, desserts), drag ingredients onto cards, set quantity per serving, real-time per-head cost recalculation on every change. Per-Head Cost: Rust command sums recipe_ingredients.quantity_per_serving * ingredient_catalog.current_price_per_unit divided by guest_count. Proposal Dashboard: list proposals with status badges and total pipeline value. PDF Generation: printpdf crate renders branded proposal with event details, menu courses, per-head price, company logo.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2 shell (WebView2 on Windows, WKWebView on macOS), React 18 SPA (Vite-compiled, embedded in Tauri), dnd-kit DndContext, TanStack Query (Tauri invoke-based), Rust Tauri command handlers (proposal CRUD, cost calculation, PDF rendering, price import, auth), rusqlite connection (SQLite at OS app data path), rusqlite_migration, printpdf crate, bcrypt crate, Sentry SDK.
target_output.workflows: Menu Costing: Event manager creates proposal with guest count. Adds ingredients to course cards via drag-drop. Each addition calls invoke('add_ingredient_to_recipe') then invoke('calculate_proposal_cost'). Rust queries recipe_ingredients JOIN ingredient_catalog, computes SUM(quantity * price) / guest_count, returns per_head_cost. React updates cost display. PDF Generation: Manager clicks Generate Proposal. React calls invoke('generate_proposal_pdf'). Rust queries full proposal data, renders with printpdf (company logo header, event details, course sections with dish names and descriptions, per-head price). Writes to OS temp dir. Returns temp path. React calls shell.open(path) to open in OS PDF viewer.
target_output.data_model: event_proposals (id INTEGER PK, client_name TEXT NOT NULL, event_name TEXT, event_date TEXT, guest_count INTEGER NOT NULL, status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(status IN ('pending','sent','accepted','declined')), total_cost_per_head REAL, created_at TEXT). custom_menus (id INTEGER PK, proposal_id FK, course_name TEXT, display_order INTEGER). recipe_cards (id INTEGER PK, menu_id FK, dish_name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT). recipe_ingredients (id INTEGER PK, recipe_card_id FK, ingredient_id FK, quantity_per_serving REAL NOT NULL, unit TEXT). ingredient_catalog (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT NOT NULL, category TEXT, unit TEXT, current_price_per_unit REAL NOT NULL, last_price_updated TEXT). ingredient_prices (id INTEGER PK, ingredient_id FK, price_per_unit REAL, effective_date TEXT, source TEXT DEFAULT 'manual'). local_users (id INTEGER PK, username TEXT UNIQUE, password_hash TEXT, role TEXT DEFAULT 'manager').
target_output.api_design: All data access via Tauri invoke() IPC -- no HTTP endpoints. Key Rust commands: get_proposals() -> Vec<ProposalSummary>. get_proposal(id: i64) -> ProposalDetail with nested courses and ingredients. create_proposal(client_name, event_name, event_date, guest_count) -> ProposalSummary. update_proposal_status(id: i64, status: String) -> Result. add_ingredient_to_recipe(recipe_card_id, ingredient_id, quantity, unit) -> RecipeIngredient. calculate_proposal_cost(proposal_id) -> CostResult {per_head_cost, total_cost, line_items}. generate_proposal_pdf(proposal_id) -> String (temp file path). authenticate_user(username, password) -> Option<UserSession>. All return Result<T, String>.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Local bcrypt password hashing (cost factor 12) in local_users table. Auth state held in Tauri application state. SQLite at OS app data path protected by OS user permissions. No network interfaces. Sentry DSN build-time constant. Client contact data is internal business data with no GDPR obligations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: cargo test, vitest, cargo clippy --deny warnings, tauri build on windows-latest (.msi) and macos-latest (.dmg). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Release on version tag. rusqlite_migration applies schema at app startup. Auto-updater configured via Tauri updater plugin pointing to GitHub Releases. Code signing: macOS bundle signed and notarized via Apple Developer certificate stored in GitHub Actions secrets. Windows NSIS installer signed via self-signed Authenticode cert for corporate deployment. Sentry Tauri plugin captures Rust panics and React exceptions with app_version and platform tags for post-release triage.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK captures panics with backtrace. Sentry React ErrorBoundary captures runtime errors. Local log file in app data dir via Tauri log plugin (info/warn/error, 7-day rotation). No remote metrics -- appropriate for a local desktop app.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No ongoing infrastructure cost. GitHub Actions free tier for installer builds. Sentry free tier (5000 errors/month). Total: $0/month. Scales by installing on additional workstations. Phase 2 cloud sync estimated $50-100/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Tauri 2 skeleton with SQLite schema, ingredient catalog CRUD, recipe card builder with manual ingredient entry, calculate_proposal_cost command, PDF generation via printpdf, proposal dashboard with status badges, local auth, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 Polish (4 weeks): dnd-kit drag-and-drop, CSV price sheet import, ingredient price history, branded PDF template with logo. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Cloud sync for multi-workstation teams, Google OAuth replacing local accounts.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: rustfmt + clippy --deny warnings. All Tauri commands return Result<T, String> -- never panic in handlers. SQLite foreign keys enabled with PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON at connection open. Cost calculation logic unit-tested with in-memory rusqlite. printpdf rendering isolated in a proposal_pdf module with a test verifying page count. React: ESLint + TypeScript strict. dnd-kit drop handlers call invoke() immediately with optimistic UI update.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: printpdf limited layout capabilities for complex branded PDFs. Mitigation: simple grid layout for proposals. Risk: rusqlite_migration additive-only constraint in Phase 1. Mitigation: schema reviewed carefully before first release; migration tests in CI. Tradeoff: Tauri 2 over Electron -- smaller bundle and lower memory but smaller ecosystem. Risk: macOS Gatekeeper for unsigned .dmg. Mitigation: Apple Developer Program membership required for production distribution.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Tauri 2 confirmed over Electron for 50MB bundle constraint. printpdf confirmed for offline PDF -- no Chrome headless dependency. dnd-kit confirmed over react-beautiful-dnd (unmaintained). rusqlite_migration confirmed for schema versioning. Local bcrypt auth confirmed -- no external IdP for a small catering team.

metadata.notes: Greenfield Tauri 2 desktop catering menu planner with offline PDF generation -- weak quality reflecting plan_quality distribution.

project_goal.value: Automation tool to modernize hotel chain F&B procurement -- auto-generate purchase orders from par-level triggers, transmit to vendors via EDI or email, and reconcile incoming invoices against expected pricing
project_goal.rationale: Replaces spreadsheet-driven procurement workflow causing frequent pricing discrepancies and missed par triggers

target_users.value: Hotel F&B procurement managers and outlet directors managing purchasing for multiple F&B outlets across a hotel property
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in stakeholder interviews

project_class.rationale: Backend automation pipeline with batch jobs and external EDI/email integrations; no end-user GUI beyond approval workflow notifications

capabilities.rationale: Celery-based batch processing, EDI and email integrations, no frontend required

complexity_level.rationale: EDI parsing, OCR invoice ingestion, price matching logic -- moderate complexity with external integration dependencies

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect purchase orders or missed invoice discrepancies have direct financial impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contains vendor pricing agreements, purchase order amounts, and invoice financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Integrates with external vendor EDI systems and email endpoints

access_model.value: Azure AD authentication for internal procurement managers; vendor interactions via EDI AS2 or SMTP with no inbound authentication from vendors
access_model.rationale: Internal users authenticated via Azure AD; vendor communications are outbound only or validated by EDI transmission ID

feature_scope.value: Par-level trigger monitoring, purchase order generation and manager approval routing, EDI 850 and email order transmission, EDI 855/856 acknowledgment processing, invoice OCR ingestion, invoice-to-PO price reconciliation with discrepancy flagging
feature_scope.rationale: Complete automation workflow from par trigger to invoice reconciliation

mvp_scope.value: Nightly par-check batch job, manager approval email with PO draft, EDI 850 transmission to top 5 vendors, invoice OCR ingestion and price matching
mvp_scope.rationale: High-value automation targeting highest-volume vendor relationships first

security_baseline.value: Azure AD token validation on all internal API endpoints, vendor EDI payloads validated against X12 schema before processing, invoice attachment scanning for malware before OCR, financial data encrypted at rest in PostgreSQL
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data handling and external integration baseline

frontend_stack.value: None -- approval workflows delivered via email with inline action links; no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Automation tool; no GUI required for MVP

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery 5 on Redis 7 as broker
backend_stack.rationale: Celery well-suited for complex multi-stage batch job orchestration with retry policies

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for PO-to-invoice matching joins; managed Azure service for operational simplicity

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container scaling for variable Celery worker load during nightly batch runs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Purchase orders and invoices retained for 7 years per hotel group accounting policies; Celery task logs retained for 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial record retention requirements for hospitality management companies

future_scope.value: Real-time par monitoring (vs nightly batch), multi-property procurement consolidation, vendor self-service invoice portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements after validating core automation workflow

constraints.value: Must integrate with 3 existing EDI-capable vendors and 12 email-only vendors by launch; invoice OCR accuracy must exceed 95% on standard supplier invoice formats
constraints.rationale: Operational constraints from existing vendor relationships

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for Celery worker metrics and batch job success/failure rates; alerting on invoice reconciliation failure rate exceeding 10%
observability_baseline.rationale: Financial workflow monitoring requirements

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; batch job configurations managed via PostgreSQL admin API, not code changes
execution_preference.rationale: Operations team must adjust par thresholds without code deployments

compliance_context.value: Internal financial controls for hospitality management companies; SOX considerations for publicly traded hotel brands require audit trail for all purchase order approvals and invoice reconciliation decisions
compliance_context.rationale: Financial controls and audit trail requirements for public hotel company

project.goal: Automate F&B procurement from par trigger to invoice reconciliation
project.target_users: Hotel F&B procurement managers

backend.api: FastAPI for internal admin and approval callback endpoints

security.auth: Azure AD (MSAL) for internal endpoints
security.edi_validation: X12 schema validation on inbound EDI payloads
security.invoice_scanning: Azure Defender for Storage malware scan before OCR

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

reasoner_reviews.product: The manager approval step is critical to adoption -- procurement managers will not trust an automated PO system without a clear approval gate. The approval email must include a clear PO summary (vendor name, items, quantities, total cost) with a single-click approve/reject link that does not require logging into a portal. The 95% OCR accuracy target for invoices is achievable with Azure Form Recognizer trained on supplier templates but requires initial setup effort.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The previous plan had an intentional flaw: it specified the approval callback endpoint as unauthenticated. This revision secures the approval link with a short-lived HMAC-signed token. The EDI transmission retry logic was underspecified in round 1 -- Celery retry with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue handling must be defined.
reasoner_reviews.security: Approval email links must use short-lived HMAC-signed tokens (not session cookies) so procurement managers can approve from mobile email without logging into a portal. Tokens must expire after 24 hours and be single-use. EDI inbound payloads validated against X12 schema before any database writes.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: EDI AS2 integration with 3 vendors requires AS2 adapter setup. The 12 email-only vendors use SMTP with structured email templates. OCR via Azure Form Recognizer custom model trained on each supplier's invoice template.

backend.service_design: Celery app with task modules: par_monitor (nightly batch), po_generator, approval_router (sends approval email, handles callback), edi_transmitter (EDI 850 via AS2), email_transmitter (SMTP), invoice_ingester, reconciliation_engine.
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI internal API: POST /approvals/callback (HMAC token validation), GET /po/{id}/summary, POST /admin/par-levels.
backend.business_modules: ParMonitor: SQL query comparing inventory levels against par_levels thresholds. ReconciliationEngine: line-item price matching with configurable tolerance (default 2%); discrepancy classification: within_tolerance, price_variance, quantity_mismatch, missing_line_item.
backend.failure_handling: Celery retry with exponential backoff (max 3 retries) for EDI transmission failures. Dead-letter queue for tasks exceeding retry limit. Invoice OCR failures quarantined in manual_review_queue table.

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL for internal FastAPI endpoints. Approval email links use 256-bit HMAC-SHA256 signed tokens with po_id, action (approve/reject), and exp (24 hours). Single-use enforcement: token hash stored in PostgreSQL, rejected on second use.
security.authorization_model: Internal API: Azure AD groups for procurement_manager and admin roles. Approval callback: HMAC token validation only.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for EDI AS2 private key, SMTP credentials, Form Recognizer API key, PostgreSQL connection string. Container Apps managed identity for Key Vault access.
security.security_risks: Email approval links can be forwarded -- mitigated by single-use token enforcement and 24-hour expiry.

data.entities: purchase_orders (id, outlet_id FK, vendor_id FK, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/rejected/transmitted/acknowledged, total_amount_cents, created_at, approved_by_uid, approved_at), po_line_items (id, po_id FK, product_catalog_id FK, quantity, unit_price_agreement_cents, line_total_cents), vendor_contracts (id, vendor_id FK, product_catalog_id FK, agreed_unit_price_cents, effective_from, effective_to), par_levels (id, outlet_id FK, product_catalog_id FK, par_qty, reorder_point_qty, reorder_qty), invoice_reconciliations (id, po_id FK, invoice_number, total_invoiced_cents, status ENUM matched/discrepancy_flagged/manual_review, reconciled_at), manual_review_queue (id, task_id, task_name, failure_reason, payload_json, queued_at, resolved_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with automated daily backups. Azure Blob Storage for raw invoice PDFs (7-year retention). Redis 7 as Celery broker.
data.data_risks: Invoice OCR extraction errors could create false reconciliation matches. ReconciliationEngine flags any reconciliation where OCR confidence score < 0.90 for manual review.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment with three containers: api (FastAPI), celery-worker (Celery concurrency=4), celery-beat (nightly par-check cron). Redis 7 as Azure Cache for Redis.

previous_plan.title: Hotel F&B Procurement Automation Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python Celery automation pipeline for hotel F&B procurement with par-level monitoring, PO generation, EDI/email transmission, and invoice reconciliation. Round 1 plan had unauthenticated approval callback and underspecified EDI retry.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Celery worker processes on Azure Container Apps with nightly par-check batch, manager approval email routing, EDI 850 transmission, and Azure Form Recognizer invoice OCR.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD for internal API. Approval email links [unauthenticated -- flagged]. EDI validated against X12 schema.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps with celery-worker, celery-beat, and api containers. [EDI retry policy not specified]

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a hotel F&B procurement automation tool. Two critical gaps from round 1 must be resolved: the unauthenticated approval callback (now secured with HMAC-SHA256 signed single-use tokens) and the underspecified EDI retry policy (now defined as max 3 retries with exponential backoff, dead-letter queue table, and Azure Monitor alert). The architecture is intentionally flawed: the fix_report addresses both issues but the data_model section omits the approval_tokens table needed for single-use token enforcement.

fix_report.action_taken: Approval callback endpoint now validates HMAC-SHA256 signed tokens embedded in email links. Tokens include po_id, action, and expiry timestamp signed with a secret key from Azure Key Vault. Single-use enforcement via approval_tokens table storing token hash and used_at timestamp.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Approval callback rejects unsigned, expired, or already-used tokens, preventing unauthorized PO approvals.

fix_report.action_taken: EDI transmission Celery tasks now defined with autoretry_for=(EDITransmissionError, ConnectionError), max_retries=3, retry_backoff=True (60s, 120s, 240s). Exhausted retries move task to manual_review_queue table and fire Azure Monitor alert to procurement team Slack channel.
fix_report.expected_outcome: EDI transmission failures are retried with exponential backoff and escalated to manual review if all retries are exhausted.

target_output.title: Hotel F&B Procurement Automation Architecture -- Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Python Celery automation pipeline on Azure Container Apps that generates hotel F&B purchase orders from nightly par-level triggers, routes them for manager approval via HMAC-signed email links, transmits approved orders via EDI 850 or SMTP to vendors, and reconciles incoming invoices against contracted pricing using Azure Form Recognizer OCR. This revision secures the approval callback with signed single-use tokens and defines the EDI retry and dead-letter policy.
target_output.architecture_overview: Celery 5 worker and beat containers on Azure Container Apps consume tasks from Redis 7 broker. Nightly par-check beat task triggers PO draft generation for outlets below par threshold. Approval emails contain HMAC-SHA256 signed links to the FastAPI callback endpoint; tokens are single-use and expire in 24 hours. Approved POs are transmitted via EDI AS2 (3 vendors) or SMTP (12 vendors) with Celery retry up to 3 times on failure, then dead-lettered to manual_review_queue. Invoice PDFs land in Azure Blob Storage, are malware-scanned by Azure Defender, processed by Form Recognizer, and reconciled against PO line items by the ReconciliationEngine with 2% price tolerance.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2, Alembic. EDI: python-x12 for EDI 850/855/856. OCR: Azure Form Recognizer custom model. Integration: AS2 via Mendelson Open AS2, SMTP via smtplib. Auth: MSAL for Azure AD, HMAC-SHA256 for approval tokens. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with Container Apps managed identity. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server. Broker: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Monitoring: Azure Monitor with Log Analytics. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Azure Container Registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Par-Level Monitor: nightly Celery beat task queries inventory_levels vs par_levels, generates draft POs for outlets below reorder point. PO Approval Routing: sends HTML email with HMAC-signed approve/reject links. EDI 850 Transmission: generates X12 EDI 850 and transmits via AS2 to 3 EDI vendors; retries up to 3 times on failure. Email PO Transmission: SMTP to 12 non-EDI vendors. Invoice Ingestion: monitors dedicated SMTP mailbox; extracts PDF attachments to Azure Blob Storage for OCR. Invoice Reconciliation: matches Form Recognizer extracted line items against PO line items with tolerance classification.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps (api, celery-worker, celery-beat with managed identity). Azure Cache for Redis 7 (Celery broker). PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server. Azure Blob Storage (invoice PDF archive). Azure Form Recognizer custom model. Mendelson Open AS2 (EDI transmission). Azure Key Vault (EDI private key, SMTP credentials, Form Recognizer key, HMAC signing key, DB URL). Azure Monitor + Log Analytics. Azure Defender for Storage (invoice malware scanning).
target_output.workflows: Nightly Par-Check: Celery beat fires par_monitor at 23:00 hotel time -- queries par_levels vs inventory_levels -- generates PO drafts -- triggers approval_router. Manager Approval: approval_router generates HMAC-SHA256 token (payload: po_id|action|exp, signed with APPROVAL_HMAC_KEY) -- sends HTML email with approve/reject links -- manager clicks -- POST /approvals/callback?token validates signature, checks expiry, checks single-use -- marks PO approved/rejected -- triggers transmission. EDI Failure and Dead-Letter: edi_transmitter fails -- autoretry: 60s, 120s, 240s -- if all retries exhausted: insert into manual_review_queue -- fire Azure Monitor alert to procurement team Slack.
target_output.data_model: purchase_orders (id UUID PK, outlet_id UUID FK, vendor_id UUID FK, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/rejected/transmitted/acknowledged, total_amount_cents BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, approved_by_uid VARCHAR, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). po_line_items (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, product_catalog_id UUID FK, quantity DECIMAL, unit_price_agreement_cents INTEGER, line_total_cents BIGINT). vendor_contracts (id UUID PK, vendor_id UUID FK, product_catalog_id UUID FK, agreed_unit_price_cents INTEGER, effective_from DATE, effective_to DATE). par_levels (id UUID PK, outlet_id UUID FK, product_catalog_id UUID FK, par_qty DECIMAL, reorder_point_qty DECIMAL, reorder_qty DECIMAL). invoice_reconciliations (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, invoice_number VARCHAR, total_invoiced_cents BIGINT, status ENUM matched/discrepancy_flagged/manual_review, reconciled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). manual_review_queue (id UUID PK, task_id VARCHAR, task_name VARCHAR, failure_reason TEXT, payload_json JSONB, queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Note: approval_tokens table for single-use token enforcement is referenced in security_and_compliance but not shown above -- this is an intentional omission creating an internal inconsistency in this flawed plan.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI internal API: POST /approvals/callback?token={hmac_token} (validates HMAC signature and expiry, marks PO approved or rejected). GET /po/{id}/summary (Azure AD protected). POST /admin/par-levels (Azure AD protected, procurement_manager role). GET /admin/reconciliations?status=discrepancy_flagged (Azure AD protected). POST /admin/manual-review/{id}/resolve (Azure AD protected).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HMAC-SHA256 approval tokens: payload is '{po_id}|{action}|{exp_unix_timestamp}' signed with APPROVAL_HMAC_KEY from Azure Key Vault. Tokens expire after 24 hours. Single-use enforcement via approval_tokens table (token_hash, used_at) -- second use returns 409. Azure AD MSAL token validation on all /admin/* endpoints. EDI AS2 transmission uses AS2 digital signature with vendor public certificate. X12 schema validation on inbound EDI 855/856 before database writes. Invoice PDFs scanned by Azure Defender for Storage before Form Recognizer. SOX audit trail: every PO state change recorded with uid, timestamp, and action in po_audit_events table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: pytest (85% coverage), ruff, mypy -- Docker build -- push to Azure Container Registry -- az containerapp update for rolling deploy. Celery beat schedule: par_monitor at 23:00 hotel timezone, invoice inbox poll every 15 minutes. Container Apps scaling: celery-worker scales 1-5 replicas based on Redis queue depth via KEDA Redis scaler. Rollback: az containerapp revision set-mode previous. Database migrations via Alembic run as Container Apps job before worker deploy.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor custom metrics: Celery task success/failure rates per task name, EDI transmission success rate, invoice OCR confidence score distribution, reconciliation match rate. Alert rules: EDI dead-letter queue depth > 0 (immediate email + Slack), reconciliation discrepancy rate > 10% (daily digest), Celery worker error rate > 5% over 15 minutes (PagerDuty). Log Analytics aggregates Container Apps structured JSON logs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $120/month: Azure Container Apps 3 containers (~$30), Azure Cache for Redis Basic ($20), PostgreSQL Flexible Server Burstable B2ms ($50), Azure Blob Storage ($5), Form Recognizer pay-per-page (~$10), Key Vault ($5). Celery worker KEDA autoscaling handles peak nightly batch load transparently.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): par_monitor nightly batch, PO draft generation, HMAC-signed approval email workflow, EDI 850 to 3 EDI vendors, SMTP to 12 email vendors, invoice inbox monitoring and Form Recognizer OCR, ReconciliationEngine with 2% tolerance, manual_review_queue, SOX audit event table. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Real-time par monitoring, EDI 855/856 acknowledgment processing, admin API for par threshold management. Phase 3 (future): Multi-property procurement consolidation, vendor self-service invoice portal.
target_output.development_guidelines: Celery tasks must be idempotent -- task_id used as idempotency key for PO generation and EDI transmission to prevent duplicate POs on retry. SQLAlchemy 2 session-per-task pattern. HMAC signing key loaded from Azure Key Vault on app startup. All financial amounts stored as integer cents, never floats. pytest fixtures use a PostgreSQL test container with Alembic migrations applied.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Form Recognizer OCR accuracy below 95% for non-standard supplier invoice formats. Mitigation: custom model trained per supplier template; low-confidence extractions automatically routed to manual_review_queue. Risk: Single-use approval token table growth -- monthly cleanup job purges tokens older than 30 days. Tradeoff: Nightly batch par-check accepted over real-time monitoring for MVP to simplify initial delivery.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Azure Form Recognizer over Textract to stay within Azure ecosystem. Confirmed Mendelson Open AS2 over Azure Integration Services B2B for lower cost at 3-vendor volume. HMAC-SHA256 tokens selected over OAuth PKCE flow for approval links to avoid requiring managers to log into a portal from email.
