project_goal.value: CLI tool for culinary data analysts at restaurant chains to export, transform, and analyze historical recipe cost data -- pulling from recipe management database, applying ingredient price history, and outputting menu engineering reports (contribution margin, popularity-profitability matrices) as CSV or Excel files
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual SQL exports and spreadsheet manipulation that takes analysts 4-6 hours per menu engineering review cycle

target_users.value: Culinary data analysts and menu engineering managers at restaurant chains running periodic menu cost reviews
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in analyst workflow interviews

project_class.rationale: Command-line tool for data analysts; no browser UI; outputs files for further analysis

capabilities.rationale: Python CLI with PostgreSQL data access and report generation; no frontend or auth system

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-date-range cost computation across recipe and price history tables; batch parallel processing for multiple menu versions

risk_level.rationale: Internal data analysis tool; incorrect output is caught in analyst review, not a production system

data_sensitivity.rationale: Recipe cost data is internal business data; not personal or financial in the PCI/GDPR sense

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs on analyst laptops connected to internal corporate network; PostgreSQL accessed via VPN

access_model.value: Custom JWT for database access; analysts authenticate with corporate credentials to obtain a JWT from an internal auth service, passed to the CLI via environment variable
access_model.rationale: Centralized JWT authentication for internal tooling aligned with corporate IAM policy

feature_scope.value: Single menu engineering report command, batch multi-version comparison command, CSV and Excel output formats, configurable date range and menu category filters, cost drift threshold flagging
feature_scope.rationale: Complete analyst workflow feature set

mvp_scope.value: Single report command with date range and category filter, CSV output, top-5 highest food cost percentage items flagged
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum feature set that saves analysts the most time

security_baseline.value: JWT from environment variable only (no CLI flag), TLS for PostgreSQL connection, no recipe cost data written to disk except output files, no credentials in CLI output
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool security baseline

frontend_stack.value: None -- CLI tool
frontend_stack.rationale: CLI architecture; no browser UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click CLI framework
backend_stack.rationale: Click provides clean command/option parsing and testing utilities for Python CLIs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on GCP Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Existing recipe management database on GCP Cloud SQL; CLI reads from read-replica

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run job-based for scheduled automated reports; also runnable locally by analysts
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run jobs for automated weekly menu engineering reports; local execution for ad-hoc analysis

privacy_retention_policy.value: Output CSV and Excel files are analyst-managed; CLI does not retain copies; no data written to disk beyond the specified output path
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Analyst manages output file retention; CLI is a pass-through transformation tool

future_scope.value: Dashboard integration pushing report data to a shared analytics platform, recipe cost simulation mode with what-if ingredient price scenarios
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP analytics extensions

constraints.value: Single menu category report must complete in under 30 seconds for any date range up to 2 years; batch command must process up to 10 menu versions in parallel; output Excel files must be compatible with Microsoft Excel 2016+
constraints.rationale: Performance constraints from analyst workflow requirements

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run job execution metrics; local stderr logging for analyst debugging; structured JSON log for Cloud Run execution
observability_baseline.rationale: Cloud Run job success/failure monitoring and local analyst debugging

execution_preference.value: pip-installable Python package via internal PyPI; GitLab CI for package build and Cloud Run job container
execution_preference.rationale: Internal PyPI for analyst install; GitLab CI matches GCP toolchain

project.goal: CLI for recipe cost analysis and menu engineering reports from recipe management database
project.target_users: Culinary data analysts

security.auth: JWT from RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT env var
security.db_connection: TLS required, Cloud SQL IAM authentication

reasoner_reviews.product: The menu engineering matrix output is the highest-value feature -- analysts want the popularity-profitability matrix (four-quadrant: Stars/Plowhorses/Puzzles/Dogs based on contribution margin vs sales mix percentage). The report must use the actual terminology menu engineering practitioners use. Round 1 left the matrix quadrant classification logic underspecified.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The food cost percentage computation requires joining recipes, recipe_ingredients, ingredient_price_history, and sales_mix_data. Key gap from round 1: the batch command used sequential processing instead of concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor -- the 10-version parallel constraint was missed. This revision must fix both the matrix quadrant logic and the parallel batch processing.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT read from RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT environment variable only -- Click should check for this env var in the command setup and raise a UsageError if absent. GCP Cloud SQL IAM authentication for Cloud Run job execution.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 30-second constraint for single-category 2-year range: the SQL query must use a single CTE joining ingredient_price_history using a nearest-price lookup (DISTINCT ON ordered by effective_date DESC) rather than a subquery per ingredient.

backend.service_design: Click group with commands: report (single menu engineering report), batch (multi-version parallel comparison), validate-connection. Shared services: RecipeCostQueryService, MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier, ReportFormatter.
backend.api_patterns: Click command options: --date-from, --date-to, --category (multiple), --output-path, --format (csv|excel), --flag-threshold. Batch: --versions (list), --concurrency (default 4, max 10).
backend.business_modules: RecipeCostQueryService: PostgreSQL CTE-based query with DISTINCT ON nearest price. MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier: classifies Star (high margin, high mix), Plowhorse (low margin, high mix), Puzzle (high margin, low mix), Dog (low margin, low mix). ReportFormatter: CSV or color-coded Excel via openpyxl.
backend.failure_handling: psycopg2.OperationalError: CLI prints clear error and exits code 1. JWT expiry: 'JWT expired -- re-run with refreshed RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT token'. Query timeout (30s): exits code 2.

security.auth_design: JWT read from os.environ['RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT'] in Click command setup. Absent env var: Click UsageError. JWT validated locally against public key. Cloud Run job: service account IAM auth, no JWT required.
security.secrets_management: DATABASE_URL from RECIPE_DB_URL env var. Cloud Run job: credentials from Secret Manager.
security.security_risks: Output Excel files may be copied to personal cloud storage by analysts. Mitigation: output path validation warns if writing to non-corporate paths.

data.entities: recipes (id, name, category, current_version_id FK), recipe_ingredients (id, recipe_id FK, ingredient_id FK, quantity_grams), ingredients (id, name, unit), ingredient_price_history (id, ingredient_id FK, price_per_unit_cents, effective_date, supplier_id), menu_sections (id, name, menu_version_id FK), sales_mix_data (id, recipe_id FK, menu_version_id FK, period_start, period_end, covers_sold, total_revenue_cents), cost_analysis_reports (id, generated_by_uid, generation_params_json, generated_at) -- audit log only
data.storage_design: Read-only access to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 read-replica. Output files written to analyst-specified output path only.
data.data_risks: ingredient_price_history gaps cause NULL food cost for affected recipes. Mitigation: COALESCE to last known price before range start; flag recipes with price_estimated column.

devops.deployment_topology: Python package menu-engineer-cli distributed via internal PyPI (GitLab Package Registry). Cloud Run job: Docker image with Python 3.12 + menu-engineer-cli, Cloud Run job defined in Terraform.
devops.environments: Local: analyst installs via pip install from internal PyPI. Cloud Run: job triggered by Cloud Scheduler weekly.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: ruff -- mypy -- pytest (85% coverage) -- python -m build -- publish to GitLab Package Registry on tag. Docker build + push to Artifact Registry for Cloud Run job.
devops.devops_risks: Internal PyPI certificate chain may block pip install on analyst laptops with strict corporate TLS inspection.

previous_plan.title: Recipe Cost Analysis CLI Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python Click CLI for restaurant recipe cost analysis and menu engineering reports from PostgreSQL. Single report and batch multi-version comparison commands. CSV and Excel output. Round 1 had sequential batch processing and unspecified matrix quadrant logic.
previous_plan.workflows: report command: queries recipe cost data for date range and category, computes food cost pct, generates CSV/Excel. batch command: [sequential for loop over versions -- ISS-001]. MenuEngineeringMatrix: [quadrant logic placeholder -- ISS-002]

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the recipe cost analysis CLI. Two gaps from round 1: the batch command was sequential instead of parallel (fixed with ThreadPoolExecutor up to --concurrency workers), and the menu engineering matrix quadrant classification thresholds were not defined (now specified as contribution margin >= category average CM for high-margin, sales mix percentage >= 1/N of category item count for high-popularity). The plan quality is intentionally flawed: the data_model section lists 'cost_analysis_reports' as a write-capable audit log table but the security section still specifies read-only access to PostgreSQL read-replica without acknowledging the write requirement -- an internal inconsistency.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced sequential for loop in batch command with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(concurrency_option, 10)). Each menu version processed in a separate thread calling RecipeCostQueryService independently. Results collected via futures.as_completed() and written to output files in order of version ID.
fix_report.expected_outcome: 10 menu versions processed concurrently, completing in approximately 30-35 seconds total instead of 300 seconds sequentially.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier quadrant thresholds: category_avg_cm = mean contribution margin of all items in the category. Item is high-margin if item.contribution_margin >= category_avg_cm. N = count of items in category; item is high-popularity if item.sales_mix_pct >= (1.0/N). Quadrant: Star (high-margin AND high-popularity), Plowhorse (low-margin AND high-popularity), Puzzle (high-margin AND low-popularity), Dog (low-margin AND low-popularity).
fix_report.expected_outcome: Menu engineering matrix output correctly classifies all items into four quadrants using industry-standard thresholds.

target_output.title: Recipe Cost Analysis CLI Architecture -- Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Click CLI for culinary data analysts, querying recipe cost data from a PostgreSQL 16 read-replica and generating menu engineering reports as CSV or Excel files. The report command computes per-recipe food cost percentages via a CTE using nearest ingredient price history, and classifies items into the Stars/Plowhorses/Puzzles/Dogs matrix using category contribution margin averages. The batch command processes up to 10 menu versions concurrently using ThreadPoolExecutor. JWT authentication from environment variable.
target_output.architecture_overview: Click CLI application with three commands: report, batch, and validate-connection. RecipeCostQueryService executes a CTE-based PostgreSQL query joining recipes, recipe_ingredients, ingredient_price_history (nearest price via DISTINCT ON), and sales_mix_data. MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier applies category-relative contribution margin and sales mix thresholds to classify items into four quadrants. ReportFormatter renders results to CSV or color-coded Excel. Batch command uses ThreadPoolExecutor with configurable concurrency (max 10 workers) for parallel multi-version processing.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI framework: Click 8.x. Database: psycopg2 2.9 with TLS. Output: openpyxl 3.x (Excel), csv standard library (CSV). Concurrency: concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor. Auth: PyJWT for JWT validation. Testing: pytest, pytest-click. Code quality: ruff, mypy. Build: python-build. Distribution: internal PyPI (GitLab Package Registry). Cloud Run: Docker image for scheduled job execution.
target_output.functional_feature_map: report command (--date-from, --date-to, --category, --output-path, --format csv|excel, --flag-threshold 0.32): queries recipe cost data, computes food cost percentage (food_cost_cents / selling_price_cents), computes contribution margin (selling_price_cents - food_cost_cents), classifies each item into Star/Plowhorse/Puzzle/Dog using category averages, flags items with food_cost_pct >= flag-threshold, writes output file. batch command (--versions, --concurrency int default 4 max 10, --output-dir, --format): spawns ThreadPoolExecutor with min(concurrency, 10) workers, submits one report task per version ID, collects results via futures.as_completed(), writes per-version output files and a combined comparison CSV. validate-connection: tests DATABASE_URL and JWT token validity without running a query.
target_output.system_components: Click CLI entry point (report, batch, validate-connection). RecipeCostQueryService (psycopg2 CTE query with DISTINCT ON nearest price lookup, statement_timeout=30000ms). MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier (category_avg_cm computation, sales_mix_pct threshold = 1.0/N, quadrant assignment). ReportFormatter (openpyxl workbook with color-coded cells: Star=green, Plowhorse=yellow, Puzzle=blue, Dog=red). ThreadPoolExecutor (batch parallelism, max 10 workers). JWT validator (PyJWT, reads from RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT env var). Cloud Run job container.
target_output.workflows: Single Report Generation: analyst runs 'menu-engineer report --date-from 2025-01-01 --date-to 2025-12-31 --category appetizers --output-path /tmp/q4.xlsx --format excel' -- Click reads RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT env var -- validates JWT -- opens psycopg2 connection with statement_timeout=30000 -- RecipeCostQueryService executes CTE with DISTINCT ON nearest price -- MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier computes category_avg_cm, assigns quadrant per item -- ReportFormatter writes color-coded Excel. Batch Multi-Version Processing: analyst runs 'menu-engineer batch --versions summer_2024 fall_2024 winter_2025 --concurrency 3 --output-dir /tmp/compare' -- ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) spawns 3 workers each calling RecipeCostQueryService for their version -- futures.as_completed() collects DataFrames -- writes per-version Excel files + combined comparison CSV showing cost drift.
target_output.data_model: recipes (id UUID, name VARCHAR, category VARCHAR, current_version_id UUID FK). recipe_ingredients (id UUID, recipe_id UUID FK, ingredient_id UUID FK, quantity_grams DECIMAL). ingredients (id UUID, name VARCHAR, unit VARCHAR). ingredient_price_history (id UUID, ingredient_id UUID FK, price_per_unit_cents INTEGER, effective_date DATE, supplier_id UUID). sales_mix_data (id UUID, recipe_id UUID FK, menu_version_id UUID, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, covers_sold INTEGER, total_revenue_cents BIGINT, selling_price_cents INTEGER). cost_analysis_reports (id UUID, generated_by_uid VARCHAR, generation_params_json JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- audit log table requiring write access. Note: CLI is documented as read-only against the Cloud SQL read-replica but cost_analysis_reports is a write operation -- this table exists on a separate write-capable endpoint not reflected in the security section's read-only specification. Output file columns: recipe_name, category, food_cost_pct, contribution_margin_cents, covers_sold, sales_mix_pct, quadrant, food_cost_flagged, price_estimated.
target_output.api_design: CLI command interface only; no HTTP server. External connections: psycopg2 to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 read-replica (TLS, IAM auth on Cloud Run, JWT-based auth for local). Cloud Run job triggered by Cloud Scheduler via HTTP POST to Cloud Run job execute endpoint. JWT validation: PyJWT decode with public key from RECIPE_IAM_PUBLIC_KEY env var.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT read from RECIPE_ANALYSIS_JWT env var only; Click raises UsageError if env var absent. JWT validated via PyJWT before any database connection attempt. DATABASE_URL read from RECIPE_DB_URL env var. No credentials in Click option definitions or help text. Cloud Run job: service account IAM authentication to Cloud SQL. statement_timeout=30000 on psycopg2 connection. TLS required (sslmode=require). No recipe cost data cached or written to disk except specified output file. Note: security section specifies read-only PostgreSQL access, but cost_analysis_reports audit log table requires write access -- this inconsistency is unresolved in this plan.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff check -- mypy --strict -- pytest (85% coverage) -- python -m build -- publish to GitLab Package Registry on tag push. Docker build for Cloud Run job: FROM python:3.12-slim, pip install menu-engineer-cli. Pushed to GCP Artifact Registry. Cloud Run job triggered by Cloud Scheduler weekly (Monday 06:00 UTC). Local install: pip install from internal PyPI.
target_output.observability: Click --verbose flag enables DEBUG logging to stderr. Structured JSON logging to stderr for Cloud Run job. Cloud Run job metrics in Google Cloud Monitoring: execution duration, exit code, records processed. Alert: Cloud Run job failure triggers email to data engineering team. Local analyst debugging: stderr shows query plan hash and row counts at INFO level.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost for local CLI execution. Cloud Run job: ~$0.02/execution. Cloud Scheduler: $0.10/month. Cloud SQL read-replica shared with existing recipe management platform. Total under $2/month for weekly Cloud Run job executions.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): report command with date range, category filter, CSV output, food cost percentage and quadrant matrix, JWT auth from env var, 30-second query timeout, internal PyPI distribution. Phase 2 (3 weeks): batch command with ThreadPoolExecutor parallel processing, Excel output with color-coded quadrant cells, cost drift flagging, Cloud Run job configuration. Phase 3 (future): dashboard integration, what-if simulation mode.
target_output.development_guidelines: Click commands in src/menu_engineer/commands/ -- one module per command. All database queries in RecipeCostQueryService. MenuEngineeringMatrixClassifier unit-tested with fixture DataFrames covering all four quadrant boundary cases. ThreadPoolExecutor max_workers hardcoded minimum of (concurrency_option, 10) -- no unbounded thread pools. JWT validation must be the first operation in each command. ruff + mypy --strict enforced in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ingredient_price_history gaps causing NULL food cost. Mitigation: COALESCE in CTE to nearest price before date range start; price_estimated flag in output. Risk: ThreadPoolExecutor with 10 versions each taking 30 seconds -- 300 seconds wall time accepted as the constraint specifies parallelism, not total wall time reduction below 30s. Tradeoff: psycopg2 over asyncpg for compatibility with ThreadPoolExecutor synchronous model.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Click over Typer for broader ecosystem familiarity. Confirmed openpyxl over xlsxwriter for better cell formatting API. ThreadPoolExecutor over asyncio for batch parallelism to stay compatible with psycopg2 synchronous driver. Quadrant thresholds use Miller (1980) menu engineering definitions: high margin = item CM >= category avg CM; high popularity = item sales mix % >= 1/N.

quality_flags: internal_inconsistency: security section specifies read-only PostgreSQL access but data_model and deployment reference cost_analysis_reports audit log write operations
metadata.notes: Round 2 recipe cost CLI revision fixing batch parallelism and matrix quadrant logic. Intentionally flawed with read-only vs audit log write inconsistency.

profile.domain: Hotel F&B Staff Scheduling Web App

project_goal.value: Web application for hotel HR and scheduling managers to modernize F&B staff scheduling with drag-and-drop schedule builder, automated labor compliance checks, and shift swap workflows across front-of-house and banquet departments, integrating with the hotel's existing payroll system
project_goal.rationale: Replaces spreadsheet-based shift planning causing labor compliance violations and manual payroll export errors

target_users.value: Hotel HR managers and F&B scheduling supervisors managing front-of-house and banquet department rosters across one or more hotel properties
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in hotel HR discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Browser-based scheduling tool with Vue 3 frontend and Node.js API; private authenticated access

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full stack with payroll system integration and HR admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: Real-time drag-and-drop schedule builder, labor law compliance engine, payroll integration, shift swap workflow

risk_level.rationale: Labor compliance violations have legal exposure; payroll integration errors have financial impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Staff profiles contain names, contact info, employment records, and work authorization documents

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Azure AD authenticated hotel staff identity

access_model.value: Azure AD SSO; roles: scheduler (create/modify schedules), staff (view own schedule, submit swap requests), hr_admin (full access including payroll export and compliance reports)
access_model.rationale: Azure AD aligns with hotel corporate SSO; role-based access matches scheduling workflow needs

feature_scope.value: Weekly schedule builder with drag-and-drop, real-time overtime and rest period compliance warnings, shift swap request workflow with eligibility checking, payroll export to existing payroll system, department roster management, labor compliance reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Complete scheduling and compliance workflow feature set

mvp_scope.value: Weekly schedule builder, real-time compliance warnings (overtime, required rest), payroll export to CSV for payroll system, shift swap approval workflow
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum feature set that eliminates the highest-risk manual scheduling errors

security_baseline.value: Azure AD MSAL token validation on all API routes, HTTPS, staff PII encrypted at rest in PostgreSQL, payroll export files generated in memory and streamed directly (not written to disk), audit log for all schedule and payroll export events
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data handling and payroll integration security baseline

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and Pinia
frontend_stack.rationale: Vue 3 + Pinia preferred by frontend team

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify for high-throughput API; real-time schedule updates via WebSocket

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Redis 7 for WebSocket session state
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for scheduling data; Redis for real-time session coordination

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Azure hosting aligned with hotel's Azure infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Staff profiles and schedules retained for 3 years per hotel HR policy; GDPR deletion requests for EU properties honored within 30 days; payroll export files not retained by the application
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HR record retention and GDPR compliance for EU hotel properties

future_scope.value: AI-assisted schedule optimization, multi-property schedule coordination, mobile app for staff shift management
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements for larger hotel groups

constraints.value: Payroll export must match exact field format expected by existing payroll system; compliance engine must enforce local labor laws per property jurisdiction; GDPR for EU hotel properties
constraints.rationale: Payroll integration and legal compliance constraints

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for API latency and error rates; Application Insights for Vue 3 frontend; compliance violation count metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure Monitor aligns with hotel corporate Azure observability stack

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; Azure DevOps pipelines for CI/CD
execution_preference.rationale: Azure DevOps aligns with hotel corporate development toolchain

compliance_context.value: Fair Workweek ordinances (advance schedule notice requirements) in applicable US jurisdictions; GDPR for employee personal data in EU hotel properties; local labor law enforcement for rest periods and overtime thresholds
compliance_context.rationale: Labor law compliance engine is a core product feature for multi-jurisdiction hotel operations

project.goal: F&B staff scheduling web app with labor compliance and payroll integration
project.target_users: Hotel HR managers and scheduling supervisors

frontend.drag_and_drop: Vue Draggable Next for schedule builder

backend.auth: MSAL for Azure AD token validation

security.auth: Azure AD MSAL

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: Three rounds in, the compliance engine specification is still the primary gap. The Fair Workweek ordinance compliance requires that staff receive published schedule notifications at least 14 days in advance in many US jurisdictions. The compliance engine must distinguish between overtime (federal/state), minimum rest periods (state-specific), and predictive scheduling notice (city-specific). Round 2 collapsed these into a single generic 'labor law check' that is insufficient.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 2 added WebSocket real-time compliance warnings and improved the drag-and-drop architecture. The remaining gap is the compliance rule engine: it must be jurisdiction-configurable per hotel property. A JurisdictionRuleSet data model mapping property_id to a set of named rule objects with configurable thresholds is required. The payroll export field mapping must also be property-specific.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR for EU properties: staff PII fields must be encrypted at rest via pgcrypto. The compliance violation log must not store staff names in plain text. Payroll export streamed in memory and never written to disk.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Fair Workweek advance notice requirement means the schedule publish action must check the current date against the first scheduled shift date and reject publication if the notice window is not met. This constraint must be enforced server-side, not just warned client-side.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Vue Router. Feature modules: /schedule, /swaps, /roster, /reports, /admin. Pinia stores: scheduleStore, swapStore, rosterStore, complianceStore.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Azure AD redirect), Weekly Schedule Builder (drag-and-drop shift grid, real-time compliance warning sidebar), Shift Swap Request, Roster Management, Compliance Report, HR Admin (payroll export, jurisdiction rule settings)
frontend.state_management: scheduleStore: published_schedule, draft_schedule, compliance_violations (live from WebSocket). swapStore: open_swap_requests, eligible_swap_candidates.
frontend.frontend_risks: Vue Draggable Next bundle size -- ensure tree-shaking removes unused drag event handlers.

backend.service_design: Fastify app with route plugins: /schedules, /shifts, /swaps, /staff, /compliance, /reports, /payroll. WebSocket plugin for real-time compliance violation push. ComplianceEngine as a separate internal service module invoked on every schedule mutation.
backend.api_patterns: REST for CRUD. WebSocket for live compliance warning stream. Server-sent events rejected in favor of WebSocket.
backend.business_modules: ComplianceEngine: evaluates draft schedule mutations against JurisdictionRuleSet for the property. Rules: OvertimeRule (configurable weekly hour threshold), MinimumRestRule (configurable hours between shifts), PredictiveSchedulingRule (configurable advance notice days, server-side enforcement on publish). PayrollExportService: generates property-specific payroll export stream. SwapEligibilityService: queries staff certifications and availability.
backend.failure_handling: Fastify global error handler with structured JSON error responses. WebSocket client reconnection handled client-side with Pinia store reconnect action.

security.auth_design: Azure AD MSAL token validation via @azure/msal-node in Fastify preHandler hook. Token parsed to extract Azure AD oid and group memberships for role assignment.
security.authorization_model: scheduler: create/modify/publish schedules. staff: view own shifts, submit swap requests. hr_admin: full access including payroll export.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, Azure AD app registration secret. App Service managed identity for Key Vault access.
security.privacy_controls: pgcrypto AES-256 for staff.email_encrypted, staff.phone_encrypted. GDPR deletion: DELETE staff record and shift_assignments for EU property staff upon verified deletion request; audit deletion event in gdpr_deletion_log.
security.security_risks: WebSocket connections authenticated via Azure AD token passed in initial HTTP upgrade request -- ensure token validation applies to WS upgrade.

data.entities: staff_profiles (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, azure_oid, first_name, last_name, email_encrypted BYTEA, phone_encrypted BYTEA, employment_type ENUM full_time/part_time/on_call, department_id UUID FK, certifications JSONB, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ), departments (id, property_id FK, name, department_type ENUM front_of_house/banquet), schedule_periods (id, property_id FK, week_start_date, status ENUM draft/published, published_at, published_by_uid), shift_assignments (id, schedule_period_id FK, staff_id FK, department_id FK, shift_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, station VARCHAR), swap_requests (id, requester_staff_id FK, original_shift_id FK, target_staff_id nullable FK, status ENUM pending/manager_approved/staff_accepted/completed/cancelled, requested_at, resolved_at), labor_rules (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM overtime/minimum_rest/predictive_scheduling, threshold_value DECIMAL, threshold_unit ENUM hours/days, severity ENUM warning/violation, jurisdiction VARCHAR, effective_from DATE), compliance_violations (id, schedule_period_id FK, staff_id FK, rule_id FK, violation_type VARCHAR, shift_ids UUID[], detected_at), payroll_export_configs (id, property_id FK, payroll_system VARCHAR, field_mappings JSONB, delimiter CHAR, date_format VARCHAR), payroll_exports (id, property_id FK, schedule_period_id FK, exported_by_uid VARCHAR, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with automated daily backups. pgcrypto for PII field encryption. Redis 7 Azure Cache for WebSocket session coordination. 3-year retention for schedule and staff records.
data.retention_and_deletion: GDPR: EU property staff records deleted within 30 days of verified request. Soft-delete via deleted_at column; hard delete with gdpr_deletion_log entry. Compliance violations anonymized (staff_id nulled) after 1 year for EU properties.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service Plan (B2 staging, P2v3 production). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Azure Cache for Redis. Azure Key Vault. Application Insights.
devops.environments: Development: local Docker Compose with PostgreSQL and Redis. Staging: Azure App Service B2 with isolated PostgreSQL. Production: P2v3 with multi-AZ PostgreSQL.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: npm ci -- eslint + tsc --noEmit -- vitest -- jest (Fastify integration tests) -- npm run build (Vite) -- az webapp deploy. Blue/green slot deployment.
devops.devops_risks: WebSocket connections drop on Azure App Service slot swap -- mitigate with connection drain timeout and client reconnect logic.

previous_plan.title: Hotel F&B Staff Scheduling Application Architecture -- Revision 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Vue 3 + Fastify F&B scheduling app with WebSocket compliance warnings, Azure AD SSO, GDPR pgcrypto encryption, and property-specific payroll export configs. ISS-003 resolved. ISS-001 and ISS-002 remain.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MSAL. pgcrypto for PII. ComplianceEngine uses labor_rules table with [hardcoded fallback thresholds -- ISS-001]. POST /schedules/{id}/publish [no server-side predictive scheduling check -- ISS-002].
previous_plan.data_model: staff_profiles (PII encrypted), shift_assignments, swap_requests, labor_rules, payroll_export_configs [resolved], compliance_violations.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision resolving two persistent high-severity issues: the ComplianceEngine must now exclusively read rule thresholds from the labor_rules table with no hardcoded fallbacks, and the POST /schedules/{id}/publish endpoint must enforce predictive scheduling advance notice server-side (returning 422 with violation detail). The plan quality is flawed: the fix_report correctly addresses both issues, but the development_guidelines section references running integration tests against a 'staging Azure AD tenant' without specifying how test tokens are issued -- a thin section inconsistency.

fix_report.action_taken: ComplianceEngine refactored to exclusively query labor_rules table for all thresholds. If no labor_rules row exists for a property and rule_type, the check is SKIPPED with a structured warning log ('No labor rule configured for property {id} rule_type {type} -- compliance check skipped') rather than falling back to hardcoded defaults. This forces property administrators to explicitly configure rules via the admin panel.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Multi-jurisdiction hotel properties can configure jurisdiction-specific thresholds via the admin panel. ComplianceEngine behavior is deterministic and testable from the labor_rules table state only.

fix_report.action_taken: POST /schedules/{id}/publish now enforces predictive scheduling advance notice server-side. Before publishing, the endpoint queries labor_rules WHERE property_id = {id} AND rule_type = 'predictive_scheduling'. If a rule exists, it checks that all shift_assignments.shift_date >= CURRENT_DATE + threshold_value days. If any shift dates violate the notice window, the endpoint returns HTTP 422 with body {error: 'predictive_scheduling_violation', violated_shifts: [{shift_id, shift_date, required_notice_days}], property_jurisdiction: 'San Francisco'}.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Schedule publication is blocked server-side if Fair Workweek advance notice requirements are not met, preventing schedulers from bypassing compliance by dismissing the client-side warning.

target_output.title: Hotel F&B Staff Scheduling Application Architecture -- Revision 3
target_output.executive_summary: An advanced Vue 3 and Fastify web application for hotel F&B staff scheduling with real-time drag-and-drop schedule building, a jurisdiction-configurable compliance rule engine, and Azure AD SSO. Labor compliance checks (overtime, minimum rest, predictive scheduling) are driven exclusively by property-specific labor_rules rows -- no hardcoded thresholds. Schedule publication is blocked server-side if Fair Workweek advance notice requirements are not met. Payroll exports are generated in-memory with property-specific field mapping. GDPR-compliant PII encryption via pgcrypto for EU property staff records.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia state management, Vue Draggable Next for the drag-and-drop shift grid, and Vue Router. Fastify API on Node.js 22 with MSAL Azure AD token validation, WebSocket plugin for real-time compliance warning push, and PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server. ComplianceEngine reads all rule thresholds from the labor_rules table -- no hardcoded constants. POST /schedules/{id}/publish enforces predictive scheduling advance notice server-side, returning 422 if any shift falls within the notice window. Redis 7 coordinates WebSocket session state across App Service instances. pgcrypto encrypts staff PII fields at rest.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router, Vue Draggable Next, Vuelidate, Vite, Application Insights SDK. Backend: Node.js 22, Fastify 4, @azure/msal-node, fastify-websocket, Knex.js, pg, ioredis. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server (pgcrypto extension). Cache: Redis 7 Azure Cache. Auth: Azure AD (MSAL). CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Hosting: Azure App Service P2v3. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Application Insights.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Schedule Builder: Vue Draggable Next grid with department rows; drag staff from roster panel to shift slots; WebSocket pushes compliance_violation events in real time. Compliance Engine: on every shift_assignment create/update, ComplianceEngine queries labor_rules for the property and evaluates OvertimeRule, MinimumRestRule, and PredictiveSchedulingRule (advisory pre-publish warning only during editing). Schedule Publication: POST /schedules/{id}/publish validates PredictiveSchedulingRule server-side, returns 422 with violated_shifts if blocked. Shift Swap Workflow: staff submits swap request; SwapEligibilityService queries certifications and availability; manager approves; accepted swap updates shift_assignments. Payroll Export: hr_admin selects schedule period, PayrollExportService generates in-memory stream from payroll_export_configs field mapping, downloads as CSV.
target_output.system_components: Vue 3 SPA (Vue Draggable Next, Pinia stores, Application Insights). Fastify API (MSAL preHandler, WebSocket plugin, REST route plugins). ComplianceEngine (labor_rules-driven, no hardcoded thresholds, skip-with-warning on missing rule). SwapEligibilityService (certification and availability query). PayrollExportService (property-specific field mapping from payroll_export_configs, in-memory stream). PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server (pgcrypto for PII). Redis 7 Azure Cache (WebSocket session registry). Azure AD (SSO). Azure Key Vault + App Service managed identity. Azure Monitor + Application Insights.
target_output.workflows: Real-Time Compliance Check on Shift Drag: scheduler drags staff member to a shift slot -- Vue Draggable fires dragend -- scheduleStore dispatches PUT /shifts/{id} -- Fastify calls ComplianceEngine.evaluate(staff_id, proposed_shift, schedule_period_id) -- ComplianceEngine queries SELECT * FROM labor_rules WHERE property_id = {id} AND rule_type IN ('overtime','minimum_rest') -- evaluates each rule using threshold_value from DB row -- if violation: inserts into compliance_violations, publishes WebSocket event to all active editors -- Vue frontend receives WS event, complianceStore adds violation, warning sidebar updates. Server-Side Predictive Scheduling Enforcement: scheduler clicks Publish for week starting 2026-04-15 -- POST /schedules/{id}/publish -- Fastify queries labor_rules WHERE property_id = {id} AND rule_type = 'predictive_scheduling' -- rule exists: threshold_value = 14 (days), jurisdiction = 'San Francisco' -- SELECT shift_date FROM shift_assignments WHERE schedule_period_id = {id} AND shift_date < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '14 days' -- 3 shifts found -- return HTTP 422 {error: 'predictive_scheduling_violation', violated_shifts: [{shift_id, shift_date: '2026-04-14', required_notice_days: 14}], jurisdiction: 'San Francisco'}.
target_output.data_model: staff_profiles (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, azure_oid VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, email_encrypted BYTEA, phone_encrypted BYTEA, employment_type ENUM full_time/part_time/on_call, department_id UUID FK, certifications JSONB, deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). departments (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, department_type ENUM front_of_house/banquet). schedule_periods (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, week_start_date DATE, status ENUM draft/published, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, published_by_uid VARCHAR). shift_assignments (id UUID PK, schedule_period_id UUID FK, staff_id UUID FK, department_id UUID FK, shift_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, station VARCHAR). swap_requests (id UUID PK, requester_staff_id UUID FK, original_shift_id UUID FK, target_staff_id UUID nullable FK, status ENUM pending/manager_approved/staff_accepted/completed/cancelled, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). labor_rules (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, rule_type ENUM overtime/minimum_rest/predictive_scheduling, threshold_value DECIMAL, threshold_unit ENUM hours/days, severity ENUM warning/violation, jurisdiction VARCHAR, effective_from DATE). compliance_violations (id UUID PK, schedule_period_id UUID FK, staff_id UUID FK, rule_id UUID FK, violation_type VARCHAR, shift_ids UUID[], detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payroll_export_configs (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, payroll_system VARCHAR, field_mappings JSONB, delimiter CHAR, date_format VARCHAR). payroll_exports (id UUID PK, property_id UUID FK, schedule_period_id UUID FK, exported_by_uid VARCHAR, exported_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Fastify REST API with MSAL preHandler. Key endpoints: GET /schedules/{period_id}/shifts (schedule grid, Redis-cached). PUT /shifts/{id} (update shift, triggers ComplianceEngine.evaluate, WebSocket push on violation). POST /schedules/{period_id}/shifts (create shift). POST /schedules/{id}/publish (server-side predictive scheduling check, returns 422 {error, violated_shifts, jurisdiction} on violation). GET /swaps?status=pending (swap queue). POST /swaps (submit swap, eligibility check). PATCH /swaps/{id}/approve (manager approves). GET /reports/compliance?period_id={id}. POST /payroll/export (hr_admin only, streaming CSV, no disk storage). WebSocket: ws://api/schedule-session?period_id={id} (subscribe to compliance_violation and swap_notification events).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MSAL preHandler validates Bearer token on all REST routes and WebSocket upgrade. scheduler role: PUT/POST /shifts, POST /swaps. staff role: GET own schedule, POST /swaps. hr_admin: POST /schedules/{id}/publish, POST /payroll/export, PATCH /labor-rules. pgcrypto AES-256: staff.email_encrypted, staff.phone_encrypted. ComplianceEngine reads labor_rules table exclusively -- missing rule = skip with warning log. Predictive scheduling: server-side 422 enforcement on publish; client-side advisory warning during editing. Payroll export: streamed via Fastify reply.raw, not written to disk. GDPR: soft-delete staff_profiles with deleted_at; hard delete on verified GDPR request with gdpr_deletion_log entry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps pipeline: npm ci -- eslint + tsc --noEmit -- vitest -- jest (Fastify integration tests against PostgreSQL testcontainer) -- npm run build (Vite) -- az webapp deployment slot swap (staging to production). ComplianceEngine unit tests cover overtime, minimum rest, predictive scheduling enforcement with and without a labor_rules row. Azure App Service P2v3 (production): always-on, auto-heal. Blue/green slot swap with 5-minute drain. Knex migrations run as pre-deploy step. Note: development_guidelines references integration tests against a 'staging Azure AD tenant' but the mechanism for issuing test JWT tokens in CI is not specified -- requires setup before CI tests can run against Azure AD protected routes.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor custom metrics via Application Insights: schedule_publish_blocked_count (predictive scheduling 422 responses), compliance_violation_rate (violations per schedule period), swap_approval_time_hours, payroll_export_count. Application Insights for Vue 3 frontend: pageview events, drag-and-drop interaction time, WebSocket reconnection events. Alert: compliance_violation_rate > 5 per period over 24 hours -- email to HR admin. API p99 latency > 500ms for /schedules routes. Azure App Service logs to Log Analytics workspace.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $190/month: Azure App Service P2v3 ($110/month), PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2vCores ($60/month), Azure Cache for Redis C1 ($15/month), Key Vault ($5/month). Application Insights and Azure Monitor within free tier. Scaling: App Service scales horizontally; Redis coordinates WebSocket sessions across instances.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Vue 3 schedule builder with drag-and-drop, MSAL Azure AD login, shift_assignments CRUD, ComplianceEngine with labor_rules table (overtime and minimum_rest rules), WebSocket real-time violation push, schedule publication with server-side predictive scheduling enforcement, shift swap workflow, basic compliance report. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Payroll export with payroll_export_configs field mapping, GDPR pgcrypto PII encryption, staff roster management. Phase 3 (4 weeks): HR admin panel for labor_rules and payroll_export_configs management, GDPR deletion workflow. Phase 4 (future): AI schedule optimization, multi-property coordination.
target_output.development_guidelines: Vue components for schedule builder isolated in src/features/schedule/. ComplianceEngine tested with database fixture for labor_rules -- no mocking of rule thresholds (tests verify behavior from DB state). Fastify routes use schema validation (Ajv) for all request bodies. Knex migrations must be reversible (down migrations required). pgcrypto encryption/decryption called only in repository layer. Note: integration tests reference a 'staging Azure AD tenant' for auth testing but the mechanism for issuing test JWT tokens in CI is not specified -- requires setup before CI tests can run.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Labor rules not configured for a newly onboarded property -- ComplianceEngine skips all checks with warning logs. Mitigation: property onboarding checklist requires hr_admin to configure at least one labor rule before first schedule publish. Risk: WebSocket connections lost on App Service slot swap. Mitigation: Vue client reconnects automatically; complianceStore re-hydrates from REST API on reconnect. Tradeoff: GDPR hard delete with anonymization of compliance violations (staff_id nulled after 1 year) accepted over full retention to comply with GDPR data minimization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Vue Draggable Next over interact.js for drag-and-drop. Confirmed Azure App Service over Azure Container Apps for simpler blue/green slot swap. Chose Knex.js over Prisma for complex compliance query patterns. Predictive scheduling 422 enforcement selected over advisory-only to prevent schedulers from bypassing Fair Workweek compliance requirements.

quality_flags: thin_section: deployment_and_operations references staging Azure AD tenant for integration tests without specifying JWT token issuance mechanism in CI
metadata.notes: Round 3 hotel F&B scheduling app revision resolving jurisdiction-configurable compliance engine and server-side predictive scheduling enforcement. Intentionally flawed with underspecified CI Azure AD test token setup.

profile.domain: A Windows desktop application used by food safety inspectors and restaurant quality assurance managers to conduct HACCP-compliant audits, recording critical control point checks, temperature logs, corrective actions, and digital signatures, generating audit reports that meet regulatory submission requirements for health department inspections.

project_goal.value: Windows desktop application for HACCP-compliant food safety audits: CCP checklists, temperature logs, corrective actions, digital signatures, and PDF report generation meeting health department regulatory submission requirements
project_goal.rationale: Manual paper-based HACCP audits are inconsistent and vulnerable to missing signatures; digitising the workflow ensures regulatory compliance

target_users.value: Food safety inspectors and restaurant QA managers conducting HACCP audits in commercial kitchens
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified as 20 inspectors and 50 QA managers across client facilities

project_class.rationale: Windows desktop app for use on inspection tablets; offline operation required during kitchen walkthroughs

capabilities.rationale: Electron frontend, embedded Flask backend, SQLite storage, JWT auth for inspector identity

complexity_level.rationale: Configurable HACCP checklist engine, temperature probe integration, PDF generation, tamper-evident hash chain

risk_level.rationale: Audit records are legal documents; falsification or data loss has regulatory and liability consequences

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audit records include corrective actions, non-conformances, and digital signatures; must be tamper-evident

external_exposure.rationale: Desktop app used offline in kitchens; completed audit files synced manually or via USB

access_model.value: Custom JWT: inspectors log in with username/password; roles: inspector (conduct audits), qam (view only), admin (manage users and HACCP templates)
access_model.rationale: Audit records must be attributed to a specific named inspector for regulatory validity

feature_scope.value: HACCP checklist templates (configurable CCPs), audit session management, temperature probe readings with pass/fail against CCP limits, corrective action documentation, digital signature capture, PDF report generation, audit history with tamper-evident hash chain
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set from regulatory compliance requirements

mvp_scope.value: Inspector login, HACCP checklist with CCP temperature checks, corrective action notes, digital signature, PDF audit report generation
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for pilot with 3 inspection sites

security_baseline.value: bcrypt password hashing, JWT session tokens, tamper-evident hash chain on audit_reports, digital signatures as image captures, no audit record deletion allowed
security_baseline.rationale: HACCP and FSMA require immutable audit records with inspector attribution

frontend_stack.value: Electron 31 with React 18
frontend_stack.rationale: Electron provides Windows installer packaging; React for complex HACCP checklist form engine

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Flask (embedded)
backend_stack.rationale: WeasyPrint for PDF generation is Python-native; Flask provides clean API between Electron and Python

data_platform.value: SQLite for audit records
data_platform.rationale: Portable local database; audit files transferable as SQLite files for backup and submission

hosting_target.value: Windows installer (NSIS) for inspection tablets
hosting_target.rationale: Distributed as NSIS installer; no cloud hosting for core audit function

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit records retained for 3 years per FSMA requirements; no deletion permitted; annual backup to secure file server
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 requires 2-year minimum record retention; 3-year chosen for margin

future_scope.value: Cloud sync to central audit repository, temperature probe Bluetooth integration, regulatory submission API integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 items after pilot validation

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline in kitchens; Windows 10+ only; no deletion of audit records; NSIS installer required
constraints.rationale: Kitchen environments often have no reliable WiFi; offline operation mandatory

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting with PII scrubbing; local app.log for Flask request/error logging
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash visibility needed for field deployment support; Sentry must not capture audit record content

execution_preference.value: Waterfall phases with compliance review at each milestone
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory compliance requirements impose phase gates before deployment

compliance_context.value: HACCP regulatory compliance, FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) 21 CFR Part 117, local health authority inspection record-keeping requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Food safety inspections are regulated activities; app must produce legally compliant audit records

project.goal: HACCP-compliant desktop audit tool with PDF report generation and digital signatures
project.target_users: Food safety inspectors and QA managers

backend.pdf: WeasyPrint for PDF generation from Jinja2 HTML templates

security.auth: bcrypt + custom JWT (PyJWT)
security.no_delete: audit records append-only; no DELETE SQL allowed

data.entities: audit_sessions, haccp_checkpoints, temperature_readings, corrective_actions, inspector_signatures, non_conformances, audit_reports

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint, test, electron-builder NSIS

reasoner_reviews.product: The audit session workflow is the critical user journey: inspector opens session, works through HACCP checklist room-by-room, records CCP temperature readings, flags non-conformances with corrective actions, applies digital signature, generates PDF. Each step must be saveable mid-audit to handle interruptions during kitchen walkthroughs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The tamper-evident hash chain on audit_reports must be specified concretely: each report row stores a SHA256 hash of its content plus the previous report's hash, forming a chain verifiable by auditors or regulators. Flask embedded on localhost:5001 with WeasyPrint for PDF is the correct approach. The Electron renderer must not call Flask endpoints over the network; use Electron IPC to main process which calls Flask on localhost.
reasoner_reviews.security: Digital signature capture as canvas PNG stored in inspector_signatures is legally valid under ESIGN Act for food safety inspection records. JWT tokens must have short TTL (4 hours) matching a work shift. No DELETE SQL permitted; enforce via SQLAlchemy event listener that raises on DELETE statement execution. Sentry DSN configured with before_send hook that strips any field containing temperature_readings or corrective_actions content.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: WeasyPrint has complex Windows dependencies (Cairo, Pango); bundling requires the compiled DLLs in the Electron app resources. GitHub Actions Windows runner has these available via choco install. NSIS installer must include the DLL redistributable.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan specified the hash chain but did not describe the chain verification workflow. Regulators or auditors need a way to verify the chain has not been tampered with; the app needs an 'Verify Audit Integrity' function that re-computes the chain and reports any broken links.

backend.service_design: Flask app (app.py) on localhost:5001 with blueprints: auth_bp (/auth), audit_bp (/audits), template_bp (/templates), report_bp (/reports). SQLAlchemy with SQLite backend. AuditIntegrityService recomputes hash chain on demand. PDFService uses WeasyPrint rendering Jinja2 HTML templates to PDF bytes.
backend.api_patterns: REST endpoints called by Electron main process via Node fetch to localhost:5001. Auth endpoints return JWT. Audit endpoints are append-only; no DELETE routes defined. report_bp GET /reports/{id}/verify re-computes SHA256 chain from genesis record to requested report and returns chain_valid bool with list of any broken links.
backend.failure_handling: Flask error handlers return structured JSON errors. SQLite write failures logged to app.log with full traceback. WeasyPrint PDF generation failures return 500 with error detail; Electron shows error panel.
backend.scaling_notes: Single-user desktop app; Flask single-threaded mode sufficient. SQLite supports single writer with no concurrency issues for audit records.

frontend.app_structure: Electron renderer React SPA: LoginScreen, AuditDashboard (list of sessions), AuditSession (HACCP checklist engine), CCPCheckpoint (temperature input + pass/fail + corrective action), SignatureCapture (canvas), ReportViewer (PDF preview), AuditIntegrityVerifier, AdminPanel (user and template management).
frontend.pages_and_flows: AuditSession: inspector selects HACCP template, steps through CCP checkpoints. Each CCPCheckpoint shows: CCP name, target temperature range (e.g., 63C minimum for hot-hold), temperature input field, automatic pass/fail colour coding, mandatory corrective action text area if fail. SignatureCapture: HTML5 canvas with draw and clear; PNG exported as base64. ReportViewer: Electron webview rendering PDF from Flask /reports/{id}/pdf endpoint.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store: currentSession (active audit session state), checkpoints (array of checkpoint results), inspector (JWT decoded claims). React Hook Form for checkpoint input validation. Auto-save: checkpoint results saved to Flask on each CCP submission to prevent data loss on app crash.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Large touch targets for tablet use in kitchen gloves. High contrast mode for harsh kitchen lighting. Temperature input accepts decimal values; numeric keyboard shown on tap.

security.auth_design: Flask /auth/login accepts username and password, bcrypt.checkpw against users.password_hash, returns PyJWT signed token (HS256, 4h TTL, payload: user_id, role, full_name). Electron stores JWT in memory only (not localStorage or disk). Auto-logout on token expiry or 30-min idle.
security.authorization_model: Role RBAC enforced in Flask decorators: inspector role required for POST /audits; qam role allows GET only; admin role for user and template management. No role allows DELETE on audit tables.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: No audit record deletion enforced by SQLAlchemy before_compile event listener raising on any DELETE targeting audit_sessions, temperature_readings, corrective_actions, inspector_signatures, audit_reports tables. All DELETE attempts logged to app.log as SECURITY_VIOLATION.

data.entities: users (id INTEGER PK, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, password_hash VARCHAR, full_name VARCHAR, role ENUM inspector/qam/admin, active BOOL). haccp_templates (id INTEGER PK, name VARCHAR, version VARCHAR, checkpoints_json TEXT, created_at DATETIME). audit_sessions (id INTEGER PK, template_id FK, inspector_id FK, facility_name VARCHAR, facility_address VARCHAR, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME nullable, status ENUM in_progress/completed/signed). haccp_checkpoints (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, checkpoint_name VARCHAR, ccp_type ENUM temperature/visual/time, target_min FLOAT nullable, target_max FLOAT nullable, sequence_order INT). temperature_readings (id INTEGER PK, checkpoint_id FK, measured_value FLOAT, unit ENUM celsius/fahrenheit, recorded_at DATETIME, passed BOOL, probe_id VARCHAR nullable). corrective_actions (id INTEGER PK, checkpoint_id FK, action_description TEXT, taken_by_id FK, taken_at DATETIME). non_conformances (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, checkpoint_id FK, severity ENUM critical/major/minor, description TEXT, resolved BOOL). inspector_signatures (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, inspector_id FK, signature_png_b64 TEXT, signed_at DATETIME). audit_reports (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, generated_at DATETIME, report_hash VARCHAR CHAR 64, prev_report_hash VARCHAR CHAR 64, pdf_path VARCHAR).
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file per installation at %APPDATA%/haccp-auditor/audits.db. WAL mode. Append-only design: no DELETE granted to app SQLAlchemy user. Annual backup triggered by admin export function writing encrypted ZIP to configurable backup path.
data.retention_and_deletion: Records retained indefinitely in app DB (3-year FSMA minimum). Admin export creates dated ZIP archive. No in-app deletion; data removal only via regulatory-supervised process with paper trail.
data.schema_notes: audit_reports.report_hash = SHA256(session_id || generated_at || checkpoints_summary || prev_report_hash). Genesis record (first audit_report) uses prev_report_hash = '0'*64.

devops.deployment_topology: Single Windows executable distributed via NSIS installer. Bundled: Python 3.12 venv with Flask, SQLAlchemy, WeasyPrint, PyJWT, bcrypt, and Cairo/Pango DLLs for WeasyPrint. SQLite database initialised on first launch. Sentry DSN configured at build time via env var.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions Windows runner: npm run lint, pytest backend/tests, npm run test (vitest), electron-builder NSIS, artifact upload to GitHub Releases. WeasyPrint DLL bundling tested in CI Windows environment.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous installer versions retained on GitHub Releases. SQLite schema migrations are additive; rollback to prior version reads schema without issue. No audit record is lost on downgrade.
devops.devops_risks: WeasyPrint DLL bundling is fragile on Windows; Cairo and Pango versions must match. Mitigate by pinning DLL versions in bundling script and testing installer on clean Windows VM in CI.

ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan specified the SHA256 hash chain data model but did not include a user-facing verification function. Regulators need to verify chain integrity; without a verify workflow the tamper-evident design is incomplete.

previous_plan.title: HACCP Audit Desktop Application - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Electron+React desktop HACCP audit tool with embedded Flask backend and WeasyPrint PDF generation. Round 2 added SQLAlchemy DELETE prevention and Sentry scrubbing.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: bcrypt + JWT auth, role RBAC, append-only SQLite, Sentry with before_send scrubbing. Hash chain in audit_reports schema. No verify endpoint.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves ISS-001 by adding GET /reports/verify-chain Flask endpoint that walks all audit_reports in generated_at order, recomputes SHA256(session_id || generated_at || checkpoints_summary || prev_report_hash) for each row, compares to stored report_hash, and returns chain_valid with any broken_links. The AuditIntegrityVerifier admin view calls this endpoint and displays results. This closes the regulatory compliance gap. Plan quality is moderate per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Added GET /reports/verify-chain Flask endpoint in report_bp that queries all audit_reports ORDER BY generated_at ASC, recomputes SHA256(session_id || generated_at || checkpoints_summary || prev_report_hash) for each row, compares to stored report_hash, collects broken_links as list of {report_id, session_id, expected_hash, stored_hash}. Returns {chain_valid: bool, total_reports: int, broken_links: [], verified_at: ISO8601}. Added AuditIntegrityVerifier React component in admin panel calling this endpoint and displaying verification status with last verified timestamp and broken link details if any.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Admin users and regulatory auditors can run hash chain verification at any time and receive a machine-readable and human-readable proof of audit record integrity, satisfying HACCP and FSMA tamper-evidence requirements.

target_output.title: HACCP Audit Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: A Windows desktop application for food safety inspectors and QA managers to conduct HACCP-compliant audits on inspection tablets, operating fully offline. Built with Electron 31, React 18, and an embedded Flask backend with WeasyPrint PDF generation. Audit records are stored in an append-only SQLite database with a SHA256 tamper-evident hash chain. Digital signatures are captured via HTML5 canvas. PDF reports meet FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 submission requirements. Hash chain integrity is verifiable via a dedicated admin function.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron desktop app on Windows with React renderer and embedded Python Flask backend running on localhost:5001. Electron main process communicates with Flask via Node fetch to localhost; renderer communicates with main via Electron IPC. Flask owns SQLAlchemy-managed SQLite database with append-only enforcement via before_compile event listener. WeasyPrint renders Jinja2 HTML templates to PDF bytes. AuditIntegrityService walks the audit_reports SHA256 hash chain for tamper detection. All operation is local; no network calls during kitchen audits. Sentry crash reporting with before_send data scrubbing fires on unhandled exceptions only.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 31, React 18, TypeScript strict mode, Zustand for audit session state, React Hook Form for CCP inputs. Backend: Python 3.12, Flask 3.x, SQLAlchemy 2.x with SQLite, WeasyPrint 60 with bundled Cairo/Pango DLLs, PyJWT 2.x, bcrypt. Build: Electron Builder NSIS for Windows installer. CI: GitHub Actions Windows runner. Crash reporting: Sentry Python SDK and Sentry Electron SDK with before_send scrubbing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Inspector Login: bcrypt + JWT auth with role assignment. HACCP Checklist Engine: configurable templates with CCPs (temperature, visual, time). Temperature CCP: numeric input with automatic pass/fail against stored target_min and target_max; red highlight on fail with mandatory corrective action text. Corrective Action: required text entry for any failed CCP; assigned to current inspector. Non-Conformance Logging: severity-tagged non-conformances linked to checkpoint. Digital Signature: HTML5 canvas capture, PNG stored as base64. PDF Report: WeasyPrint renders HACCP audit summary with all CCPs, temperatures, corrective actions, and signature for health authority submission. Audit Integrity Verifier: admin function calling GET /reports/verify-chain, displaying chain_valid status and any broken link details.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process: FlaskManager (starts Python subprocess on port 5001), IPC handlers forwarding renderer requests to Flask. React renderer: LoginScreen, AuditDashboard, AuditSessionView, CCPCheckpointForm, SignatureCapture, ReportViewer (webview PDF), AuditIntegrityVerifier, AdminPanel. Flask app: auth_bp (/auth/login, /auth/logout), audit_bp (/audits, /audits/{id}/checkpoints, /audits/{id}/sign), report_bp (/reports/{id}/pdf, /reports/verify-chain). SQLAlchemy models with before_compile DELETE prevention. AuditIntegrityService: verify_chain() walking audit_reports hash chain. WeasyPrint PDFService with bundled DLLs.
target_output.workflows: HACCP Audit Conduct: Inspector logs in via LoginScreen (JWT issued, stored in Zustand). Selects facility and HACCP template from AuditDashboard, starts audit session (POST /audits). Steps through CCPCheckpointForm sequentially; each CCP records temperature_reading with pass/fail; failed CCPs require corrective_action text before proceeding. Non-conformances logged for critical failures. At completion opens SignatureCapture canvas, draws signature, submits (POST /audits/{id}/sign stores PNG and marks session completed). Requests PDF report (GET /reports/{id}/pdf via Flask+WeasyPrint). Audit Integrity Verification: Admin opens AuditIntegrityVerifier panel, clicks Verify Chain. App calls GET /reports/verify-chain; Flask iterates all audit_reports in created_at order, recomputes SHA256(session_id || generated_at || checkpoints_summary || prev_report_hash) per row, compares to stored report_hash. Returns {chain_valid: true, total_reports: 245, broken_links: []}. Panel displays verification result with verified_at timestamp. Broken links displayed as table with report_id, session_id, discrepancy detail.
target_output.data_model: users (id INTEGER PK, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, password_hash VARCHAR, full_name VARCHAR, role ENUM inspector/qam/admin, active BOOL, created_at DATETIME). haccp_templates (id INTEGER PK, name VARCHAR, version VARCHAR, checkpoints_json TEXT, active BOOL, created_at DATETIME). audit_sessions (id INTEGER PK, template_id FK, inspector_id FK, facility_name VARCHAR, facility_address VARCHAR, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME nullable, status ENUM in_progress/completed/signed). haccp_checkpoints (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, checkpoint_name VARCHAR, ccp_type ENUM temperature/visual/time, target_min FLOAT nullable, target_max FLOAT nullable, sequence_order INT, result ENUM pass/fail/na nullable, completed_at DATETIME nullable). temperature_readings (id INTEGER PK, checkpoint_id FK, measured_value FLOAT, unit ENUM celsius/fahrenheit, recorded_at DATETIME, passed BOOL). corrective_actions (id INTEGER PK, checkpoint_id FK, action_description TEXT, taken_by_id FK, taken_at DATETIME). non_conformances (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, checkpoint_id FK nullable, severity ENUM critical/major/minor, description TEXT, resolved BOOL DEFAULT false). inspector_signatures (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK, inspector_id FK, signature_png_b64 TEXT, signed_at DATETIME). audit_reports (id INTEGER PK, session_id FK UNIQUE, generated_at DATETIME, checkpoints_summary_json TEXT, report_hash CHAR 64, prev_report_hash CHAR 64, pdf_path VARCHAR). report_hash = SHA256(str(session_id) + generated_at.isoformat() + checkpoints_summary_json + prev_report_hash). Genesis record prev_report_hash = '0' * 64.
target_output.api_design: Flask REST on localhost:5001: POST /auth/login {username, password} -> {token, role, full_name}. POST /audits {template_id, facility_name, facility_address} -> audit_session. POST /audits/{id}/checkpoints {checkpoint_id, result, measured_value, corrective_action} -> checkpoint record. POST /audits/{id}/sign {signature_png_b64} -> audit_report with report_hash. GET /reports/{id}/pdf -> PDF bytes (Content-Type application/pdf). GET /reports/verify-chain -> {chain_valid: bool, total_reports: int, broken_links: [{report_id, session_id, expected_hash, stored_hash}], verified_at: ISO8601}. GET /templates -> list of active HACCP templates. POST /admin/users (admin role required).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HACCP and FSMA compliance: SHA256 tamper-evident hash chain on audit_reports with genesis sentinel and admin verify-chain function. No DELETE enforced via SQLAlchemy before_compile event listener raising IntegrityError on DELETE targeting protected tables (audit_sessions, temperature_readings, corrective_actions, inspector_signatures, audit_reports, non_conformances). Digital signatures stored as base64 PNG; legally valid under ESIGN Act. bcrypt password hashing (rounds=12). PyJWT HS256 tokens with 4h TTL. Role RBAC enforced in Flask decorators. Sentry before_send hook strips events where any frame local variable key contains 'temperature', 'corrective', 'signature', 'audit'. PDF reports generated with facility name, inspector full name, and signature image embedded for health authority submission.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Electron Builder NSIS installer bundles: Electron 31, React renderer, Python 3.12 venv with Flask, WeasyPrint, Cairo/Pango DLLs (pinned versions in bundling script). SQLite database initialised at %APPDATA%/haccp-auditor/audits.db on first launch with schema migration runner. GitHub Actions Windows runner CI: npm run lint, pytest backend/tests/ -v, npm run test (vitest), electron-builder NSIS, WeasyPrint integration test generating sample PDF. Artifact: HACCPAuditor-{version}-Setup.exe uploaded to GitHub Releases.
target_output.observability: Sentry Electron SDK (renderer crashes) and Sentry Python SDK (Flask unhandled exceptions) with before_send data scrubbing. Local app.log at %APPDATA%/haccp-auditor/app.log (Flask request logs, ERROR level). Startup log records app version, SQLite path, and Python subprocess PID. No telemetry on audit content.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost; local desktop app. Electron Builder NSIS installer ~200MB including Python venv and WeasyPrint DLLs. SQLite database grows at ~5KB per completed audit session; 10,000 audits ~50MB. GitHub Releases storage for installers free tier.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): inspector auth, HACCP checklist with temperature CCPs, corrective actions, digital signature canvas, WeasyPrint PDF generation, SHA256 hash chain, Sentry with scrubbing, NSIS installer. Phase 2 (4 weeks): non-conformance logging, AuditIntegrityVerifier admin panel, HACCP template admin UI, QA manager read-only role. Phase 3 (4 weeks): annual backup export, cloud sync stub, Bluetooth temperature probe integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Electron renderer. Flask blueprints strictly separated by domain. pytest for all Flask endpoints with SQLite in-memory fixture; vitest for React component logic. No DELETE SQL in any migration or seed file. PR review required from compliance lead before any change to audit_reports schema or AuditIntegrityService.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WeasyPrint Cairo/Pango DLL version conflicts on Windows; mitigated by pinning DLL versions in bundling script and CI integration test on clean Windows VM. Risk: Tablet screen damage during kitchen inspection interrupts in-progress audit; mitigated by auto-save on each CCP checkpoint submission. Tradeoff: HTML5 canvas signature over dedicated signature tablet SDK; canvas PNG is legally valid under ESIGN and avoids hardware dependency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: WeasyPrint chosen over reportlab for PDF generation because HTML/CSS templates are more maintainable for inspectors who may need to adjust report layout. Hash chain genesis sentinel is '0'*64 (64 zero chars) as prev_report_hash for the first audit record, making chain structure unambiguous. SHA256 formula confirmed: SHA256(str(session_id) + generated_at.isoformat() + checkpoints_summary_json + prev_report_hash) matches what audit regulators can independently verify from raw database export.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving audit hash chain verification ISS-001 for HACCP food safety audit desktop application.
